title: DECISION: No silent degradation — VectorStore-only search path, SemanticIndex fallback removed
description:
## Decision

VectorStore is the ONLY search path. The SemanticIndex fallback and JSON cache deserialization (load_with_lock, 1.1GB HashMap) are removed from the query flow entirely. On any VectorStore failure (load/build/search/save), propagate a detailed error message to the user — never silently degrade to the slow path.

## Rationale

Silent fallback to SemanticIndex (4-5s cold JSON load + per-query ONNX model reload) makes the user believe that IS the tool's performance. They never discover the fast path failed, never report the bug, and never get it fixed. The only acceptable fallback is a clear, actionable error message that tells the user what broke and how to fix it.

A tool should run at 100% performance or tell you why it can't. Running in degraded mode without the user's knowledge is a UX anti-pattern — it hides bugs behind mediocre-but-functional behavior.

## Alternatives Considered

- **Keep SemanticIndex fallback (current design)**: Rejected — hides VectorStore failures from users, makes performance bugs invisible, and preserves the slow JSON cache load path that m01/ac0 exist to eliminate.
- **Fallback with a visible warning**: Rejected — warnings are ignored by users, especially in CLI tool output. The tool still "works" so the warning is treated as noise.
- **Fallback with opt-in flag (--allow-degraded)**: Rejected — over-engineered for the problem. If VectorStore is broken, fix VectorStore.

## Affects

- TLDR-zxb: Wire search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP (must implement error-only, no fallback)
- TLDR-m01: Rewire EmbeddingCache onto usearch Index (store_search.rs fallback code removed)
- store_search.rs: SemanticIndex import and fallback branch removed entirely
close_reason: Decision recorded and linked to zxb + m01. Enforced in zxb implementation.

title: DECISION: Incremental re-indexing lives in the daemon (RAM writable index), gated on warm path
description: Because view() is read-only (see storage-split decision), incremental updates (remove stale function vectors + add changed ones, instead of the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable usearch Index. DECISION: that index lives in the DAEMON (TLDR-atc). The daemon holds the writable Index, watches file mtimes, applies per-function deltas, and periodically save()s to disk. Cold CLI invocations use read-only view() of the last saved file. CONSEQUENCE: incremental indexing is NOT a property of usearch alone — it is unlocked by usearch + the daemon warm path together. This elevates TLDR-atc from a latency win to the keystone for incremental indexing.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-01). Decision is final and recorded; closing so it stays a durable, queryable record without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if the decision is revisited.

title: Add daemon warm path for semantic search (no semantic route exists — every query is cold)
description: DISCOVERED 2026-06-01 via daemon route scan + tldr explain. tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes. crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. CONSEQUENCE: every 'tldr semantic'/'similar'/'embed' invocation runs COLD in a fresh process -> SemanticIndex::build -> EmbeddingCache::open -> load_with_lock (cache.rs:236) deserializes the full ~1.1GB cache each time (RSS ~8GB), reloads the 110MB ONNX model (~0.5s), then embeds the query (~0.2s). For warm/interactive use this is a BIGGER lever than usearch: usearch view() removes the 4.3s load, but a daemon warm path removes the load AND the 0.5s model load AND keeps the index resident -> ac0 estimated ~230ms warm. SCOPE: add /semantic (+/similar,/embed) daemon route + handler that holds a warm SemanticIndex (mmap'd vectors once usearch lands) + a warm Embedder (model loaded once) in DaemonState, keyed by project+model+mtimes like other warm caches. Pairs with TLDR-ac0 (usearch storage) and TLDR-h27/y0q (CLI correctness).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic/similar/embed route through the daemon when running; warm query latency ~200-300ms (no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload); cold fallback preserved when daemon absent; results identical to cold path.
notes:
UNLOCKS INCREMENTAL INDEXING (research 2026-06-01): usearch view()'d mmap index is READ-ONLY (issue#97 — cannot add to disk). Incremental updates (remove stale keys + add changed-function vectors, avoiding the full ~30min re-embed) REQUIRE a RAM-resident writable index. The daemon warm path is exactly that home: hold the writable usearch Index in DaemonState, apply deltas on file-mtime change, periodically save(). So this P0 is not just a latency win — it's the prerequisite for incremental re-indexing. Cold CLI keeps using read-only view().
2026-06-02 STATUS: warm path + root-relative cache keys committed (401a7ac); off-executor spawn_blocking committed (7199f03). REMAINING: live stop-mid-build validation (was pending the cache being free). The spawn_blocking follow-up bug (handle_notify on async thread) is TLDR-qr9, now folded into TLDR-ac0.2 (in-daemon watcher → internal channel). Consider closing atc once the live stop-mid-build check is done.
close_reason: Warm path ships VectorStore — load_or_build_store is near-instant on fresh store. Daemon holds resident (Model, VectorStore). Remaining improvements (incremental delta, watcher, concurrent reads) are separate issues (t8f, ac0.2, ac0.1).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: POST-ZXB (2026-06-02): The daemon now holds a resident VectorStore (Mutex<Option<(Model, VectorStore)>>) instead of SemanticIndex. Warm command uses load_or_build_store — near-instant when store is fresh on disk. The original atc acceptance ("warm query latency ~200-300ms, no per-query 1.1GB load, no per-query model reload") is MET for the common case (store exists, nothing changed). The "cold fallback preserved when daemon absent" criterion changes per TLDR-lx7: no fallback, errors propagate. Consider closing atc — the warm path works, remaining improvements (incremental delta, watcher) are separate issues (t8f, ac0.2).

title: Default 0.50 similarity threshold miscalibrated for query-prefix regime (hides correct results)
description: DISCOVERED while dogfooding tldr semantic on our own codebase (TLDR-blm work). The Arctic query prefix (TLDR-dlk, default-ON) improves RANKING decisively (eval recall@5 0.071->0.857) but LOWERS absolute cosine scores, because the asymmetric prefix puts query embeddings in a different region than raw-code passages (query.passage cosine < passage.passage cosine). The user-facing 'tldr semantic' applies a fixed default --threshold 0.50. Result: the CORRECT answer for 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' is cosine_similarity() but it scores 0.46 -> filtered out -> CLI prints 'No matches found above threshold'. So the feature looks BROKEN to a user despite the recall win. The eval harness bypasses the threshold (pure ranking) which is why it reported 0.857. FIX OPTIONS: (a) lower/remove the default threshold and rely on top-k ranking; (b) recalibrate threshold for the prefixed-query score distribution; (c) make threshold prefix-aware. Repro: tldr semantic 'cosine similarity between two embedding vectors' crates/tldr-core/src/semantic --top 3  (default threshold -> no matches; --threshold 0.0 -> cosine_similarity appears at rank 3, score 0.46).
acceptance_criteria: tldr semantic with default settings surfaces the correct top-ranked result for the gold queries (not filtered by threshold); eval-measured ranking and CLI-visible results agree.
notes: Child of TLDR-blm epic. Surfaced by the prefix verdict; a user-facing gap between eval-measured ranking (good) and CLI-visible results (threshold-suppressed).
close_reason: Default semantic threshold 0.5->0.0 (rank top-N, no cutoff) so the prefix-regime scores (~0.46) are no longer hidden; also fixed the formatter's hardcoded 0.50 display to show the actual threshold. PROVEN: 'tldr semantic <q>' with default settings now returns cosine_similarity instead of 'No matches'. semantic.rs.

title: Interim correctness fix: disable wrong-answer daemon arms (slice direction/variable, impact weaker) — route to correct local path until n74
description: Child of fast-lane epic. CODE-VERIFIED (audit 9/64 + Codex UNFIT verdict; 7vb instances): daemon Slice arm hardcodes Backward regardless of -d, ignores --variable, collision-prone cache key — WRONG ANSWERS served whenever the daemon is up. Impact daemon arm returns weaker answers than local. FIX (interim, tiny): remove/bypass these daemon arms so the CLI always computes via the correct local path (correctness > speed until the n74 CSR rebuild restores fast daemon routing with flag parity). NOT in scope: the never-worked dead-path arms (calls/smells/complexity) — those already fall back to correct local answers (user-ratified defer on TLDR-7vb). Acceptance: slice -d forward returns a forward slice with daemon up; --variable honored; impact daemon==local on the frozen corpus; regression test pinning direction+variable through the daemon path.

title: [EPIC] Fast lane: code-verified bugs + proven cheap wins (pre-rewrite)
description:
Groups the CODE-VERIFIED bugs and MEASURED/cheap quality wins that proceed AHEAD of the gated rewrite (strategy ratified 2026-06-06, recorded on TLDR-n74). Membership rule: an item belongs here iff (a) the defect is code-verified or the lever is measured/proven-cheap, AND (b) it has no dependency on the rfz-gated CSR/17d rewrite.

## Bug members (all code-verified)
- Wrong-answer daemon arms interim disable (child issue): slice daemon arm hardcodes Backward + ignores --variable (audit 9/64, 7vb inst.4); impact daemon arm returns weaker answers. Until n74, correctness > speed: route these to the correct local path.
- TLDR-lkx: DataClumps detector nonexistent (always 0 findings), FeatureEnvy empty without --deep. Decision: implement or remove from advertised surface.
- TLDR-bpf cluster: .tldrignore not honored end-to-end — TLDR-1qv (semantic corpus over-indexes excluded dirs = WASTED EMBED TIME + search noise), TLDR-1j2 (watcher gaps), TLDR-9w8 (two divergent matcher impls).
- TLDR-d3s: memory probe reports resident, not phys_footprint — we cannot see the number that matters.

## Cheap proven wins
- TLDR-3rh: ONNX arena/batch bounding — 11.5GB -> <=4GB daemon peak.
- TLDR-yll (observability half): RSS/footprint in daemon status.
- TLDR-zro (Lever A) + TLDR-iqr (Lever B): most of the edit->query latency win without the CSR rewrite.
- TLDR-5kw: token-lean -f compact rendering — the product mission (token savings) applied to the primary agent surface.

## Quality levers (separate lane, measured-ranked per memory 00)
TLDR-utj.3 fusion (lever b) -> TLDR-utj.2 reranker epic -> TLDR-9m2 LLM descriptions (lever c, biggest) -> TLDR-utj.5 MCP client (agent reach) -> TLDR-utj.1 model swap (eval-gated).

## Exclusions (deliberate)
TLDR-7vb dead-path class (calls/smells/complexity daemon arms that NEVER worked): silent fallback already yields CORRECT local answers; fixing dead paths on a surface scheduled for replacement is waste (user-ratified on 7vb). Only the WRONG-ANSWER subset gets the interim fix.

title: Reranker eval + wiring: behind a flag until eval proves the win; provenance in result cards
description: Child of TLDR-utj.2. Before/after recall@5/MRR recorded on the harness; wire into search pipeline post-fusion behind a flag; promote to default only on eval win. Result cards carry reranked-by:<model> provenance. Flag-parity contract applies.

title: Resident reranker in daemon: model lifecycle + pinned-generation join (dead candidates dropped pre-rerank)
description: Child of TLDR-utj.2. Reranker model RESIDENT like the embedder (never per-call cold-load); model-version key dimension; A8 contract upstream: candidates join-verified against pinned generation BEFORE reranking — never spend reranker budget on deleted symbols. Latency budget set by measurement in the decision child.

title: Reranker decision: cross-encoder vs ColBERT — eval-harness shootout + latency/storage trade
description: Child of TLDR-utj.2. Run both candidates on the eval harness (n=14 baseline, expand set if discriminative power too low — the M-vs-L lesson: small n cannot separate close contenders). Measure: recall@5/MRR delta over fusion-only, p50/p99 rerank latency per 100 candidates, storage cost (ColBERT per-token vectors). Cross-encoder: highest accuracy, ~50-200ms/100, zero index cost. ColBERT: faster, storage-heavy, can double as retriever. DECISION recorded as ADR-grade comment with numbers.

title: T3: CAS persistence (warm restarts) + mark-and-sweep GC + byte-budget LRU; retire OnceCell buckets; closes jsi class
description:
Parent: TLDR-17d. Implements T3 persistence + lifecycle for the CAS (spec: TLDR-n74 2026-06-06 comment; Codex F6/F8).

1. On-disk content-addressed store for S2 -> WARM RESTARTS (restart re-parse only verifies hashes; unchanged files' facts load from CAS). Reuse the VectorStore content-keyed persistence pattern (Codex F8), do not fork a second regime.
2. GC = MARK-AND-SWEEP from the current file set: live = body hashes referenced by current parses; sweep on idle/threshold. Per-segment LRU alone is INSUFFICIENT — stale body hashes stay valid keys forever and accumulate across edits (Codex F6).
3. RAM tier: per-segment byte budgets + LRU; eviction always safe (recompute on miss) because keys are content-addressed.
4. RETIRE the daemon per-language OnceCell buckets (state.rs invalidate_caches blunt clear) — the CAS + generationed snapshot replace them; no layered second regime.
5. CLOSES the TLDR-jsi bug class: cache clear = atomic CAS drop; content-addressed keys cannot serve stale-wrong data; daemon quiescence no longer a coherence requirement for clear.
DEPENDS ON: S2 child (the store being persisted).

title: S6: search segments — BM25 per-file delta postings + S2-keyed embeddings (caching contract for utj.3)
description:
Parent: TLDR-17d. Implements segment S6 (spec: TLDR-n74 2026-06-06 comment).

BM25 postings become a resident, per-file-delta-updatable segment: file edit -> remove old file's postings, add new ones — never whole-index rebuild on the query path (today public search rebuilds BM25 + full graph EVERY query; that dies). Embeddings ride S2 (content-keyed, t8f incremental — align, don't fork).
COORDINATES WITH TLDR-utj.3 (BM25 resident in daemon + RRF fusion): utj.3 owns search QUALITY (fusion); this issue owns the CACHING/INVALIDATION contract (delta postings, content keys). Decision input: TLDR-rfz prototype 2 decides rebuild-vs-incremental-postings ON NUMBERS — if full rebuild measures cheap enough, this issue collapses to 'rebuild from warm S2 on dirty' and only the segment KEYING ships.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: CONTRACT ADDITION (A8, user-ratified): embedding lane is ASYNC (L2-async; ONNX ~100ms+/fn, 11.5GB arena class — see TLDR-3rh); results JOIN-VERIFIED against pinned generation (dead keys dropped, positions rebased); response carries embedding-staleness field. Stale = mis-rank possible, dead symbols impossible.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: KEY-CORRECTNESS NOTE (2026-06-06, surfaced in user discussion): the embedding KEY is NOT body-hash alone. Enrichment text includes signature + CALLEES — a callee rename in ANOTHER file changes foo()'s embedding text while foo's body hash is unchanged. Embedding key = (body hash, ENRICHMENT-SLICE hash [signature + resolved callee list from the pinned generation], model ver). Consequence: a compose-input change elsewhere can legitimately queue re-embeds via the async lane — bounded by O(functions whose resolved callee set changed), never whole-corpus. Without this, stale enrichment silently survives cross-file renames.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: A12 (2026-06-06, user-ratified): EMBEDDING KEY IS BODY-LOCAL — key = (body hash, signature hash, model ver). Resolved-callee context is NEVER baked into embedding text; structural context (callers/callees) joins AT QUERY TIME from the pinned generation (O(deg) from L3) and fuses via RRF with dense + BM25 signals (rides utj.3). SUPERSEDES the earlier enrichment-slice-hash note: that note correctly identified the staleness hazard but solved it in the wrong direction (honest key over volatile inputs -> frequent invalidation); A12 removes the volatile input from the artifact instead. CONSEQUENCES: (1) a callee rename re-embeds ZERO functions — only a function's own body edit re-embeds it; (2) query-time context is FRESHER than baked-in (current at query moment vs stale-between-re-embeds); (3) deployed baseline already runs enrich OFF (memory 00) so this costs nothing today; (4) GATE: any future baked-in enrichment proposal must BEAT query-time fusion in the eval harness before earning a place in the embedding key. Design principle extracted: never bake volatile cross-file data into the most expensive immutable artifact — serve volatility from the always-fresh cheap structure.

title: S4/S5: identity-ordered FuncIds + slice-hash reuse tickets for derived artifacts via pinned-generation lookup
description:
Parent: TLDR-17d. Implements S4 identity discipline + S5 reuse tickets (spec: TLDR-n74 2026-06-06 comment; Codex F3/F4).

1. FuncId assignment DETERMINISTIC FROM IDENTITY ORDERING (file, qualified name, ordinal) — never positions. Rides on/coordinates with TLDR-n74's CSR build.
2. SLICE-HASH REUSE TICKETS for derived artifacts: pagerank/betweenness/hubs-rank/SCC/cycles/layers <- blake3(identity table + CSR bytes, positions EXCLUDED, + array lengths); coupling/cohesion <- module-edge hash; inheritance closure <- type-edge hash; clones index <- S2 fingerprint multiset (incremental: re-compare changed functions only); health/debt aggregates <- monoid fold over S2 metric rows.
3. JOIN CONTRACT (the F4 epoch-mismatch killer): artifacts are ALWAYS looked up THROUGH a pinned generation — the pinned snapshot's slice hashes select the artifact. Generation = consistency token; slice hash = reuse ticket. Epoch mixing (old pagerank vector against new node table) is unexpressible by construction, not guarded by checks.
4. Replaces 'first query per generation pays' with 'first query per input-slice-change pays': a body-only edit (no call-edge change) produces a new generation with IDENTICAL topology hash -> pagerank/betweenness survive the rebuild warm.
ARBITRATED: no secondary equality check on hash match (blake3-256; user-ratified drop of Codex F3 rec).
DEPENDS ON: TLDR-n74 (CSR must exist), S2 child (fingerprints, metric rows).

title: S3: closed compose-input fact set — fold receiver/local-type extraction into FileIR; compose goes source-blind
description:
Parent: TLDR-17d. Implements segment S3 (spec: TLDR-n74 2026-06-06 comment).

Define the CLOSED SET of per-file facts compose consumes, each independently blake3-hashed: (a) signature set, (b) call-site list, (c) import list, (d) RECEIVER-INDEX / LOCAL-TYPE FACTS. Item (d) is the load-bearing piece (Codex F5 BLOCKER: today apply_type_resolution scans full file source; Python/TypeScript receiver indexes are built from source text; ImportMap depends on body parse). This issue executes ADR-9's 'fold receiver indexes into FileIR' move: extraction happens ONCE per file-version during the parse walk; compose afterward consumes ONLY these hashed facts — never raw source. The compose key (multiset of per-file S3 hashes) determines S4 rebuild.
NOTE: this is the byte-identical-output incremental step toward lever C (the oracle swap itself stays USER-DEFERRED per TLDR-t0s).
ACCEPTANCE: compose provably source-blind (type-level: compose signature takes facts, not source); frozen-corpus byte-hash gates green; single parse walk computes all hashes (no source re-reads per invalidation cycle, Codex F7).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SPEC ADDITION (A11, user-ratified): document today's fallback/widening (unresolved-receiver) semantics explicitly in the fact-set spec — preserved byte-identically, proven by frozen-corpus gates (closure = capture of today's resolver inputs, Codex-verified per-file-only).

title: S2: per-function fact CAS — (body-hash, env-hash) keyed bundles/metrics/fingerprints/embeddings, position-relative
description:
Parent: TLDR-17d. Implements segment S2 of the cache model (spec: TLDR-n74 2026-06-06 comment).

PER-FUNCTION content-addressed store. Key = (function body hash, FILE SEMANTIC-ENV HASH, lang, analyzer ver) where env = imports + enclosing-class chain (Codex F1: identical text across files is NOT semantically identical — receiver resolution depends on file context). Values, all identity-free:
1. Analysis bundle: source slice + tree + CFG + DFG + SSA — POSITION-RELATIVE to function start (Codex F2: absolute 1-indexed lines are pervasive today in cross_file_types.rs; consumers must be audited; absolute start line + qualified identity live ONLY in the generation node table, rebased at join/render).
2. Metric row: cyclomatic/cognitive/halstead/loc/smell-facts (serves complexity/cognitive/halstead/loc/smells/debt/health/hotspots as one fact set).
3. Normalized token fingerprint (serves clones/dice/similar).
4. Embedding (semantic; already content-keyed per t8f — align keying, don't fork).
INVARIANTS: editing fn A keeps all sibling functions' entries warm (49/50 property); an edit ABOVE a function never invalidates it (body hash is position-independent); import/class-context edits invalidate the whole file's entries HONESTLY. Consumers: reaching-defs, taint, slice, chop, available, dead-stores, cognitive, complexity, smells, clones, semantic — one warm memo serves the whole agent sequence.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE LOCK (A9, user-ratified): env hash = DIRECT imports + enclosing-class chain ONLY, never transitive. Blast-radius pre-measured in TLDR-rfz.

title: [EPIC] Content-addressed cache segment model: warm-once cross-command reuse + minimal invalidation
description:
Tracks implementation of the CONTENT-ADDRESSED CACHE SEGMENT MODEL (spec: TLDR-n74 comment 2026-06-06; Codex review + arbitration: TLDR-5qm same date; user-ratified). Goals: (a) WARM ONCE — cache segments shared across CLI flows, not per-command caches; (b) MINIMAL INVALIDATION — a single-file edit kills only the segments whose input slice actually changed.

## The contract (binding for every child)
Every cached artifact declares its exact input slice; key = blake3 of that slice + (schema, analyzer, grammar) versions; invalidation = key mismatch, NEVER explicit flush. No identity (names/paths/absolute positions) inside cached values — identity lives in the generation node table, joined at query time.

## Segments -> children
- S2 per-function fact CAS (child issue): bundles/metrics/fingerprints/embeddings keyed (body hash, file-env hash)
- S3 closed compose-input set (child issue): receiver/local-type facts fold into FileIR
- S4/S5 reuse tickets (child issue): identity-ordered FuncIds + slice-hash keyed derived artifacts via pinned-generation lookup
- S6 incremental search segments (child issue): BM25 delta postings (coordinates with TLDR-utj.3)
- T3 persistence + GC (child issue): on-disk CAS, mark-and-sweep, byte-budget LRU
- S1 (parse) needs no new work beyond keying discipline; S7 (external truth: churn/diagnostics/doctor/coverage/fix) is local-by-design, NO daemon residency — captured here only as a constraint.

## Invalidation cascade (the acceptance story, end-to-end)
Single-file edit: hash file -> re-parse (one walk computes ALL hashes, no source re-reads) -> diff per-function body hashes -> only changed functions' S2 entries die -> S3 sub-hashes recomputed -> compose-input change? O(V+E) rebuild, new generation -> S5 artifacts reused where slice hashes match (body-only edit keeps pagerank/betweenness/clones warm). Comment edit above a function invalidates NOTHING below it (position-relative S2 + identity-ordered FuncIds).

## Gates
- Blocked by TLDR-rfz (measurement gate: CSR + BM25 + edit-path hash fan-out budgets) — no child starts before its verdict.
- Frozen-corpus byte-hash gates apply to all output-identical changes (TLDR-hqb).
- Codex review per child on TLDR-5qm (standing mandate).

## Kills
TLDR-jsi bug class (cache-clear coherence) becomes unexpressible; daemon per-language OnceCell buckets retire (with ADR-9 Am.2).

title: Measurement gate (pre-n74): CSR + BM25 prototype benchmarks on frozen rust corpus — confirm ADR-9 budgets before implementation
description:
## Purpose (the gate)
Discharge the user-ratified MEASUREMENT GATE (TLDR-5qm comment 2 arbitration of Codex F1 BLOCKER; recorded on TLDR-n74 comments 1/10/24) BEFORE n74 implementation starts. ADR-9's complexity contract ("single-digit ms rebuild at 26k edges", O(V+E) scaling, O(deg)/O(answer) queries) is currently DERIVED from data-structure analysis, not MEASURED on real corpora. ADR-3 discipline: measure before committing. This issue is the research-phase home for that measurement — it blocks TLDR-n74; n74 must not start until the verdict here is recorded.

## Corpus
Primary: frozen rust corpus /tmp/tldr-bench@001fdcb — 745 files / 20,320 funcs / 26,312 edges (TLDR-hqb baseline). If the /tmp copy is gone, regenerate per scripts/bench_phase_timing.sh harness at pin 001fdcb. Scaling check: synthetic 10x/100x replication of the edge list (the 100x point matches ADR-9's monorepo design target ~2.6M edges) — django/zod/nest/caddy corpora optional corroboration.

## Prototype 1 — minimal CSR build + query (the F1 blocker itself)
Throwaway bench (bench/ or /tmp, NOT production code; criterion or simple Instant loops with warmup):
1. INGEST: dump the real edge list once from the existing pipeline (function nodes + call edges with file/line meta) to a flat file — ingest cost is NOT part of the measured rebuild (per ADR-9 the rebuild consumes per-file facts already in RAM).
2. BUILD (the measured core): intern table (symbol->u32), Vec<FuncMeta> SoA nodes sorted by file, forward CSR (offsets+targets+edge_meta), reverse CSR via counting sort, name->SmallVec<FuncId> index, file->Range<FuncId> index, per-file sorted (start,end,FuncId).
3. MEASURE build: full snapshot build time from in-RAM fact vectors at 1x/10x/100x. GATE BUDGET: single-digit ms at 26k edges; scaling consistent with O(V+E) (no superlinear bend at 100x). Record peak RSS of the snapshot at each scale (informs the ~200MB@100x projection in ADR-9).
4. MEASURE queries on the built snapshot: callers/callees by-name (O(deg), expect sub-microsecond); reverse-BFS closure from a hub node (impact, O(touched)); full O(V) dead scan; by-name lookup; cursor binary search. Record p50/p99 over >=1k iterations.
5. ArcSwap publish + read overhead: sanity-check only (expect ns).

## Prototype 2 — resident BM25 (audit 10/64 addition)
Over the same corpus's function texts: (a) full index build time; (b) query latency p50/p99; (c) SINGLE-FILE UPDATE cost both ways — full rebuild vs incremental postings update — and DECIDE rebuild-vs-incremental on the numbers (reserve both seams per audit 10). Tokenization can be naive whitespace+ident-split; this measures the data structure, not search quality.

## Explicitly OUT of gate (do not prototype unless triggered)
Per-name occurrence postings (audit 28/64): prototype ONLY IF the live-scan path measures painful here — postings are a prefilter, never a verification replacement.

## Acceptance / exit criteria
- Numbers recorded as a comment HERE and cross-posted to TLDR-n74; Codex review of methodology+numbers logged on TLDR-5qm (standing mandate).
- VERDICT recorded, one of: CONFIRMED (budgets hold -> ADR-9 contract becomes measured fact; n74 unblocked) | REVISED (budgets fail -> amend ADR-9/TLDR-t0s numbers + n74 acceptance BEFORE implementation; user arbitrates) | INVALIDATED (structural surprise -> back to design; user arbitrates).
- BM25 rebuild-vs-incremental decision recorded on n74.
- Prototype code stays throwaway (committed under bench/ or a scratch branch for reproducibility, never wired into crates).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: GATE ADDITION (Codex F7, cache-segment-model review on TLDR-5qm, user-ratified): EDIT-PATH HASH FAN-OUT benchmark — for a single-file edit on the frozen corpus, measure: file content hash + per-function body hashes + S3 sub-hashes (signatures/call-sites/imports/receiver-facts), ALL computed in the single parse walk (no source re-reads per invalidation cycle), plus post-rebuild S5 slice hashes (blake3 over identity table + CSR bytes) at 1x/10x/100x. BUDGET: hash fan-out fits the ~4ms re-parse envelope at 1x; slice-hash cost at 100x recorded (expect low-ms for ~20MB CSR).

comments.author: Udhayakumar

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
GATE NUMBERS (2026-06-06, autonomous session; full data: bench/rfz-csr-gate branch, bench/rfz-gate/{RESULTS.md,results.json,a9_results.json}; M2 Max 64GB, release, single-threaded; corpus slice 19,852 nodes / 25,987 edges = 98.8% of hqb edges after join).
— CSR BUILD (incl. ArcSwap::store): 1× p50 3.23ms p99 4.10ms → SINGLE-DIGIT-MS BUDGET HOLDS. 10× 44.8ms, 100× 512ms. Decade ratios 13.88×/11.41× — roughly linear, NO accelerating bend, but first decade breaches the pre-registered ~12–13× band. 1% cross-replica rewire: +10%, no divergence.
— 100× PROJECTIONS EXCEEDED: 512ms vs ADR-9 'tens-of-ms'; snapshot 570MB analytic / 920MB footprint Δ vs '~200MB' (prototype double-stores symbols; arena interning shrinks, unlikely to old projection).
— QUERIES: all budgets hold with margin at 1× AND 100×: callers 16ns/267ns, callees 17ns/418ns, by-name 11ns, cursor 20ns/28ns, impact BFS 1.8ns/touched (hub 1750 touched = 3.1µs), dead scan 5.4µs/0.50ms, ArcSwap load 5.8ns.
— BM25 (19,852 docs): build 250ms; query p50 3.4µs p99 57µs; incremental per-file p50 1.27ms p99 5.95ms max 7.81ms vs 250ms rebuild → pre-registered rule fires: INCREMENTAL (sample-scoped; prototype constant doesn't transfer — production needs delta-postings per 94k).
— F7 POST-PARSE HASH FAN-OUT: p50-file 18µs, max-file (785 fns) 334µs — <1ms budget holds. S5 slice hash: 1× 1.9ms, 100× 226ms over 164MB (NOT low-ms — identity table dominates; feeds 17d: hash CSR arrays only ≈13ms, or per-segment).
— A9 BLAST RADIUS (271 commits, 1,865 edit pairs): warm fraction 0.448 CI95 [0.426, 0.470] vs target 0.98 → 49/50-warm FAILS at (commit,file) granularity. Churn when flipped: p50 18 fns. NOT conclusive for save-event granularity (unmeasurable from history). Codex ② Q5: genuine, not artifact; re-scope env key to per-function used-imports is the right design response.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: PROPOSED VERDICT (NOT FINAL — user arbitration required; Codex ② Q7 concurs): **REVISED**. CONFIRMED too strong (100× projections exceeded: 512ms/920MB vs tens-of-ms/200MB; A9 warm 0.448 vs 0.98; first-decade scaling ratio 13.88× out of pre-registered band). INVALIDATED too strong (1× contract holds with 3× margin; all query budgets hold with 10–100× margin at both scales; structure is sound). Required ADR-9/t0s/n74 amendments before implementation (detail in RESULTS.md §7): (1) re-state 100× monorepo numbers to measured; (2) widen/explain decade tolerance; (3) A9 env-key re-scope to per-function used-imports OR accept ~0.45 warm and re-cost S2 CAS hit rate — USER DECISION; (4) 17d: S5 hash CSR arrays only (~13ms@100×), never full identity table; (5) BM25 incremental as default seam, constant re-measured on production structure. Codex rounds ①+② logged on TLDR-5qm: 0 rejections, 0 unresolved disagreements. n74 stays BLOCKED until arbitration.

title: [EPIC] Codex design review: ADR-9/10/11 architecture + per-command CLI flow audit feedback
description:
All Codex review output for the new serving architecture lives here (created 2026-06-05, user-mandated).

## Scope
1. ONE-OFF: whole-design review of ADR-9 (resident graph snapshot / daemon-is-the-database, TLDR-t0s), ADR-10 (no silent fallback, TLDR-14i), ADR-11 (render-at-the-edge / binary transport / compact-first, TLDR-kd6) — recorded as the first comment(s) on this epic.
2. PER-COMMAND: every one of the 64 CLI subcommands audited in TLDR-uiy gets a MANDATORY Codex review of its proposed new flow before the audit entry is considered final. Codex feedback recorded here, cross-referenced to the TLDR-uiy audit comment.

## Method per command
- Claude drafts the flow analysis (tier, lifetime, verdict) per TLDR-uiy method
- Codex reviews the draft (codex rescue subagent)
- Accepted feedback folded into the TLDR-uiy record; rejected feedback recorded here WITH rationale
- Disagreements between Claude and Codex surfaced to user for arbitration

## Relationship
- Parent: TLDR-7pp (implementation epic)
- Sibling: TLDR-uiy (the audit itself — this epic is its review gate)

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CODEX WHOLE-DESIGN REVIEW (one-off, 2026-06-05) — ADR-9/10/11 reviewed adversarially, grounded in repo code. RAW FINDINGS:
F1 BLOCKER/ADR-9: codebase doesn't match the ADR — daemon today caches whole ProjectCallGraph per Language via OnceCell (tldr-daemon/src/state.rs:52-65), IR is HashSet/HashMap (cross_file_types.rs:1534-1624), so CSR complexity + 'single-digit ms rebuild' claims are projections, not measured on the real pipeline. REC: benchmark a minimal CSR prototype on real corpora before committing.
F2 MAJOR/ADR-10: current CLI is fallback-FIRST by design (daemon_router.rs:28-131 returns None on any error -> local compute; calls.rs:113-192). ADR-10 rollout is a hard UX break unless every call site updates in lockstep; no central daemon-required contract exists. REC: shared routing policy per command class, structured daemon-missing errors, explicit --oneshot/--local, upgrade-aware messaging.
F3 MAJOR/ADR-11: transport today is axum HTTP + JSON envelopes (server.rs:121-153); no rkyv/bincode path, no schema negotiation, version field unused for handshake. rkyv has alignment/validation/schema-evolution/debuggability sharp edges. REC: versioned envelope + conservative serializer (bincode) first, JSON debug fallback until binary path proves stable; rkyv only with a concrete daemon/CLI-skew migration story.
F4 MAJOR/cross-cutting: dirty-state + concurrent rebuild UNSPECIFIED — what happens when N requests hit a stale snapshot, build fails midway, or watcher invalidates during in-flight rebuild. Today invalidation is blunt clear() (state.rs:231-239). REC: generationed snapshot protocol — readers pin a generation, rebuild off to the side, atomic publish only on success, explicit 'stale-but-serving' / 'rebuild-failed' semantics.
F5 MAJOR/ADR-9+11: 'answer blobs die' cost model incomplete — daemon still returns owned Vecs of edges/strings; no answer cursors/paging/partial materialization specified, so compact-first is not operational yet. REC: define output contracts per query class (full/paged/summary/compact) and verify token savings on real calls/impact/dead/semantic outputs.
F6 MINOR/ADR-10: version-skew underspecified — socket path embeds version (server.rs:49-71) but no handshake; reachable-but-incompatible daemon fails at deserialization. REC: handshake endpoint enforced before routing index-backed commands.
F7 MINOR/ADR-9: 'daemon IS the database' framing oversells — the real design is a typed query service over a resident generationed index; naming it a database drags in semantics the implementation doesn't need.
VERDICT (Codex): direction plausible, design overconfident vs current code. Top-3 before implementation: (1) prototype-benchmark the CSR claims, (2) redesign per-command routing policy with escape hatches before flipping ADR-10, (3) concrete transport compatibility + failure-mode matrix before replacing JSON.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CLAUDE ASSESSMENT of Codex whole-design review (accept/reject per epic method; disagreements -> user arbitration):
F1: ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED. Codex misreads ADR-9 as a claim about current code (it is the TARGET spec; OnceCell/HashMap state is exactly what's being replaced). But the KERNEL is valid and is pure ADR-3 discipline: 'single-digit ms rebuild' and the O() contract are derived from data-structure analysis, NOT measured on a prototype. PROPOSED ACTION: add a MEASUREMENT GATE to TLDR-n74 — minimal CSR build benchmark on the frozen rust corpus (745/20,320/26,312) must confirm the rebuild budget BEFORE the snapshot lands. Downgrade severity BLOCKER->MAJOR (it blocks ratification of n74's perf claims, not the design direction).
F2: ACCEPT. Known (it is what TLDR-npl exists for) but the concrete recs are better than npl's current sketch: per-command-CLASS routing policy table (which the TLDR-uiy audit is literally producing), structured daemon-missing error type, --oneshot/--local escape hatches, upgrade-aware messaging. FOLD INTO TLDR-npl.
F3: ACCEPT-AS-HEDGE. Within ADR-11's letter ('rkyv preferred OR bincode acceptable'). Codex tips the order: bincode + versioned envelope FIRST, JSON debug fallback retained, rkyv only with a skew-migration story. NEEDS USER ARBITRATION (changes ADR-11's stated preference).
F4: ACCEPT — strongest finding. ADR-9 says 'lazy parallel recompose' but never specifies single-flight/generation semantics. Needs an ADR-9 AMENDMENT: generationed snapshot protocol + an explicit user decision on stale-reads policy (serve-old-while-rebuilding vs block-until-fresh). USER ARBITRATION REQUIRED on the stale-reads question.
F5: ACCEPT — and note the per-command output-contract work IS epic TLDR-uiy; each audit entry will now record the command's output contract (full/paged/summary/compact). Token verification on real outputs = TLDR-5kw acceptance criterion.
F6: ACCEPT. Fold handshake-before-routing into TLDR-npl (pairs with F2).
F7: NOTE ONLY. Rhetorical: 'typed query service over a resident generationed index' IS ADR-9 minus the slogan. No action.
PROCESS CHANGES ADOPTED FROM THIS REVIEW: every TLDR-uiy audit entry from 2/64 onward records (a) index-backed vs FS-truth (ADR-10 Am.1), (b) OUTPUT CONTRACT class (F5), (c) routing policy class (F2).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ARBITRATION OUTCOME (2026-06-05, user): F1 -> measurement gate ratified, recorded on TLDR-n74. F3 -> BINCODE-FIRST ratified = ADR-11 Amendment 1 (TLDR-kd6). F4 -> generationed snapshot + BLOCK-UNTIL-FRESH + single-flight ratified = ADR-9 Amendment 1 (TLDR-t0s). F2+F6 -> folded into TLDR-npl scope. F5 -> output-contract class becomes a standard field of every TLDR-uiy audit entry. F7 -> note only. WHOLE-DESIGN REVIEW COMPLETE — per-command reviews proceed under the method in this epic's description.

This epic now continues as the STANDING Codex-review log for the TLDR-n74 implementation phase: every substantive n74/7pp implementation step gets Codex review recorded here, same method (Claude drafts -> Codex reviews -> accepted feedback folded in, rejections recorded with rationale, disagreements arbitrated by user).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CODEX REVIEW: content-addressed cache segment model (2026-06-06; Claude re-think of the 6-cluster flow table for cross-command cache reuse + minimal invalidation). RAW FINDINGS:
F1 BLOCKER: body-hash sharing across files UNSOUND — identical text != identical semantics; receiver resolution reads full source/imports/enclosing class (resolution.rs apply_type_resolution, Python/TypeScript receiver indexes, ImportResolver keyed on current_file).
F2 MAJOR: position-relative bundles shift correctness risk to render; absolute 1-indexed lines pervasive (cross_file_types.rs, semantic/types.rs); enclosing_class_for_call compares absolute lines.
F3 MAJOR: topology-hash keying needs collision hardening; repo already treats u64 collisions as correctness bugs (vector_store.rs).
F4 BLOCKER: dropping generation as cache key cross-wires epochs — FuncId remap with UNCHANGED topology hash joins old pagerank vectors to new node tables = silently wrong answers.
F5 BLOCKER: 'compose never reads bodies' FALSE today — apply_type_resolution scans full source; receiver indexes built from source text; ImportMap depends on body parse.
F6 MAJOR: CAS + per-segment LRU grows forever; needs reachability GC (semantic cache only removes on explicit delete/delta today).
F7 MAJOR: hashing fan-out (file + per-function + S3 sub-hashes + slice hashes) unproven vs ~4ms edit budget; avoid multiple source re-reads per invalidation cycle.
F8 MINOR: reuse VectorStore content-keyed machinery for S2 persistence; retire daemon language-bucket OnceCell rather than layering a second regime.
VERDICT (Codex): directionally sound, not implementation-ready; safest path = env-hash-scoped S2 keys, (generation, topology_hash) joint artifact keying, retire OnceCell.
CLAUDE ASSESSMENT: F1 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED (S2 key gains file semantic-env hash; preserves 49/50-warm; cross-file dedup sacrificed, was never the goal). F2 ACCEPT (relative positions in bundles; identity + absolute position only in node table; all joins via pinned snapshot). F3 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED (blake3-256 over canonical bytes + lengths; secondary equality check REJECTED — rec targets weak/rolling hashes, 256-bit collision negligible). F4 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED (identity-ordered FuncId assignment + lookup-through-pinned-generation: generation = consistency token, slice hash = reuse ticket; stronger than Codex's joint-key suggestion — epoch mixing becomes unexpressible rather than guarded). F5 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED (contract stands, set was wrong: S3 = closed compose-input set INCLUDING receiver/local-type facts; confirms FileIR receiver-fold is load-bearing). F6 ACCEPT (mark-and-sweep from current file set). F7 ACCEPT (benchmark added to TLDR-rfz gate). F8 ACCEPT.
ARBITRATION (user, 2026-06-06): all accepted as amended; F3 equality-check DROP ratified. Spec recorded as comment on TLDR-n74.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CODEX REVIEW: layer taxonomy (L0-L5 + side lane) + anticipated-benefit claims (2026-06-06). RAW: F1 MAJOR B2 'near-zero on comment edits' oversold (no normalization exists; body hash changes); F2 MAJOR B1 '~3s repayment' should be scoped to DaemonState buckets (semantic store already has delta warmth); F3 MAJOR B3 'search rebuilds BM25+graph every query' WRONG — cited get_or_build_bm25 caching + regex search handler; F4 MAJOR L2 'two grains one layer' elides two invalidation regimes (corpus_digest vs per-chunk); F5 MAJOR embeddings operationally incompatible with us-scale structural facts (ONNX inference, model-version key, daemon blocking) — split to sublayer/async lane; F6 MAJOR aggregates slice-keying useless (any edit changes the slice) + taxonomy omits the EXISTING QueryCache/salsa.rs resident memo layer; F7 MINOR 'never daemon-resident' side-lane overclaims. VERDICT: taxonomy 'not sound as written'; benefits B1/B4/B5/B6 real-or-partial, B2/B3/B7 oversold-or-wrong.
CLAUDE ASSESSMENT (re-verified against the REAL in-CLI daemon, not the unwired axum crate):
F1 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED — B2 restated honestly: edit ABOVE a function = zero; edit INSIDE a body = that function's structural facts recompute (cheap) + embedding refresh (NOT cheap -> moved to async lane per F5); no L3 rebuild (compose-input hashes unchanged). Comment-stripped normalized hashing PARKED as a measured lever (complicates position-correct rendering; per-function recompute likely cheap enough).
F2 ACCEPT — B1 scoped to the graph path / DaemonState buckets; semantic vector store's existing delta warmth (t8f) credited; ~3s = campaign-measured parse+compose, applies to graph rebuild repayment.
F3 REJECT WITH EVIDENCE — TWO-DAEMONS TRAP, THIRD STRIKE. Codex cited crates/tldr-daemon/ (UNWIRED axum crate). The REAL public path: tldr search -> enriched_search -> Bm25Index::from_project INLINE (enriched.rs:925,986) + build_project_call_graph (enriched.rs:1593); CLI search.rs has NO daemon routing; in-repo test comment (enriched.rs:2214) states 'from_project() rebuilds every time'. B3 STANDS. Reinforces the n74 spec-opens-with-host-decision rule.
F4 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED — L2 stays ONE layer; grains named explicitly (L2a function-grain S2, L2b file-grain S3); RULE CLARIFIED: layer membership = key/lifetime class (content-derived, project-blind), not downstream effect. Codex's corpus_digest evidence is current-code, not target.
F5 ACCEPT — embeddings become L2-ASYNC sub-lane: same content keys + model-version dimension, refresh DECOUPLED from the synchronous edit path; queries serve last-good embedding with staleness provenance. Aligns with resident-Embedder IndexManager design.
F6 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED — (a) whole-project aggregates (health/debt) DROPPED from L4 reuse tickets: recomputed per generation as O(V) fold over warm L2 rows (us-ms at 20k funcs; slice-hashing the fold input is itself O(V)). (b) QueryCache/salsa.rs is NOT a missing layer — it is a layer the design RETIRES (memoized materialized answers = the answer-blob sin ADR-9 kills); taxonomy now states its fate explicitly.
F7 ACCEPT-AS-AMENDED — side lane reworded: external-truth ANSWERS (git/tool/env-scoped) never served from daemon caches; daemon operational state is out of taxonomy scope.
NET: taxonomy survives with named grains + async embedding lane + explicit QueryCache retirement + honest B1/B2 wording; B3 reaffirmed against Codex; B7 clarified as TARGET cost model.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CODEX REVIEW ROUNDS 2+3: corrected cache plan (2026-06-06).
ROUND 2 — F3 CONCESSION: Codex re-examined the real search path and CONCEDED its layer-taxonomy F3: 'search.rs:68-120 SmartSearchArgs::run calls enriched_search directly, no daemon client; Bm25Index::from_project inline at enriched.rs:923-926 and 985-987; build_project_call_graph per invocation at enriched.rs:1583-1599; get_or_build_bm25 wired only into the separate daemon binary, never the CLI search flow. CONCEDE — B3 stands: public search pays full BM25+graph build per invocation.' Two-daemons trap third strike CONFIRMED by the reviewer itself.
ROUND 2 NEW FINDINGS on corrected plan: (a) MAJOR async-embedding consistency — stale embeddings could surface deleted/renamed symbols unless enrichment resolves against current facts; response staleness contract undefined. (b) BLOCKER L2b receiver-facts 'not closable project-blind for Python/TS dynamic dispatch'. (c) MAJOR env-hash blast radius underspecified. Plus ungated items: BM25 rebuild-vs-delta (already gated — noted), async-lane benchmarks, L4 ticket granularity, env-hash blast radius. Verdict round 2: NOT research-complete.
CLAUDE COURSE-CORRECTION (amendments A8-A11): A8 semantic results JOIN-VERIFIED against pinned generation (dead keys dropped, positions rebased from node table), response carries embedding-staleness field — stale embeddings can mis-rank, never surface dead symbols. A9 env hash = DIRECT imports + enclosing-class chain ONLY, never transitive (transitive effects flow through compose/L3); blast-radius measurement -> rfz gate. A10 L4 ticket granularity: start with the 4 coarse slices, refine on post-implementation hit-rate telemetry, not speculation. A11 closability REFRAMED (partial disagreement with the BLOCKER): the plan never claimed to solve dynamic dispatch — it claims the fact set captures EXACTLY today's resolver inputs; today's resolver is already per-file by construction; the byte-hash gates mechanically PROVE input closure (under-capture -> output divergence on 5 corpora); existing fallback/widening semantics get DOCUMENTED, not invented.
ROUND 3 — FULL CONVERGENCE: Codex re-examined resolution.rs L258-301 + type_resolver.rs L426-612 (Python builder) + L828-1005 (TS builder): 'no cross-file or global mutable input found' — AGREE on closability reframe (BLOCKER dissolved to documented-semantics amendment). AGREE A8 (resolution path consumes no embeddings; pinned join affects ranking only). AGREE A9 (consistent with observed resolver inputs). AGREE A10. AGREE A11 (fallback/widening already explicit in per-file scans). FINAL VERDICT: 'COMPLETE — pending the measurement gate only.'

title: [EPIC] CLI x ADR-9 fit audit: every command walked through the tier model, one at a time
description:
Systematic design review (started 2026-06-05, user-driven): walk EVERY CLI subcommand through the ADR-9/10/11 architecture (tier model, resident CSR snapshot, render-at-the-edge, no-silent-fallback) and record whether its flow fits, what it needs, and any design decisions that fall out. The audit IS the design review for epic TLDR-7pp — misfits surface here before implementation, not during.

## Method (per command, recorded as a comment on this epic)
1. WHAT it computes (inputs -> output)
2. WHICH tier serves it (T0 filesystem / T1 per-file facts / T2 project snapshot+indexes / T3 persist / per-query compute)
3. LIFETIME of its answer (per file-version / project-version / query / external-input)
4. VERDICT: CLEAN-FIT | FIT-WITH-NOTE (works, needs a recorded design note) | MISFIT (needs design work — gets its own issue)
5. DECISIONS arising (added to this epic; promoted to ADR if architectural)

## Command inventory (64, audit order = help order)
tree structure calls impact dead reaching-defs taint available slice search context smells extract imports importers complexity churn debt health hubs whatbreaks patterns inheritance change-impact deps diagnostics doctor references clones dice loc cognitive halstead coverage hotspots embed semantic similar daemon cache warm stats surface contracts dead-stores chop specs invariants verify cohesion temporal resources coupling interface explain todo secure definition diff api-check vuln fix bugbot help

## Expected clusters (hypothesis, to be verified command-by-command)
- Graph queries (calls/impact/dead/hubs/...) -> T2 CSR primitives
- Per-file/per-function analyses (complexity/cfg/dfg/slice/...) -> T1 facts or on-demand per-file compute
- Semantic flow (search/embed/similar) -> Flow-2, untouched (boundary per utj note)
- External-tool wrappers (diagnostics/doctor/fix) -> out of scope for the tier model, but ADR-10 routing still applies
- Infra (daemon/cache/warm/stats) -> the machinery itself
close_reason: Audit complete: all 64 CLI subcommands walked through the ADR-9 tier model, each Codex-reviewed (TLDR-5qm) and user-ratified. Outputs: 7 ADR amendments + 15 cross-cutting contracts folded into TLDR-n74 spec; divergence/bug findings filed as TLDR-7vb, TLDR-lkx, TLDR-jsi. TLDR-5qm stays open as the standing Codex-review log for the n74 implementation phase.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 1/64: tree — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE.
WHAT: file tree (--ext filter, -H hidden). Today: whole-project JSON blob (warm step 3) / cold FS walk.
TIER: not a graph query — a RENDERING of the file table (ADR-11's purest case). Daemon ships filtered path list; CLI renders.
LIFETIME: invalidated by create/delete/rename, NOT content edits — different class than FileIR memo.
DECISIONS ARISING:
- D1 (-> TLDR-iqr/TLDR-4vb): watcher contract gains EVENT-TYPE granularity: modify -> evict 1 memo entry + dirty; create/delete/rename -> file-table version bump + dirty.
- D2 (-> TLDR-bpf): resident FILE TABLE (sorted paths + per-dir Range slices + per-entry flags lang/ignored/hidden) becomes a first-class T2 artifact, shared with the unified ignore oracle — one membership source of truth.
- D3: raw-FS views (-H etc.) are EXPLICIT live FS walks — they query the filesystem, not the index; honest semantics, and the resident table never holds node_modules/target junk (millions of paths at 100x).
OPEN QUESTION (ADR-10 boundary, awaiting user): loud-failure for ALL commands vs only INDEX-BACKED answers (FS-truth commands like tree -H / doctor run local — nothing to be stale about). Recommendation: index-backed only.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: OPEN QUESTION RESOLVED (ADR-10 boundary): user ratified INDEX-BACKED ONLY. Recorded as Amendment 1 on TLDR-14i. Per-command classification (index-backed vs FS-truth) becomes a standard field of each audit entry from 2/64 onward. ALSO: process change from this point — every audit entry gets a MANDATORY Codex review before being considered final; Codex feedback logged on new epic TLDR-5qm (whole-design review of ADR-9/10/11 happens first, also on TLDR-5qm).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 2/64: structure — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE. (Codex-reviewed: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES, log on TLDR-5qm.)
WHAT: per-file functions/classes/imports for a path -> CodeStructure. Today: daemon whole-JSON blob OR silent local fallback; language auto-detect silently defaults to PYTHON; one language per call; max_results = hard truncation (stops at N files, no marker).
TIER: T2 projection — node table sliced via file->Range<FuncId> (O(slice); directory = concat slices over file-table subtree) + imports folded into snapshot per D1.
LIFETIME: project-version. ADR-10 CLASS: index-backed -> daemon-required. OUTPUT CONTRACT: paged-list (first command where O(answer) = whole project at 100x; scoped by default, whole-project dump = T3 export).
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05):
- D1: per-file IMPORT summaries FOLD INTO the T2 snapshot at compose (one-tier read; import resolution already feeds the project-version module index). Serves structure/imports/importers/deps.
- D2 -> PROMOTED to ADR-9 Amendment 2 (TLDR-t0s): snapshot is LANGUAGE-UNIFIED — one snapshot, unified FuncId space, file table carries per-file language, -l becomes a filter, silent Python default dies. Per-language normalization to be defined in TLDR-n74 spec.
- D3 (cross-cutting, applies to ALL list-shaped commands from here on): PAGED-LIST standard contract — total count + truncation marker + continuation cursor in the response envelope; replaces hard caps. Lands with bincode envelope in TLDR-n74.
CODEX NEW FIND folded: ignore-spec DIVERGENCE today — local fallback uses IgnoreSpec::default(), daemon structure handler passes None -> silently different results between paths. Cross-filed to TLDR-bpf (one-oracle rule); ADR-10's dual-path kill also kills this divergence class. NEXT: 3/64 calls.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 3/64: calls — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE (+ live defects filed as TLDR-7vb). (Codex-reviewed: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES; log on TLDR-5qm — NOTE: Codex grounded two BLOCKERs in the WRONG daemon, the unwired tldr-daemon axum crate; I re-verified against the real in-CLI daemon and the conclusions held with different evidence.)
WHAT: whole cross-file call graph — nodes = full function inventory (BUG-AGG12-4) + edges truncated at max_items=200 via LEXICAL STRING SORT as 'importance'. json/text/dot (dot already renders at the CLI edge — ADR-11-conformant).
TIER: T2 — purest snapshot client; the answer IS the CSR enumerated. O(page) serving; whole-graph dump = T3 export (100x full JSON ~850MB disqualified).
LIFETIME: project-version. ADR-10: index-backed -> daemon-required. OUTPUT CONTRACT: paged-list.
DEFECTS CONFIRMED (filed TLDR-7vb, closed by n74): (A) daemon path DEAD — schema mismatch -> silent fallback always; (B) V1-vs-V2 oracle split daemon-vs-local; (C) warm step caches an answer no consumer can deserialize. ADR-10 exhibit.
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05):
- D1: canonical paging order = SNAPSHOT ORDER (FuncId-major, deterministic across identical snapshots); lexical sort-on-truncate dies (at 100x it would sort 2.6M strings to show 200).
- D2: SPLIT the conflated answer — calls = EDGES (paged, snapshot order); node INVENTORY is its own paged view; envelope totals preserve BUG-AGG12-4 faithful-count intent. Schema break versioned with the bincode envelope (n74).
- D3 (cross-cutting -> TLDR-npl): FLAG-PARITY RULE — every behavior-affecting CLI flag rides the request envelope; the daemon never substitutes its own defaults for unforwarded flags. (Today: DaemonCommand::Calls lacks max_items/respect_ignore; router does not even forward language.)
- D4 (-> TLDR-n74): TWO daemon implementations in-tree (real: tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs 104KB, what daemon start spawns; unwired: tldr-daemon axum crate with divergent schemas). n74 spec MUST OPEN with the host decision. MISFIT-class finding, recorded not yet ratified.
NEXT: 4/64 impact.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 4/64: impact — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE (+ divergence instances appended to class issue TLDR-7vb). (Codex: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES, all 4 suspects CONFIRMED with line refs + 1 NEW defect found — cache-key collision; log on TLDR-5qm.)
WHAT: transitive callers by function NAME, --depth 5, --file filter, --type-aware. TIER: T2 — THE reverse-CSR case (BFS over reverse adjacency, O(touched V+E); ADR-9 names impact as why reverse CSR is load-bearing). LIFETIME: project-version. ADR-10: index-backed. OUTPUT CONTRACT: paged-list; dot renders at edge (conformant).
DEFECTS -> TLDR-7vb instance 2: enrichment parity (silently weaker daemon answers), --file/--lang flag drop + cache-key collision (filtered query gets unfiltered answer), type-aware fabricated zeros, V1-vs-V2 cross-command graph split.
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05):
- D1 SYMBOL-ADDRESSING CONTRACT (cross-cutting -> n74 query-primitive spec): resolution via snapshot name-hash index BEFORE traversal entry; ambiguous name -> ALL candidates as multi-target answer (existing impact_analysis precedent, analysis/impact.rs:131-175); --file/--class narrow; silent-pick banned. Applies to impact/whatbreaks/references/callers/dead-entry and all symbol-addressed commands.
- D2 ENRICHMENT = TRANSITIONAL MARKED ORACLE: reference-scan enrichment survives as explicitly-MARKED secondary pass (output labels enrichment-sourced callers); per-language it DIES when that language's resolver is fixed at compose (TLDR-olg); end state = compose-only, query path never touches source text.
- S3: --type-aware flag RETIRED at n74 cutover (type resolution is compose-time default; fabricated stats worse than no flag).
- Codex correction folded into D1: disambiguation needs no new mechanism — document the name-hash -> BFS handoff; impact already returns all matches.
NOTE: --file filter semantics today = suffix-or-exact match (not glob) — carry into envelope spec docs. depth=0 -> current node + truncated:true (documented). NEXT: 5/64 dead.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 5/64: dead — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE (+ divergence instance 3 on TLDR-7vb: ALGORITHM FORK by daemon state). (Codex: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES, all 4 suspects confirmed w/ line refs; log on TLDR-5qm.)
WHAT: never-called functions. Today: local default = REFCOUNT (identifier-occurrence counting); --call-graph = V1 graph reachability; daemon = ALWAYS graph mode -> algorithm varies by daemon liveness. CLI-side extras local-only: framework directive tagging (reads source in CLI layer), .d.ts exclusion, MAX_FILES=10_000 silent cap.
TIER: T2 — entry-point BFS over forward CSR + zero-in-degree on reverse CSR, O(V) per ADR-9 contract. LIFETIME: project-version. ADR-10: index-backed. OUTPUT CONTRACT: paged-list.
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05):
- D1 ONE ANSWER, TWO SIGNALS: graph reachability PRIMARY; per-name identifier-occurrence counts become a COMPOSE-TIME fact in the snapshot serving as per-finding CONFIDENCE (dead+refcount=0 high; refcount>0 dynamic-use suspect). Refcount classification semantics (ref_count>1 threshold, short-name guard, visibility-based possibly_dead/dead) explicitly preserved in the confidence layer — gated by differential test vs today's refcount mode. Algorithm fork + --call-graph flag DIE.
- D2 FRAMEWORK FACTS AT COMPOSE: directives (use server/client — SEPARATE labels, today conflated), .d.ts classification, entry-point decorators become FileIR facts (file-version lifetime); CLI stops reading source text. Edge-case rules (5-line window etc.) specified in n74; preserved byte-identically first.
- D3 (folded, mechanical): entry points resolve via the SYMBOL-ADDRESSING contract (today: name-pattern-only matching; cache key hashes joined strings — collision-prone).
- D4 (folded): MAX_FILES cap DIES on the snapshot path (no walk); on explicit --oneshot it becomes a LOUD envelope truncation marker, never silent.
NEXT: 6/64 reaching-defs.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 6/64: reaching-defs — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE. (Codex: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES — corrected 3 of my 5 suspects; log on TLDR-5qm.) FIRST OF THE PER-FUNCTION CFG/DFG CLUSTER (templates taint/available/slice/cfg/dfg).
WHAT: per-(file,function) def-use/use-def chains, uninitialized uses, IN/OUT sets; --var/--line edge-side post-filters (conformant, answer is small); NO daemon route today (main.rs:627 dispatches local; daemon's Cfg arm serves only tldr cfg).
TIER: T1 — per file-version lifetime, no project snapshot needed. ADR-10: INDEX-BACKED per Am.2 (ratified this audit): served from content-hash-keyed T1 memos = the EXISTING daemon LRU query-cache (512MB, file-hash invalidation) evolved into the T1 tier — Codex correction accepted: no new mechanism. OUTPUT CONTRACT: summary (per-function report, small).
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05):
- D1 CLUSTER DECISION -> ADR-10 AMENDMENT 2 (TLDR-14i): per-function analyses are index-backed via T1 memo; FS-truth narrowed to raw-FS-metadata commands.
- D3 (folded): --params flag RETIRED — Codex found it is DEAD SURFACE (declared reaching_defs.rs:58-60, never threaded into run(); core's build_reaching_defs_report_with_params exists unused).
- D2 (folded, corrected): unsupported-format loud failure ALREADY enforced at main.rs:584-590 validator; the dot/sarif fallback arms in reaching_defs.rs:140-151 are unreachable DEAD CODE — remove at cutover.
NOTES: Python default on extension-detect failure (reaching_defs.rs:70-73) — dies under ADR-9 Am.2 (language from file table). -f compact emits IDENTICAL JSON to -f json — no token-lean rendering for this cluster -> TLDR-5kw scope. NEXT: 7/64 taint.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 7/64: taint — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE (cluster template applies cleanly). (Codex: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES, all claims verified w/ line refs; log on TLDR-5qm.)
WHAT: per-(file,function) taint flows (sources/sinks/flows, SQL/cmd/code injection). Local-always today (VERIFIED: no DaemonCommand::Taint arm exists). Heavier pipeline than reaching-defs: source read + statements map + full-file ParserPool parse + minimal SSA, with graceful degradation chain (no tree -> regex detection; no SSA -> VarRef path).
TIER: T1 per Am.2 — per file-version lifetime. ADR-10: index-backed. OUTPUT CONTRACT: summary (per-function, small). Template smells confirmed: Python default (taint.rs:58-60), compact==json (taint.rs:144), dot/sarif arms dead behind main.rs validator.
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05):
- T1 BUNDLE GRANULARITY (cluster-wide): the T1 memo entry is the SHARED PER-FILE BUNDLE (source bytes + tree + CFG + DFG + SSA, content-hash keyed) — slice/reaching-defs/taint on one function share one memo; source bytes ride in the bundle (Codex: compute_taint_with_tree takes source:&[u8], so eliminating CLI reads requires bundling source). LRU bounds the working set.
- PROVENANCE MARKER (folded — impact marked-oracle pattern applied): the silent quality-degradation chain gets an explicit envelope field analysis_mode: ast+ssa | ast | regex. Same honesty class as enrichment provenance.
NOTE: SARIF is rejected for taint today but is the NATURAL format for security findings — boundary note for the project-wide secure/vuln audits later (taint=per-function dive; secure=project-wide scan). Known pre-existing taint test failures (TLDR-6q2/wks ~36) — no interaction observed. NEXT: 8/64 available.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 8/64: available — VERDICT: CLEAN-FIT (first of the audit; cluster template applies exactly). (Codex: AGREE-WITH-CHANGES on minor notes; log on TLDR-5qm.)
WHAT: per-(file,function) available-expressions/CSE analysis; query modes --check/--at_line/--killed_by return small focused answers; --cse_only filter. Local-always today (verified, no daemon arm). TIER: T1 bundle per Am.2. LIFETIME: file-version. ADR-10: index-backed. OUTPUT: summary + query modes.
NOTES: (a) compact INCONSISTENCY across cluster — available minifies (to_string), reaching-defs/taint pretty-print for compact -> TLDR-5kw; (b) dot/sarif fall through _ arm to pretty JSON (unsupported; validator guards; document as unsupported); (c) core fn needs source_lines &[String] — T1 bundle source-bytes suffices, lines reconstructed at use; (d) Python default (dies under Am.2).
DECISION RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-05): QUERY-MODES promoted to CROSS-CUTTING ENVELOPE CONTRACT — commands declare named modes (full | focused sub-queries); agents asking a focused question never pay for the full report; daemon computes only what the mode needs (O(answer), ADR-11 token economy). Each subsequent audit records which modes the command should offer. NEXT: 9/64 slice.

comments.text:

OUTPUT CONTRACT: summary; RICH SHAPE IS THE ONLY SHAPE in target (legacy thin format dies). QUERY-MODES: OOR diagnostic (slice_oor_explanation) is already a focused mode — present on BOTH paths (verified, no parity gap there); natural modes: full | lines-only | oor-check.
TEMPLATE CONFORMANCE: compact rides JSON path; sarif/dot properly absent from allowlists (intentionally unsupported — cleaner than reaching-defs/taint dead arms); Python default (dies under Am.2).
DECISIONS: direction/variable/mode become envelope + cache-key dimensions (flag-parity, already ratified — this is its starkest justification). NO interim fix (user, 2026-06-05): wait for n74, calls precedent. NEXT: 10/64 search.

Am.2 note: search spans the project (language-unified); -l becomes filter; Python default dies. NEXT: 11/64 context.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AUDIT 20/64: hubs — VERDICT: FIT-WITH-NOTE. (Codex: partially-noncompliant current; log on TLDR-5qm.)
WHAT: hub/centrality detection (in_degree/out_degree/pagerank/betweenness, --algorithm, top-k). No daemon route today; builds V1 graph per query.
TIER — THREE COST CLASSES + NEW RATIFIED PATTERN: (a) degree hubs = CSR degree arrays (offsets[i+1]-offsets[i]), O(V log k) holds (today: HashMap projections — rewire); (b) pagerank = per-generation LAZY T2-DERIVED ARTIFACT; (c) betweenness = lazy derived + SAMPLED DEFAULT at scale.
DECISIONS RATIFIED (user, 2026-06-06):
- LAZY T2-DERIVED ARTIFACTS: expensive whole-graph derivations computed on first request per snapshot generation, cached for the generation lifetime (immutable; invalidation free via generations). Third artifact class: T2-core / T1 bundles / T2-derived.
- BETWEENNESS POLICY: above threshold, deterministic SAMPLED approximation by default, MARKED in output (provenance exact|sampled(k)); --exact opt-in with cost warning. TODAY'S SILENT-ZEROS PATH DIES (hubs.rs core: n>5000 silently returns all-zero scores presented as real — worst honesty defect of the metric family).
DEFECTS: silent-zero betweenness (above), silent top-k truncation, per-query graph builds. LIFETIME: project-version (per-generation for derived). ADR-10: index-backed. OUTPUT: paged-list per algorithm. NEXT: 21/64 whatbreaks.
