title: ADR-11: Render-at-the-edge — binary daemon-to-CLI transport; all user formats are CLI-side renderings; compact is the primary surface; 100x scale is the design target
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-05, user-ratified) Three bindings:
1. INTERNAL TRANSPORT IS BINARY: the daemon->CLI hop is machine-to-machine and ours on both ends — it carries rkyv (preferred; zero-copy, the bytes ARE the structure) or bincode (acceptable), never a JSON document. The expensive boundary ceases to exist; nothing is serialized 'constantly, again and again, periodically' for internal movement.
2. FORMATTING COST IS PAID ONLY FOR WHAT THE HUMAN/AGENT ASKED TO SEE (the ratified philosophy): -f json / jsonl / text / compact are RENDERINGS applied at the CLI edge to the (usually small) answer. JSON demotes from 'the data' to 'one view of the data' — no consumer contract breaks, and rendering 200 rows costs nothing.
3. -f compact IS THE PRIMARY SURFACE (user: 'will be the most used method'): the agent-facing token-lean rendering is a first-class product feature, measured in TOKENS (the product metric), not milliseconds. JSON key-repetition costs 2-4x tokens for identical information.

## Scale target: 100x current (user-stated plan)
~75k files / ~2M functions / ~2.6M edges. Derived budgets that this ADR's choices must hold at:
- Snapshot resident ~200-300MB; rebuild O(V+E) ~50-150ms (lazy per edit) — holds.
- Graph walks 10-50ms worst case — holds (linear math).
- Full dump: JSON ~850MB/5-8s = DISQUALIFIED at target scale; rkyv view ~50-80MB/sub-second = required, not optional. This is why transport-binary is an architecture decision, not an optimization.
- First warm ~25-35s once; per-save parallel recompose ~1-3s; Tier-1 memo ~1-3GB.

## Tripwires armed (measure, then act — ADR-3 discipline)
- T1: if per-save recompose at real scale measures painful -> incremental compose re-enters the design space.
- T2: if restart (~30s re-parse at 100x) or memo RAM (~GBs) measures painful -> UNPARK TLDR-zde's disk chunk store (rkyv per-file facts, mmap restart) — it slots under Tier 1 unchanged; the seam was kept clean for exactly this.
- T3: rebuild-vs-surgery for the CSR snapshot revisited only on measured rebuild pain.

## Rejected
- Swapping the user-facing default format as a PERF measure (perf lives in the transport + answer-size, not the edge rendering).
- Document formats (incl. MessagePack/JSONL) for the internal hop — strictly dominated by zero-copy.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 1 (2026-06-05, user-ratified; from Codex design review F3 on TLDR-5qm): TRANSPORT ORDER = BINCODE-FIRST. Implementation starts with bincode + a VERSIONED ENVELOPE + version handshake from day one; JSON retained as an explicit debug fallback until the binary path proves stable; rkyv adoption deferred until a concrete daemon/CLI version-skew migration story exists (alignment/validation/schema-evolution sharp edges acknowledged). ADR-11's 'rkyv preferred' is amended to 'rkyv aspirational, bincode first'. Zero-copy remains the eventual goal per the 100x full-dump budget.

title: Lever B: per-file FileIR memoization in daemon RAM + lazy dirty-flag recompose (Path A.5)
description: DashMap<(lang,path), (content_hash, Arc<FileIR>)> in the daemon — Path B's one true kernel (parse = pure per-file function of content) without disk/rkyv/schema versioning. Watcher invalidate_by_input plumbing exists; today it nukes a whole-project entry — repoint it to evict ONE memo entry + set project dirty flag. Compose LAZY on next query after dirty (a 20-save burst = one recompose). Receiver indexes (Rust/Py/TS) FOLD INTO FileIR as parse-time artifacts so compose never touches source text (arrives at lever C's architecture with byte-identical output; oracle swap stays deferred per ADR-9). Content-hash keyed; freshness rules shared with the corpus oracle (kkt/1qv lesson — caches must not drift). ACCEPTANCE: edit loop measured save->fresh-graph; memory delta of retained FileIRs measured (expect low hundreds of MB; FileIR = compact facts, NOT ASTs/source); hash gates green; does NOT reopen ADR-8 (no storage layer — recorded as Path A addendum).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCALE NOTE (ADR-11, 100x target): Tier-1 memo projected ~1-3GB at 100x — the acceptance memory measurement gains a second purpose: it feeds tripwire T2 (unpark zde disk chunks if restart ~30s or memo GBs measure as pain). Design the memo so a disk layer can slot beneath it without interface change (content-hash keys already guarantee this).

title: Lever A: parallelize compose (rayon per-file resolution map)
description: Compose runs single-threaded today (every profile: rayon pool parked during resolution; rayon 1.10 already a dep, parse already par_iter with the flat-no-nesting house rule). Parallelize the per-file resolution loop: per-file work reads own FileIR+source + immutable Func/ClassIndex; the two &mut memo caches (ReExportTracer, ImportResolver) are caches over PURE lookups — start with per-worker caches (rayon map_init; some duplicate lookups, zero contention); concurrent-internals or pre-resolve-imports are fallback designs. Determinism: final edge sort already exists, so identical edge SET = identical output — the five frozen hashes prove it. EXPECTED: compose 0.56s->~0.1s rust, 2.85s->~0.4s django. ACCEPTANCE: all five corpus hashes byte-identical; counts identical; bench medians recorded vs TLDR-hqb; determinism x3.

title: [EPIC] Ultra-fast serving: tier model implementation (ADR-9/ADR-10)
description:
Implements the architecture decided in ADR-9 (TLDR-t0s: resident graph snapshot, lifetime ledger, Big-O contract) and ADR-10 (TLDR-14i: no silent fallback). Target end state, all linear-or-better:

  First start:   parse all (parallel ~2.6s) + compose (parallel ~0.3s) -> tiers populated
  Save a file:   re-parse 1 file (~4ms) -> dirty flag
  Next query:    dirty? lazy parallel recompose + O(V+E) snapshot rebuild (~0.1-0.4s); clean? answer in ms
  Daemon down:   loud failure (ADR-10), never silent degradation

GATES (inherited from the campaign): every output-identical change rides the frozen-corpus byte-hash gates (rust/django/zod/nest/caddy, TLDR-hqb baselines + harness scripts/bench_phase_timing.sh); the ADR-9 complexity contract is the review rubric — no per-item collection scans.

Sequencing: A (parallel compose) first — gate-able today, touches builder only. Then B (per-file memo + lazy dirty). CSR snapshot + blob retirement next. ADR-10 CLI change independent. Go fix rides on TLDR-olg.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN TARGET UPDATE (ADR-11): plan for 100x current scale (~75k files / ~2M funcs / ~2.6M edges). Derived budgets: snapshot ~200-300MB resident, rebuild 50-150ms, walks 10-50ms worst, first warm 25-35s once, per-save recompose 1-3s, Tier-1 memo 1-3GB. TRIPWIRES (measure-then-act): T1 per-save recompose pain -> incremental compose; T2 restart/memo pain -> UNPARK TLDR-zde disk chunks under Tier 1; T3 rebuild pain -> CSR surgery. New child: token-lean compact rendering (TLDR-5kw).

title: ADR-10: No silent fallback — daemon-required CLI by default; one-shot only as an explicit command
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-05, user-ratified in discussion) When no daemon is running (or it is unhealthy), graph-serving CLI commands FAIL LOUDLY — "daemon not running — start with: tldr daemon start" — instead of silently degrading to a local one-shot build. One-shot execution remains available ONLY as an explicit, typed-out choice (e.g. --oneshot flag or a dedicated subcommand; exact surface decided at implementation). Default invocations always ride the daemon + caches and are expected to answer in milliseconds.

## Rationale (user's words: "My problem is silent, not the fallback itself")
- Silent fallback MASKS daemon failures: the user experiences mysterious 3-24s answers instead of learning the daemon is down.
- Performance becomes non-deterministic: same command, 1000x latency variance, no signal why.
- Explicit is the opposite of silent: CI and scripting keep a deliberate one-shot path; what dies is the AUTOMATIC degradation.

## Lineage
Same principle as TLDR-lx7 ("No silent degradation — VectorStore-only search path, SemanticIndex fallback removed"). This extends the lx7 doctrine from the semantic flow to the analysis flow.

## Scope notes for implementation
- Error message must name the fix (daemon start command) and exit non-zero.
- CI recipe: start daemon -> warm -> run -> stop, OR explicit one-shot flag — choose per pipeline, never implicitly.
- daemon_router is the touch point; health check (socket liveness) precedes routing.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 1 (2026-06-05, user-ratified during TLDR-uiy audit 1/64): SCOPE = INDEX-BACKED COMMANDS ONLY. Loud-failure/daemon-required applies to commands whose answer comes from the snapshot/index (graph queries, per-file facts served from memo, semantic store). Pure FS-TRUTH commands — those that by definition read the live filesystem and have nothing to be stale about (tree -H raw walks, doctor tool-availability probes) — run local without requiring the daemon. Rationale: ADR-10 protects against SILENT STALENESS and masked daemon failure; an FS-truth command has no staleness to mask. Boundary rule of thumb: if the command's correct answer changes when the daemon's index is stale, it is index-backed -> daemon-required. Recorded from TLDR-uiy open question; recommendation accepted as-is.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 2 (2026-06-05, user-ratified during TLDR-uiy audit 6/64): FS-TRUTH CLASS NARROWED to raw-FS-METADATA commands only (tree -H live walks, doctor tool probes). Per-file/per-function ANALYSIS commands (reaching-defs, taint, available, slice, cfg, dfg, ...) are INDEX-BACKED: daemon-served from content-hash-keyed T1 memos (the existing daemon LRU query-cache — 10k entries/512MB, file-hash invalidation, docs/CACHE_ARCHITECTURE.md — evolves into the T1 tier rather than a new mechanism). Rationale: one ADR-10 story for all analysis; agent sequences on one function hit warm memos; staleness contract identical to T2.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 3 (2026-06-06, user-ratified during TLDR-uiy audit 17/64): GIT-TRUTH CLASS. Commands whose truth source is git history (churn, temporal, diff-family, the churn half of hotspots) run LOCAL — correct-by-construction against git state, like FS-truth. Recorded future lever (measure-first): daemon cache keyed by (HEAD sha, params) — history is immutable per commit — engaged only if a git-truth command measures hot (churn already fans out into ~5 git subprocesses per invocation). Three ADR-10 classes now: INDEX-BACKED (daemon-required, fail loud) / FS-TRUTH (raw FS metadata, local) / GIT-TRUTH (local + HEAD-keyed cache lever).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 4 (2026-06-06, user-ratified during TLDR-uiy audit 26/64): EXTERNAL-TOOL-TRUTH CLASS. Commands whose truth source is an external tool run against the live working tree (diagnostics, doctor probes, fix linters) run LOCAL always — the daemon adds only staleness risk. Future lever (measure-first): result cache keyed by (tool, tool-version, content-hash). PROVENANCE MANDATORY in all formats: which tool produced each finding + which tools ran/failed (today SARIF/GitHub drop attribution). Four-class taxonomy COMPLETE: INDEX-BACKED / FS-TRUTH / GIT-TRUTH / EXTERNAL-TOOL-TRUTH (+ hybrid composites per change-impact). In-tree precedent: the daemon Diagnostics arm already hard-rejects with 'use tldr diagnostics' (daemon.rs:1103-1110) — becomes the documented pattern for out-of-class variants.

title: ADR-9: The daemon IS the database — resident graph snapshot serves all queries; answer blobs and external DBs rejected
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-05, user-driven design session) The call-graph serving layer is rebuilt around a RESIDENT, IMMUTABLE GRAPH SNAPSHOT in the daemon, designed from data-structure-and-algorithm analysis (constraint: nothing >= O(n^2)). No external database. Whole-project answer blobs leave the hot path.

## The structure (the spec)
- SYMBOLS: intern table — every name/path -> u32 (arena + hashmap).
- NODES: Vec<FuncMeta> by dense FuncId(u32), struct-of-arrays.
- EDGES: forward CSR (offsets[V+1] + targets[E] + edge_meta[E]) AND reverse CSR (counting-sort build). Reverse adjacency is LOAD-BEARING (callers/impact/dead), not an optimization.
- INDEXES: name -> SmallVec<FuncId> (hash); file -> Range<FuncId> (nodes sorted by file = slices); per-file sorted (start,end,FuncId) for cursor queries (binary search).
- SNAPSHOT: the bundle is immutable behind ArcSwap; readers lock-free, never see half-built state.

## Complexity contract (acceptance rubric for all implementation work)
callers/callees O(deg) | impact/blast O(touched V+E) BFS | dead O(V) | hubs O(V log k) | cycles/layers O(V+E) Tarjan | by-name O(1) | structure(file) O(slice) | cursor O(log F) | FULL SNAPSHOT REBUILD O(V+E+symbol bytes) ~ single-digit ms at 26k edges. The defect class this campaign killed (per-item work scanning a collection) is made UNEXPRESSIBLE: compose and queries consume only indexes and arrays, never source text, never per-item linear scans.

## Incrementality: REBUILD, DON'T SURGERY
Per-file facts (content-hash keyed FileIR incl. receiver indexes) are the ONLY mutable state. On dirty: lazy (on next query) parallel recompose + O(V+E) snapshot rebuild + ArcSwap. Incremental graph surgery (tombstones/splicing) rejected: bookkeeping costs more than the linear rebuild. Re-visit only if a real monorepo measurement (~100x scale, ~200MB snapshot, tens-of-ms rebuild projected linear) says otherwise.

## Answer blobs die (the JSON correction)
Two sins separated: JSON-the-format (bad, known) vs CACHING MATERIALIZED ANSWERS (worse, structural). Queries are computed on demand from the snapshot in O(answer); serialization happens only (a) per-response at the socket boundary (~KB, format immaterial) and (b) at the persist/export boundary (restart snapshot, --format json dumps), where a binary codec may be measured in later. Measured warning baked in: serializing a 10-30MB whole-project JSON costs 50-150ms = comparable to the entire parallel recompose — it would be the next hot path; removing it is structural, not cosmetic.

## External databases REJECTED (numbers, not fashion)
- Redis: ~50-200us per socket round-trip vs ~10-100ns in-process; a BFS doing thousands of lookups becomes 1000x slower; solves cross-machine sharing we don't have. (Also rejected for storage in zde research.)
- SQLite/embedded: B-trees + query planning for a fixed 4-table schema that is naturally a static array.
- Parquet/DataFusion: OLAP scans; we do point lookups + pointer chasing. (Stays parked for the churn/temporal analytics niche only.)
DECISIVE: the dataset is microscopic by DB standards (few MB; fits in L2/L3 — a single Redis round-trip costs more than walking our ENTIRE graph) and the requirement is LATENCY, not capacity/durability. The daemon is the database; the CLI is its query language.

## Durability seam (ADR-8 compatibility)
This unparks the QUERY-SERVING half of TLDR-zde's graph-native design (CSR + interning) WITHOUT the disk layer. Restart = re-parse (parallel ~2.6s) + recompose (~0.3s) ~= 3s, accepted. If restart pain is ever MEASURED, the parked zde chunk store (rkyv/mmap) slots exactly under the per-file fact layer, unchanged. Nothing is paid for before a measurement asks (ADR-3).

## Component-lifetime ledger (the design's spine)
per FILE-VERSION: FileIR + receiver indexes + import resolution. per PROJECT-VERSION: module index, Func/Class indexes, compose edges, CSR snapshot. per QUERY: graph walk + small-answer serialization ONLY. per SESSION: daemon residency, semantic store. The historical sin was misfiled lifetimes (per-build and per-call-site work with per-file lifetimes).

## Deferred in the same session
Lever C (AST-extracted binding facts replacing the text oracle — output-changing, needs eval harness not hash gates): USER-DEFERRED. Note: folding receiver indexes into FileIR arrives at C's architecture incrementally with byte-identical output; the oracle swap stays deferred.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 1 (2026-06-05, user-ratified; from Codex design review F4 on TLDR-5qm): GENERATIONED SNAPSHOT PROTOCOL + BLOCK-UNTIL-FRESH. 'Lazy parallel recompose' is now specified: (a) snapshots carry a generation number; rebuild constructs the new generation off to the side; publication is a single atomic ArcSwap ONLY on success — readers never see partial state; on rebuild FAILURE the old generation stays installed and the error is reported loudly (ADR-10 spirit). (b) STALE-READS POLICY = BLOCK-UNTIL-FRESH: a query arriving while dirty waits for the recompose (~0.3s today, 1-3s projected at 100x); answers are always current. (c) SINGLE-FLIGHT: N concurrent queries hitting a dirty snapshot share ONE rebuild — no stampede. Explicit daemon states: serving-fresh / rebuilding(waiters) / rebuild-failed(serving=old, error surfaced).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: AMENDMENT 2 (2026-06-05, user-ratified during TLDR-uiy audit 2/64): LANGUAGE-UNIFIED SNAPSHOT. The resident snapshot is ONE structure across all languages: unified FuncId space, file table carries per-file language, -l flags become filters. Per-language graph partitioning (today's per-Language OnceCell caches) is a current-implementation artifact, not a requirement; the silent default-to-Python class of bug becomes unexpressible. Also folded at compose: per-file IMPORT summaries (D1) — structure/imports/importers/deps serve from one tier. Per-language AST normalization rules to be specified in TLDR-n74.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ADR-9 LIFETIME-LEDGER ADDENDUM (2026-06-06, user-ratified, A12 on TLDR-n74): embedding lifetime = per FUNCTION-BODY-VERSION (body-local key: body hash + signature hash + model ver). Cross-file structural context is NOT part of any embedded artifact's lifetime — it joins per QUERY from the pinned snapshot. This keeps the most expensive recompute (ONNX ~100ms+/fn) bound to the narrowest lifetime in the ledger.

title: ADR-8: PATH A RATIFIED — surgical fixes ARE the solution; chunk-store storage layer parked
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-05, USER-RATIFIED; supersedes ADR-6's deferral) Path A is adopted. The surgical fix campaign (F1 + Gate-1 rounds 1-5) IS the solution to the problems that motivated the chunk-store design. The Path B storage layer (TLDR-zde: rkyv chunks, CSR/fst, mmap compose) is PARKED — design preserved, not scheduled, reopened only by a new MEASURED pain.

## Rationale (all inputs measured; evidence on TLDR-myd/TLDR-olg/TLDR-7mp)
- TIME: full rust build 3.1s (was 226s); full daemon warm rebuild ~15s (was 3.5min). Chunks would save ~2.7s parse. Not load-bearing.
- MEMORY: callgraph warm transient ELIMINATED — 549MB peak (was 11.4GB, ~20x). The only GB-scale memory left is the EMBED phase (TLDR-3rh/yll), which no chunk store touches (ADR-7).
- RESTART: rebuild-on-restart ~15s/549MB; a query_cache.bin disk persist layer additionally exists. The '3.5min + 22GB restart' premise is fully dissolved.
- THE CORE INSIGHT (user-articulated): the problems were symptoms of seven small defects mistaken for inherent costs because nothing had been measured. We nearly built a storage layer to cache around a missing hash index.

## What Path A still owes (the honest residue, now the active queue)
1. TLDR-olg fix half: same defect class in python/TS/go scanners (priority: py > TS > go > java), under ADR-5 gates.
2. TLDR-3rh/yll: embed-phase memory (~11.5GB inference, 27GB marathon peak) — the real remaining memory problem.
3. Continuous measurement: benchmark baselines with variance so regressions are detectable (experiment suite, this date).

## Deprioritized by this decision
- TLDR-zde (Path B storage): PARKED. - TLDR-1in (F2-F4 structural memory): target eliminated. - TLDR-335 (malloc attribution): target eliminated. All retain their designs; any may be reopened by new measurements.

## Alternatives Considered
- Proceed with Path B anyway (sunk design cost): rejected — no measured problem remains in its scope.
- Close Path B permanently: rejected — per-file invalidation/cross-process sharing may matter at 10x scale; the design and ADR chain preserve the work.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ADR-9/ADR-10 (TLDR-t0s/TLDR-14i) extend Path A with the serving architecture decided 2026-06-05: resident CSR graph snapshot in the daemon (the query-serving half of zde's layout, RAM-only), answer blobs off the hot path, no external DB (Redis/SQLite/Parquet rejected on latency arithmetic), no-silent-fallback CLI. Implementation epic: TLDR-7pp (levers A/B + CSR snapshot + ADR-10 CLI; lever C deferred as TLDR-cbk). ADR-8's 'reopen only on measured pain' discipline carried forward: disk durability seam stays unpaid-for.

title: Measure post-F1 warm memory peak (closes TLDR-9ae's deferred verification; ADR-6 decision input)
description:
TLDR-9ae closed with 'memory-delta measurement queued' — this is that queue entry. F1 (drop to_value DOM from warm inserts, commit 100f465) is shipped but its memory effect is UNMEASURED: the callgraph-phase plateau was 11.4GB PRE-F1; post-F1 number unknown.

PROTOCOL (cheap now — warm callgraph rebuild is ~22s after the Gate-1 campaign, was 3.5min): restart daemon with current binary (store fresh on disk, embedding cache warm -> only callgraph rebuilds) -> poll status Memory line through the warm -> record peak + settled. Compare vs 11.4GB pre-F1 plateau.

NOTE: requires starting a resident daemon (user authorization point per 2026-06-05 session).

WHY IT MATTERS: one of exactly two missing inputs to the Path A/B call in ADR-6 (TLDR-7mp) — if F1 already took most of the callgraph transient, the chunk store's memory case shrinks toward zero and Path A wins. Deeper attribution (if the number is still big) = TLDR-335's full protocol.
close_reason: Measured: post-F1 warm peak 11.1GB resident vs 11.4 pre-F1 — H-mem falsified; plateau is AST retention (F2-F4 / TLDR-1in territory); full timeline + interpretation in comment

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
MEASURED (2026-06-05, daemon authorized; round-5 binary at 97b0324+, same resident-probe family as the pre-F1 baseline for apples-to-apples):

RESULT — H-mem FALSIFIED: post-F1 warm peak 11.1GB resident (status getrusage high-water AND 1s ps poll agree: max 11.66M KB) vs pre-F1 plateau 11.4GB. F1 (to_value DOM removal) moved the RESIDENT peak by ~0.3GB ≈ noise. The 'minus several GB' expectation in TLDR-9ae did not materialize at the resident level.

TIMELINE (1s poll, 1570 files, 28,223 vectors, embedding cache warm):
- 0-11s: climb to 9.6GB (parse/index build)
- 11-111s: creep 9.6 -> 11.1GB (callgraph/structure phase — now ~110s total, was ~3.5min pre-Gate-1-campaign)
- ~115s: CLIFF 11.1 -> 3.0GB (phase boundary frees ~8GB in one step — IDENTICAL shape to pre-F1)
- 115-135s: ~3GB (semantic store load)
- settled: 794MB warm-idle (pre-F1 settled was 1.4GB — improved)

INTERPRETATION:
1. The plateau is NOT the serialization DOM. It is retained parse state — tree-sitter ASTs + FileIRs + duplicated indices (causes b/c/d in the TLDR-k8s 15:15 analysis). The flat-then-cliff signature survives F1 untouched.
2. CAVEAT (TLDR-d3s): this is the RESIDENT probe. Pre-F1 vmmap showed resident 13.7GB vs phys_footprint 22.0GB (compression). F1's DOM may well have shrunk the FOOTPRINT peak — unmeasurable until d3s ships the phys_footprint probe. F1 is NOT proven useless; it is proven not-the-resident-plateau.
3. Wins confirmed: warm phase-1 3.5min -> ~110s (Gate-1 campaign); settled idle 1.4GB -> 0.8GB.

CONSEQUENCE for ADR-6 (TLDR-7mp) decision input 1: the callgraph transient (~11GB) PERSISTS post-F1 and is AST-retention-shaped. Whoever fixes it must stream/drop ASTs: surgical F2 (TLDR-1in AST streaming) or Path B's parse->chunk->drop-AST. This NARROWS Path A's lead: Path A now requires F2-F4 to deliver the memory win it was assumed to already have via F1. TLDR-335 (malloc attribution) regains priority to size components b/c/d. TLDR-d3s regains priority to see the compressed footprint.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CORRECTION (2026-06-05 afternoon — the morning conclusion was WRONG; owned per the ADR ethos): the 11.1GB peak I attributed to the callgraph phase was the SEMANTIC DELTA RE-EMBED. Evidence chain: (1) this project's vector store (stores/11277ce0a7c37db2, matches daemon socket hash) was WRITTEN at 14:13:43 — mid-warm — so the semantic step did real ONNX work (round-5 commits had made the overnight store stale); (2) the 9.6->11.1GB/~100s creep matches TLDR-3rh's ~11.5GB cold-inference figure within 0.4GB; (3) a pure callgraph CLI build measured 269MB peak resident / 215MB footprint — it CANNOT produce a 10GB plateau; (4) two controlled re-warms (daemon restarted; second with .tldr/cache moved aside; store fresh both times; daemon log confirms call_graph+structure+tree REBUILT, no '(cached)') peaked at 549MB resident / 538MB footprint, ~15s.

CORRECTED VERDICT — H-mem CONFIRMED BEYOND EXPECTATION: post-F1+Gate-1-rounds, the callgraph-phase warm transient is ELIMINATED: 11.4GB pre-fix -> 0.55GB = ~20x. The flat-then-cliff signature I matched this morning was the embed arena release, not AST retention. F1-vs-rounds attribution within the 20x is not separable without intermediate binaries (~10min LTO build each; not worth it — both are shipped together).

METHOD LESSON (new, feeds ADR-4's list): label memory peaks by PHASE EVIDENCE (store writes, phase durations, isolated single-phase runs), never by adjacency to a prior measurement's number. 11.1 ~= 11.4 was a coincidence trap.

ALSO DISCOVERED during the controlled runs: .tldr/cache/query_cache.bin — a disk persist layer for warm results already exists (whole-project granularity; observed salsa misses=3 on restart in these runs, so hydration didn't serve hits here, but the rebuild it would skip is now only ~15s/549MB anyway).

title: Hook-based pre-injection mode: push context into agent prompt before the model thinks
description:
From GrapeRoot competitive analysis (TLDR-2o4), finding #1 — the big one.

PROBLEM: tldr is entirely PULL — the agent must decide to call tldr (MCP/CLI), formulate a query, and pay a tool-call round-trip per lookup. An agent that doesn't reach for tldr gets zero value. GrapeRoot PUSHES ~4k-token structured context into the prompt in ~150ms via Claude Code hooks (UserPromptSubmit / SessionStart), zero tool calls. They ran our exact architecture (MCP graph tools) first and pivoted away after measuring per-call protocol overhead compounding across turns.

PROPOSAL: a hook-based pre-injection mode on top of the existing daemon. The daemon already has the warm Salsa caches and a fast answer path. Sketch:
- 'tldr hooks install' writes a UserPromptSubmit (and/or SessionStart) hook into Claude Code settings
- hook hits the daemon with the user prompt, daemon ranks relevant files/symbols (existing search + semantic when available), packs a compact structured summary (signatures, call edges, maybe inline hot-function bodies) under a token budget
- inject as additionalContext; degrade gracefully (empty output) if daemon down or project unindexed
- latency budget: must answer in low hundreds of ms or skip — never block the prompt

OPEN QUESTIONS: token budget (GrapeRoot uses ~4k; we should make it configurable), ranking quality from a raw user prompt (modality gap applies — our Arctic prefix work is an advantage here), how this composes with tldr-mcp (pre-injection for turn-1 context, MCP for deep on-demand queries is a plausible split).

title: (EPIC) Phase-1 ship readiness: quality gates green
description: Phase 1 (daemon presence-liveness + watcher + warm pipeline) cannot be called ship-ready while known quality gates are red. Scope: the pre-existing test failures (54 tldr-cli + 1 tldr-core), the -q output-swallowing CLI bug, and the flaky MCP microbench. Exit criteria: cargo test green across all crates (or failures explicitly waived with rationale), clippy clean, no -q data loss. Sibling ship-blockers tracked in their own epics: TLDR-bpf (ignore contract), TLDR-k8s (22GB warm memory).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ARCHITECTURE INTERSECTION (ADR-10/ADR-11, 2026-06-05): two ship-readiness-relevant behavior changes queued in epic TLDR-7pp: (1) TLDR-npl — default CLI fails loudly without a daemon (BREAKING behavior change; release notes + docs + CI recipe updates needed); (2) TLDR-5kw — -f compact becomes the recommended agent surface (goldens must be added deliberately; the TLDR-wks schema-drift family is the cautionary tale). Also note TLDR-3bk (-q swallows JSON) sits in the same output-rendering surface ADR-11 reorganizes — fix may merge into the render-at-edge work.

title: F1: remove serde_json::to_value triple-copy from warm cache inserts
description: daemon.rs:141-144 (calls) and 160-163 (structure): result → to_value DOM → insert(&val) where insert (salsa.rs:232) serializes AGAIN via to_vec. The Value DOM (5-15x JSON text size; every key a heap String) is pure waste — insert<T: Serialize> can take &result directly. One-line change per site + verify cache.get::<serde_json::Value> round-trips identically (to_vec(&result) and to_vec(&to_value(&result)) produce the same bytes for serde-compatible types). Expected: minus several GB off the plateau. Code-verified; does NOT need the Phase-A measurement to proceed.
notes: Code landed (100f465): 3 warm sites pass native result to insert; no to_value DOM. Memory verification (warm peak delta) deferred until the running cold build completes — daemon restart required.
close_reason: F1 landed in 100f465 (3 warm sites, no to_value DOM); functional verification via identical warm behavior + tests; memory-delta measurement queued now that the cold build completed

title: (EPIC) Ignore contract: .tldrignore honored end-to-end — watcher, corpus, unified matcher
description: Phase-1 ship blocker. tldr auto-creates .tldrignore (warm.rs:240) but only callgraph scan + warm walks honor it. Confirmed live 2026-06-04: (a) watcher delta-embeds tldrignored paths (TLDR-1j2 repro: 3→4 vectors on vendored/ write, gitignored control unchanged); (b) initial semantic build over-indexes them (TLDR-1qv: 2 of 3 scratch-corpus vectors came from the ignored dir). Decided semantics: REINDEX-ONLY filtering (presence/liveness unchanged — cargo build in target/ still defers idle shutdown). Exit criteria: a path matched by .tldrignore or .gitignore never reaches the southbound pipeline (enqueue/salsa-invalidate/chunk/embed/index) from EITHER the watcher delta path or the full-build corpus enumeration, verified by the /tmp/tig repro + new unit tests.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ARCHITECTURE INTERSECTION (ADR-9 lifetime ledger, 2026-06-05): Tier-1 of the serving model (per-file FileIR memo, TLDR-iqr) is keyed by (lang, path, content_hash) — its MEMBERSHIP must come from the same unified ignore oracle this epic builds (TLDR-9w8 matcher). The kkt/1qv drift lesson is now load-bearing for THREE caches: semantic corpus, query cache, and the new FileIR memo. One membership oracle, one front door — chunk/memo enumeration must not grow its own ignore logic.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: CROSS-FILED from TLDR-uiy audit 2/64 (Codex find): ignore-spec DIVERGENCE in structure — CLI local fallback passes IgnoreSpec::default() to get_code_structure while the daemon handler (ast.rs:101-104) passes None; the two paths silently return different results TODAY. One-oracle rule applies; likely the same divergence exists in other dual-path commands — check each during the TLDR-uiy audit.

title: warm-build 22GB memory blow-up: triple-copy insert + AST retention; debug plan + disk-backed chunk-invalidated salsa cache
description: Callgraph/structure warm phase transiently holds ~22GB phys_footprint (~900x of 24MB source) — verified NOT the embedding phase. Four code-verified causes: (a) triple-copy cache insert incl. removable serde_json::to_value DOM (daemon.rs:141-144,160-163 + salsa.rs:232), (b) build_indices_parallel retains source+AST+FileIR for all files (builder_v2.rs:638), (c) duplicated indices (builder_v2.rs:682-685), (d) no string interning. Design notes contain: full evidence, step-by-step MallocStackLogging/malloc_history debug protocol (run later), ranked fix plan F1-F4, and a design sketch for persisting the salsa cache to disk with PER-FILE CHUNK-BASED invalidation (replaces whole-project entries; kills restart recompute + invalidation cliff; watcher invalidate_by_input plumbing already exists).
design:
# Warm-build 22GB memory blow-up: root cause, debug plan, and persistent chunked salsa cache

## 1. WHAT IS THE ISSUE

`tldr warm` on tldr-code (~24MB source, 1570 files) drives the daemon to ~22GB
physical footprint — a ~900x input blow-up — during the CALLGRAPH/STRUCTURE phase
(first ~3.5min), NOT the semantic/embedding phase as originally assumed.

Evidence (2026-06-04, branch build, instrumented via status Memory + ps + vmmap):
- Warm-cache run: dead-flat 11.4GB RSS plateau for ~3.5min, then an 8GB cliff
  (11.3→3.4GB between consecutive 15s samples) at the phase boundary.
- Cold run: vmmap at ~3min (embedding NOT started): Physical footprint 22.0GB,
  of which MALLOC_LARGE = 21.8GB across only 317-375 regions (avg ~70MB/region =
  giant Vec/map doubling-reallocs). status Memory said 13.7GB (peak 16.0) at the
  same moment — the mach resident-size probe underreports because macOS compresses
  idle pages; phys_footprint is the honest metric.
- Flat-then-cliff = one big retained working set, dropped wholesale when the warm
  step's scope ends. Not a leak; a transient retention problem.

## 2. WHY 22GB — four compounding causes (code-verified)

(a) TRIPLE-COPY cache insert (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs:141-144,
    repeated for structure at 160-163):
      result = build_project_call_graph(...)      // copy 1: native structs, whole project
      val = serde_json::to_value(&result)         // copy 2: JSON DOM, 5-15x JSON text size
                                                  //   (every key a heap String, every node
                                                  //    a tagged enum in a Map)
      cache.insert(key, &val, vec![])             // copy 3: serde_json::to_vec(&val)
                                                  //   inside insert (salsa.rs:232)
    ALL THREE coexist at the insert call. The Value DOM is PURE WASTE:
    insert<T: Serialize> can take &result directly. Cheapest big win.

(b) Parallel parse retains everything (tldr-core callgraph/builder_v2.rs:638):
    build_indices_parallel holds source text + tree-sitter AST (10-50x source)
    + FileIR per file concurrently; then ALL FileIRs are retained in CallGraphIR
    through the entire resolution phase.

(c) Admitted index duplication (builder_v2.rs:682-685): "We need our own copies
    because the IR's indices use a different format" — FuncIndex/ClassIndex built
    twice in different shapes, both alive simultaneously.

(d) No string interning: owned String file paths / names / signatures duplicated
    across tens of thousands of nodes, edges, indices — then duplicated AGAIN in
    the JSON DOM.

Blow-up arithmetic: 24MB source → ~2.5GB ASTs+FileIRs → x2 duplicated indices →
+ JSON DOM (5-15x) stacked on the native copy → + serialized bytes → ~22GB transient.

## 3. HOW TO DEBUG — STEP-BY-STEP (run later; ~20min total)

PRECONDITION: current cold build finished; embedding cache repopulated on disk, so
the embed phase will be cache-hits (~1min) and only the callgraph phase (~3.5min,
the phase under investigation) recomputes. Salsa cache is in-RAM only → daemon
restart guarantees the callgraph phase re-runs.

Step A1 — instrumented daemon:
    TLDR=~/Workspace/03-Parcadei-Ecosystem/tldr-code/target/release/tldr
    cd ~/Workspace/03-Parcadei-Ecosystem/tldr-code
    $TLDR daemon stop -p .
    MallocStackLogging=1 $TLDR daemon start -p .
    $TLDR daemon list          # → PID
    $TLDR warm .

Step A2 — sample mid-plateau (~60-180s after warm ack, while status shows
    busy: warm-build and Memory > 8GB):
    malloc_history <PID> -allBySize | head -60     # TOP ALLOCATION SITES W/ STACKS
    vmmap --summary <PID> | grep -E 'Physical footprint|MALLOC'
    footprint <PID>                                # categorized snapshot
    Record the top-10 sites by total bytes; map each to causes (a)-(d).

Step A3 — secondary single-number harness (callgraph phase ISOLATED, no daemon):
    $TLDR daemon stop -p .
    /usr/bin/time -l $TLDR warm .       # foreground warm = callgraph only,
                                        # no semantic step; prints max RSS
    NOTE: foreground path skips the daemon's to_value/insert copies, so
    (A3 max-RSS) vs (A2 footprint) DIFFERENCE ≈ cost of cause (a) alone.

Step A4 — after fixes land, re-run A1-A3; acceptance = peak phys_footprint
    during warm. Targets: <8GB after fix (a); <4GB stretch after (a)+(b).

## 4. FIX PLAN (ranked by effort/payoff)

F1 (trivial): delete the to_value intermediate at daemon.rs:141-144 and 160-163 —
    pass &result straight to cache.insert. Expected: minus several GB.
F2: stream/drop AST+source immediately after FileIR extraction inside
    build_indices_parallel; consider bounding rayon width for peak control.
F3: unify the duplicated indices (IR-internal vs step-9 copies).
F4: intern strings (ustr/lasso) for paths+names across nodes/edges/indices.

## 5. SALSA CACHE → DISK + CHUNK-BASED INVALIDATION (user proposal, design sketch)

Current state (salsa.rs — homegrown, not the salsa crate):
- In-RAM DashMap<QueryKey, CacheEntry{serialized JSON bytes}>; only stats are
  persisted (.tldr/cache/salsa_stats.json). Daemon restart loses ALL entries →
  full 22GB recompute every restart.
- Granularity = WHOLE-PROJECT result per query type ("calls" for the entire repo
  is ONE entry). invalidate_by_input(file_hash) dependency tracking exists, but
  since values are project-sized, ONE changed file nukes the whole entry and the
  next query pays full recompute (22GB again). This granularity mismatch is the
  structural root of both the memory spike and the recompute cliff.

Proposal — per-file chunked, disk-backed cache:
- Persist per-FILE chunks (FileIR or equivalent) keyed by (relative path,
  content hash), e.g. .tldr/cache/fileir/<path-hash>.bin — compact binary
  (bincode/rkyv; rkyv enables mmap zero-copy loads), NOT JSON.
- Project-level results (call graph, structure) become a COMPOSE step over
  chunks: load all fresh chunks + run cross-file resolution. The expensive
  per-file parse/extract is memoized; only the resolution pass recomputes.
- Invalidation becomes chunk-based: file change → recompute ONLY that file's
  chunk (mirrors the t8f semantic per-file delta), then re-run compose.
- Daemon restart: load chunks from disk (seconds, mmap) instead of re-parsing
  the world (3.5min + 22GB).

Benefits: kills restart recompute; kills whole-project invalidation cliff;
bounds peak memory (compose can stream chunks instead of retaining all ASTs).

Risks / open questions:
- Cross-file edge resolution genuinely needs a global pass — measure compose
  cost alone (expected: small fraction of parse cost, but verify).
- Format versioning: schema_version field in chunk header; mismatch → recompute.
- Atomic write discipline: reuse the vector_store generation pattern
  (write-new-gen + commit marker) to avoid torn caches.
- Disk size: FileIR binary for 24MB source — estimate during implementation;
  likely tens-to-hundreds of MB, fine for a cache dir.
- Consistency with the semantic store freshness digest (TLDR-kkt) and with
  .tldrignore membership rules (TLDR-1qv) — chunk enumeration must use the same
  corpus walker or the two caches drift.
- The watcher's process_dirty_file already calls invalidate_by_input per file —
  the IPC/watcher plumbing for chunk-level invalidation ALREADY EXISTS; only the
  storage granularity and compose step are new.

## 6. RELATED
- TLDR-yll: RSS observation tooling (status Memory line) + max-RSS policy; the
  probe should report phys_footprint, not just resident (underreport observed).
- TLDR-1j2/TLDR-1qv: .tldrignore gaps; chunk enumeration must share corpus rules.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Child TLDR-zde design state: in-house review APPROVE+amendments (Codex parked on provider outage; brief at /tmp/tldr-analysis-store-review-brief.md). Two-flow constraint locked; CSR/rkyv/fst layout locked; format choice (rkyv vs epserde for composed artifact) and Gate-1 compose bench remain the open gates. Research round 3 (counter-evidence pass) in progress before locking alternatives.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
EPIC STATE UPDATE (2026-06-05, post Gate-1 campaign — read with the ADR chain TLDR-cv9..ab6):

The epic's original framing ('22GB warm blow-up; fix = triple-copy removal + chunk-store') is now PARTLY SUPERSEDED by measurement:

1. TIME axis RESOLVED: the 3.5min warm rebuild was ~98% compose-phase defects, not parse cost. Fixed (5 rounds, output-identical): build 226s -> 3.1s, compose 223s -> 0.56s. A warm callgraph rebuild now costs ~22s end-to-end in the daemon path. Gate-1 PASSED — recompose-always stands; the chunk store is NO LONGER a time play (saves only ~2.7s parse).

2. MEMORY axis RE-ATTRIBUTED (ADR-7): the headline peak (27.0GB high-water, 4.5h cold build 2026-06-05) is the EMBED phase — owned by TLDR-3rh/yll, NOT this epic's callgraph work and NOT addressable by the chunk store. This epic's remaining memory scope = the callgraph-phase transient: 11.4GB measured PRE-F1; F1 (TLDR-9ae) shipped; post-F1 number = TLDR-myd (new child, P1). If TLDR-myd shows F1 took most of it, remaining children re-rank: TLDR-1in (F2-F4 structural) becomes the main lever, TLDR-335 (malloc attribution) the diagnostic if numbers stay high, TLDR-zde Path B becomes optional.

3. HYPOTHESES now open under this epic:
- H-mem: post-F1 callgraph plateau is well under 11.4GB (the to_value DOM was 5-15x JSON text). Test: TLDR-myd (~22s run).
- H-lang: python/TS compose dominated by the same per-call-site-scan defect class (TLDR-olg, related). If true, the fix pattern is established and cheap.
- H-27GB: 27.0 vs historical 22.7 is measurement improvement (getrusage high-water vs 15s polling), not regression — overnight diff touched zero semantic/ code. Falsifiable only by an instrumented main-branch cold build (~4.5h, night-run candidate, NOT scheduled).

Child status: TLDR-9ae CLOSED (F1 shipped; verification -> TLDR-myd). TLDR-zde design field rewritten to post-Gate-1 state. TLDR-335/1in/3rh/d3s unchanged.

Also confirmed: warm phase-1 3.5min -> ~110s; settled 1.4GB -> 0.8GB.

Morning's 11.1GB was the embed delta (ONNX), mis-attributed. CONSEQUENCES FOR THIS EPIC: (1) TLDR-1in (F2-F4) and TLDR-335 (malloc attribution) DEPRIORITIZE — their GB-scale target no longer exists; close-or-defer candidates pending user confirmation. (2) The epic's remaining live memory scope is EMBED-phase only: TLDR-3rh (arena/batch bounding, ~11.5GB delta/cold inference confirmed again today at 11.1GB) and TLDR-yll (pressure policy; 27GB marathon peak). (3) TLDR-d3s still useful (footprint probe — today's manual footprint polling worked but should be in-status). (4) TLDR-zde Path B parked per ADR-6 recommendation.
