Memory note for this epic: retained FileIRs are compact facts (NOT ASTs/source — the 11GB class stays dead); TLDR-iqr acceptance includes measuring the retained-memo delta.

title: watcher: honor .tldrignore in watch_decision (reindex-only) + close gitignored-delete passthrough gap
description: Watcher never consults .tldrignore: edits in tldrignored dirs trigger full southbound reindex (salsa invalidate + re-embed + index insert). Decided scope: filter in watch_decision BEFORE the exists()/is_corpus_file branch using scanner.rs Gitignore-based matcher loaded once in spawn_watcher; presence_decision UNCHANGED (reindex-only per user decision 2026-06-04). Also closes the deleted-gitignored-file passthrough. Full design + tests + verification plan in design notes.
design:
# Watcher: honor .tldrignore (+ close .gitignore delete-path gap) in watch_decision — REINDEX-ONLY

## Problem (verified 2026-06-04, explanation session)

The in-daemon watcher (crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/watcher.rs) never consults
`.tldrignore`. `watch_decision` (watcher.rs:74) filters via: Access drop → `.tldr/` +
store-dir prefix → `is_corpus_file` (tldr-core chunker.rs:312). `is_corpus_file` honors
`.gitignore` / DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS / generated sentinels but has ZERO `.tldrignore`
references (confirmed: no matches in chunker.rs or walker.rs).

Consequence: an edit to a source file in a tldrignored-but-not-gitignored dir flows
OS event → enqueue → process_dirty_file (daemon.rs:1359) → salsa invalidation →
re-chunk + re-embed + index insert. Southbound work on a path the user excluded.

Secondary gap in the SAME decision fn: a DELETED gitignored file falls through the
vanished-path passthrough branch (is_corpus_file can't judge gone paths) and gets
enqueued; apply_delta's store-side filter drops it, but only after a wasted southbound hop.

Irony: the CLI-foreground warm AUTO-CREATES `.tldrignore` (warm.rs:240-251) — the
product promises the contract, the watcher breaks it.

## Decided scope (user decision 2026-06-04)

- REINDEX-ONLY: ignored paths must never reach the southbound pipeline (no enqueue,
  no salsa invalidation, no chunk/embed/index work).
- presence_decision is UNCHANGED: ignored-dir writes still count as presence
  (preserves the deliberate TLDR-3w5 choice that `cargo build` writing to gitignored
  `target/` keeps the daemon alive).
- OS events cannot be suppressed (FSEvents has no subtree exclusion); the guarantee
  is filtered-before-channel.

## Design

1. Reuse the Gitignore-based matcher from tldr_core::callgraph::scanner::load_tldrignore
   (scanner.rs:529 — proper glob semantics via matched_path_or_any_parents). Do NOT use
   warm.rs's name-stem HashSet load_tldrignore (warm.rs:323) — crude component matching.
   load_tldrignore is currently private; export it (or add a small pub helper
   `is_tldrignored(root, path, is_dir) -> bool`). filter_tldrignored (scanner.rs:554)
   is already pub but batch-shaped, wrong shape for per-event checks.
2. In spawn_watcher (watcher.rs:125): load the `.tldrignore` matcher ONCE, plus a
   root-`.gitignore` matcher (same GitignoreBuilder pattern) to close the deleted-
   gitignored-file gap. Move/clone both into the debounce-handler closure.
3. In watch_decision: check `matched_path_or_any_parents(rel_path, is_dir)` for BOTH
   matchers BEFORE the `path.exists()` / `is_corpus_file` branch. Order matters:
   the check must run before the exists() branch so DELETES inside ignored dirs are
   also dropped — same delete-trap shape as the existing `.tldr/` prefix check
   (is_corpus_file always returns false for vanished paths). For vanished paths use
   is_dir=false; parent-dir patterns like `vendored/` still match via _or_any_parents.
4. presence_decision: NO change. The presence tap (watcher.rs:193) stays pre-filter.
5. Tests (watcher.rs tests module already has the pattern):
   - tldrignored existing file modify → !watch_decision, presence_decision still true
   - tldrignored DELETED file → !watch_decision (delete-trap variant)
   - gitignored DELETED file → !watch_decision (the secondary gap)
   - .tldrignore absent → behavior identical to today
   - glob pattern (`*.gen.py`) and nested dir pattern (`vendor/sub/`) respected

## Documented limitations (v1, do not solve)

- Matchers load once at daemon start; editing .gitignore/.tldrignore mid-session
  requires daemon restart. (Possible follow-up: watcher sees events on those two
  files → rebuild matchers.)
- Presence-side and root-matcher only: nested .gitignore files and git global
  excludes are NOT covered by the new explicit matchers (is_corpus_file still
  covers them for existing files; the new matcher only needs to catch deletes).

## Related but SEPARATE defect (filed independently)

enumerate_corpus_files (chunker.rs:417) — the single source of truth for what gets
embedded — also ignores .tldrignore, so the initial warm build over-indexes
tldrignored dirs (wasted embed minutes + RSS + polluted search results). Fixing the
watcher alone leaves the full-build path inconsistent with the delta path.

## Verification plan (repro already scripted in session)

Scratch project /tmp/tig with `vendored/` in .tldrignore; warm; create new .py under
vendored/ → BEFORE fix: status vector count increases (bug). AFTER fix: count stable,
Watcher presence age still resets (reindex-only semantics confirmed).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: CONFIRMED LIVE 2026-06-04 (branch build, new-code daemon, scratch /tmp/tig with 'vendored/' in .tldrignore): baseline Index warm (3 vectors) → created vendored/v2.py → 3s later Index warm (4 vectors), Watcher presence 2s ago. Watcher delta-embedded + index-inserted a tldrignored path = southbound call proven. BONUS: the baseline of 3 vectors itself confirms TLDR-1qv — corpus was src/main.py + vendored/v.py + vendored/v_new.py, i.e. 2 of 3 baseline vectors came from the tldrignored dir (initial warm over-indexed it). Negative control (.gitignore honored) pending re-run due to shell quoting error.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Negative control PASSED 2026-06-04: created gitignored/g2.py in /tmp/tig → index stayed at 4 vectors (vs vendored/v2.py in tldrignored dir which DID increment 3→4). Asymmetry fully proven: .gitignore honored by watch_decision, .tldrignore not consulted. Repro complete; issue ready for implementation.

title: (EPIC) Presence-based daemon liveness — daemon dies only when the project is dormant
description: GOVERNING PHILOSOPHY (user, 2026-06-04; full text on TLDR-3w5): the idle question changes from 'is anyone talking to my socket?' to 'is anyone alive in this project?'. Liveness = ANY tldr CLI invocation (all ~64 surfaces) + raw watcher events (pre-corpus-filter; a cargo build is proof of life) + the daemon's own in-flight work. Idle shutdown only when no human, no agent, no file event, no internal work for idle_timeout. Accepted trade-off: long-build machines effectively never idle out (warm availability over memory thrift). Codex-reviewed 2026-06-04 (adversarial, file:line grounded): ordering A->C->B->D with MCP parity + memory escape hatch parallel after A. A=TLDR-3w5 (ActivityTracker), C=status observability, B=CLI-wide poke, D=async warm ack (TLDR-utj.7).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: EPIC PROGRESS (2026-06-04 session, branch tldr-cli-daemon-support): 6 of 7 children CLOSED — A/TLDR-3w5 (ActivityTracker: per-source timestamps + busy tokens w/ age, watcher pre-filter tap, closure-owned guards), C/TLDR-qzc (status: per-source presence ages, busy+age, idle deadline, semantic index state via non-blocking probe), B/TLDR-nke (CLI-wide registry-gated datagram poke, zero latency, TLDR_NO_POKE opt-out), D/TLDR-utj.7 (async warm: instant started/already_building ack, detached WarmJob, single-flight latch), TLDR-axz (MCP parity: sender moved to tldr_core::liveness, poke per tools/call; KNOWN LIMITATION: gates on server-process cwd, not per-tool path), TLDR-d26 (idle_timeout keep-key-redefine docs + startup policy log + purged fictional --idle-timeout flag from docs). REMAINING: TLDR-yll only — part 2 (RSS in status) shipped; parts 1 (characterize 22.7GB build RSS, instrumented full build needed) + 3 (opt-in max-RSS policy) open. Every child verified LIVE, not just unit-tested. Quality gates at close: tldr-cli full suite = exactly the 54 known pre-existing failures (TLDR-6q2/wks), tldr-core = 1 known pre-existing (inheritance_cpp stale assertion, 6q2 item 2), tldr-mcp 59/59 green, clippy clean.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ARCHITECTURE INTERSECTION (ADR-10, 2026-06-05): no-silent-fallback (TLDR-14i/TLDR-npl) RAISES THE STAKES for presence-based liveness — default CLI now FAILS LOUDLY when the daemon is absent, so daemon availability is the product's availability. Implications: (1) liveness/idle-stop policy errors become user-visible errors, not silent slow paths; (2) the status/health surface (already shipped: Memory line, liveness ages) is the diagnostic users see first; (3) memory-pressure policy (TLDR-yll) should prefer DEGRADE-AND-REPORT over die: a dead daemon now means refused service, not slow service.

title: daemon idle-timeout kills in-progress builds/deltas — fix: presence-based ActivityTracker (cxa child A)
description: REPRO (live, 2026-06-04): user ran 'tldr daemon start' + 'tldr warm' on tldr-code (1565 files, ~90min ArcticL build), left it alone; 30min later daemon self-terminated mid-build ('.tldr/daemon.log: No client activity for 1800s, shutting down'); next 'tldr semantic' said 'daemon not started'. ROOT CAUSE: crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs:304-314 — idle check uses last_activity, ONLY updated on accepted client connections (line 323). Invisible to the idle loop: (1) the background warm/index build, (2) the in-process watcher's delta work. Any build longer than idle_timeout_secs is structurally unfinishable unattended. ADOPTED DESIGN (Codex-reviewed 2026-06-04; this issue = child A of epic TLDR-cxa, the presence-based liveness philosophy): ActivityTracker with PER-SOURCE timestamps (socket / cli-poke / watcher / internal) + busy tokens/refcount WITH AGE (RAII covers panic-unwind; hung spawn_blocking at daemon.rs:490/1183 must be visible as stale-busy, exposed via TLDR-qzc). Watcher tap: post-debounce/pre-filter (before watch_decision, watcher.rs:163) as AtomicU64 relaxed store, RE-EXCLUDING daemon self-writes (.tldr/store paths, watcher.rs:83) — counting own store writes = self-perpetuating immortality. Idle predicate: any(per-source presence fresh) || busy_refcount>0. Superseded options (a)/(b)/(c) and full history in comments. WORKAROUND until fixed: while true; do tldr daemon status >/dev/null 2>&1; sleep 600; done. Downstream: TLDR-qzc (status observability) -> TLDR-nke (CLI poke) -> TLDR-utj.7 (async warm ack); parallel: TLDR-axz (MCP parity), TLDR-yll (memory), TLDR-d26 (config migration).
close_reason: Fixed: presence-based ActivityTracker landed (commit on tldr-cli-daemon-support). Per-source timestamps + busy tokens with age; closure-owned guards on warm/delta spawn_blocking; watcher pre-filter tap excluding self-writes and Access events. 316 daemon tests green; 54 suite failures verified pre-existing (TLDR-6q2/TLDR-wks).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE ADDITION (verified in code 2026-06-04): last_activity is written in exactly ONE place — daemon.rs:323 accept loop, on socket accept. Full activity audit: RESETS timer = daemon status/query/notify/stop, warm, semantic, plus the opportunistic daemon-routed analysis commands (tree/structure/calls/extract/imports/importers/dead/complexity/slice/impact/context/smells) — even their is_daemon_running probe (check_socket_alive, daemon_router.rs:244) connects and resets. DOES NOT reset = (1) the index build (the filed bug), and (2) the IN-PROCESS WATCHER + per-file deltas (ac0.2) — file edits flow over an internal channel, never the socket, so a daemon actively applying deltas all afternoon still counts as idle and self-terminates mid-delta-stream. The fix must define activity as: socket accept OR build-in-progress OR watcher-delivered work — not just builds. Fix option (a) should consult both the build flag and a watcher last-delivery timestamp (or option (b)'s RAII guard held by both the build task and the delta worker).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: GOVERNING PHILOSOPHY for the fix (user, 2026-06-04): the idle question must change from 'is anyone talking to my socket?' to 'is anyone alive in this project?'. Daemon dies ONLY when the project is dormant. Liveness signals, ALL of which must reset/defer the idle timer: (1) ANY tldr CLI invocation — all ~64 surfaces, not just the ~18 daemon-routed ones; requires a cheap non-blocking fire-and-forget poke from non-routed commands when a daemon is registered for the project (must never slow the CLI down). (2) RAW watcher events, counted BEFORE the corpus filter — a long cargo build writing to target/ is filtered from indexing (correct) but is still proof of life (someone started that build); liveness taps the pre-filter event stream. (3) The daemon's own in-flight work — index build, delta processing ('never abandon your own job'). Idle shutdown fires only when no human, no agent, no file event, no internal work for idle_timeout. Accepted trade-off (explicit): on machines with long-running builds the daemon effectively never idles out — warm availability is chosen over memory thrift.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: CODEX REVIEW ADJUSTMENTS (2026-06-04, adopted): (1) TRACKER SHAPE: per-source timestamps + busy tokens/refcount — NOT a single timestamp; daemon status (TLDR-qzc) must answer 'what kept me alive' and expose busy AGE, since hung spawn_blocking work (daemon.rs:490 warm, daemon.rs:1183 deltas) can hold busy forever; RAII covers panic-unwind but not hangs. (2) WATCHER TAP: post-debounce/pre-filter (before watch_decision at watcher.rs:163, where the handler iterates at watcher.rs:161) as AtomicU64 relaxed store — but MUST re-exclude daemon self-writes (.tldr/store paths, currently excluded at watcher.rs:83): counting our own store writes as presence = self-perpetuating immortality. (3) Idle predicate: any(per-source last_presence fresh) || busy_refcount>0. This issue is child A of epic TLDR-cxa; ordering A->C(qzc)->B(nke)->D(utj.7).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: IMPLEMENTED (2026-06-04, branch tldr-cli-daemon-support). New module crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/activity.rs: ActivityTracker with per-source AtomicU64 presence timestamps (Socket/CliPoke/Watcher/Internal; CliPoke reserved for nke) + busy tokens with label+age (Mutex<HashMap> for snapshot, lock-free AtomicUsize count for the 100ms idle loop). Idle predicate: busy_count==0 && all presences stale. BusyGuard drop touches Internal so the idle countdown restarts at build COMPLETION (else a 90-min build is killed the moment it finishes). WIRING: daemon.rs accept loop touches Socket (replaces last_activity entirely); warm build + delta spawn_blocking each take a busy guard owned INSIDE the closure (advisor catch: a guard held in the awaiting async task dies if the 30s-timeout client cancels the connection task, reintroducing the original bug — closure ownership makes guard lifetime == blocking work lifetime, and a hung warm shows as stale-busy age for qzc). watcher.rs: new pure presence_decision() (self-write prefixes excluded; Access events deliberately excluded — daemon's own corpus reads during build would otherwise be self-perpetuating immortality, documented inline) tapped post-debounce/pre-corpus-filter as relaxed atomic touch from notify's sync thread. TESTS: 8 activity unit tests (incl. the original bug encoded: busy defeats idle with all sources stale; panic-unwind release; drop-restarts-countdown), 3 presence_decision tests (target/ write counts, self-writes don't, Access doesn't), e2e watcher smoke extended to assert the tap fired, old test asserting the buggy behavior replaced. 316 daemon-suite tests green.

title: Deploy Phase 1 (TLDR-7xz) binary — installed ~/.local/bin/tldr is pre-Phase-1 and still silently cold-serves
description: Found by scripts/smoke-test-cli.sh. ~/.local/bin/tldr (md5 2455d061, built Jun 3 18:03) is identical to target/release/tldr and PREDATES commit 3788a00 (Jun 4 01:18, Phase 1 one-warm-path). Live behavior contradicts the TLDR-7xz close note ('serve matrix verified live'): 'tldr similar <file>' runs a cold inline build instead of the parked 'not available in this version' message, and 'tldr semantic' serves cold without a daemon instead of 'daemon not started — run tldr daemon start'. Fix: cargo build --release && reinstall to ~/.local/bin, then re-run scripts/smoke-test-cli.sh and confirm similar -> HONEST(parked) and semantic -> HONEST(daemon not started) when no daemon is up.
close_reason: Rebuilt (cargo build --release, 3m14s, commit c64183b tree = 3788a00 code) and reinstalled to ~/.local/bin (md5 2455d061 -> 6adaafff, Jun 4 09:13). Smoke suite re-run: PASS=61 HONEST=3 SKIP=5 FAIL=0. Phase 1 serve matrix now live and verified by scripts/smoke-test-cli.sh: semantic -> exit 1 'daemon not started — run tldr daemon start'; similar -> exit 1 'not available in this version, seeded similarity needs a warm daemon API'; semantic --hybrid -> 'not available in this version, hybrid (BM25 + dense) search is moving into the warm daemon engine'. Stale-deploy diagnosis confirmed — no Phase 1 regression.

title: MCP daemon client — un-park agent semantic search
description: Shared daemon IPC client boundary usable from tldr-mcp (today the IPC client lives in tldr-cli only; tldr-mcp deps = tldr-core). Route MCP tldr_semantic through the warm daemon; structured not_ready/indexing isError with retry guidance (never block an agent tool call on a build). Highest agent-facing value in Phase 2.

title: BM25 resident in daemon + RRF fusion — un-park hybrid
description: Bring the BM25/lexical inverted index into the warm resident layer alongside the VectorStore; fuse with RRF (rank-based, no score normalization); watcher updates it incrementally like the dense store. Un-parks semantic --hybrid (+ --langs if folded here) at FULL warm quality. Kills the old silent bm25_only degraded mode for good.

title: [EPIC] Reranker: two-stage retrieval precision — cross-encoder vs ColBERT decision, resident judge, eval-gated wiring
description:
[Promoted feature->EPIC 2026-06-06, user-directed: 'it needs its own respect.']

WHY AN EPIC: the reranker is the precision stage tldr completely lacks — retrieval is currently single-stage (dense or BM25 rank = final rank). Two-stage retrieve-wide-then-rerank is the standard fix for the last-mile precision problem and is ORTHOGONAL to the cache rewrite (TLDR-17d): it improves WHAT search returns; the cache work improves HOW FAST. It composes with A12 query-time fusion (TLDR-n74): fusion produces the wide candidate set (50-200), the reranker judges it to top 5-10.

## Architecture position
search query -> dense (body-local vectors) + BM25 + structural signals -> RRF fusion (utj.3) -> WIDE candidate set -> RERANKER (this epic) -> top-k answer cards. Reranker model RESIDENT in the daemon (warm, like the embedder) — never per-call cold-load. Latency budget: the reranker must keep total search p50 under the agent-acceptable envelope; budget set by measurement, not assertion.

## Children
1. DECISION: cross-encoder vs ColBERT late-interaction — eval-harness shootout (recall@5/MRR on the n=14 harness, expanded if needed) + latency/storage trade: cross-encoder ~50-200ms/100 candidates, highest accuracy, zero index cost; ColBERT faster per query, per-token vector storage, can double as retriever. Decision recorded as ADR-grade comment.
2. RESIDENT RERANKER: daemon residency, model lifecycle (load/version key), async-safe integration with the pinned-generation join (A8 contract: dead candidates dropped BEFORE reranking — never waste reranker budget on deleted symbols).
3. EVAL + WIRING: before/after recall@5/MRR recorded; wire into the search pipeline behind a flag until eval proves the win; flag-parity and provenance contracts apply (result cards say 'reranked-by: <model>').

## Relationships
- Sibling: TLDR-utj.3 (BM25+RRF fusion) — fusion feeds the reranker its candidates; fusion first or parallel.
- Independent of: TLDR-rfz / TLDR-17d (cache rewrite) — no shared surface; can proceed during research phase per user's fast-lane strategy.
- Serves: the search-QUALITY problem class the cache architecture explicitly does not touch (memory 00: fusion + reranking + LLM descriptions are the remaining quality levers).

title: Code-specialized embedding model swap + eval comparison
description: Evaluate ArcticL (baseline R@5 0.786, n=14 harness) vs code-specialized decoder models: voyage-code-3 (API, Matryoshka), jina-code-embeddings 0.5b/1.5b (open, Qwen2.5-Coder backbone, last-token pooling), Nomic CodeRankEmbed (open), CodeSage-v2 (open, Matryoshka). Self-hostable constraint matters (current stack = fastembed/ONNX local). Decide + swap + full rebuild story. Biggest single quality lever.

title: Park MCP tldr_semantic with structured isError message
description: mcp/tools/search.rs:168-176 cold-builds SemanticIndex + hybrid fusion on EVERY agent call — the exact silent-degraded-24/7 mode the philosophy forbids, on the agent-facing surface. tldr-mcp has NO daemon client (Cargo.toml deps: tldr-core only) — building one is Phase 2. Park: structured isError (protocol.rs:246-269 pattern; error results are not cached so retry works): 'not available in this version, agent semantic search is moving to the warm daemon engine'.
close_reason: MCP tldr_semantic parked: handle_semantic returns structured isError starting 'not available in this version,' and points agents at tldr_search/tldr_bm25 (both remain live); tool stays listed with [PARKED] description. Old behavior cold-built a SemanticIndex on EVERY agent call. Unit test asserts message format.

title: Daemon cold-query honesty: no inline full build under the query lock
description: index_manager.rs:104-121 builds the store inline under the write lock on a cold query — a no-persisted-store first query blocks for the full embed build. Change: persisted store exists -> load inline (seconds, that IS works-beautifully); no persisted store -> return 'index not built — run tldr warm' instead of building inline. Build stays owned by warm/embed.
close_reason: Daemon cold-query honesty shipped: IndexManager::query returns typed QueryError (NotReady/Building/Internal); inline cold-build under the query write-lock REMOVED; readiness pre-check fires before embed_query (no ONNX on cold); try_read_for(250ms) maps an in-progress warm build to honest Building instead of blocking. Daemon maps NotReady/Building to machine-distinguishable status:'not_ready'. Unit tests: cold_query_returns_not_ready_without_building_anything, model_mismatch_returns_not_ready; daemon test: test_semantic_cold_query_returns_not_ready.

title: Require-warm routing for tldr semantic: kill silent cold fallthrough
description: semantic.rs:83-120 routes to daemon opportunistically and silently falls through to cold search_with_store (ONNX reload per query) on any miss. Replace: daemon answers -> serve (works beautifully). Daemon absent -> 'daemon not started — run tldr daemon start'. No persisted store -> 'index not built — run tldr warm'. NEVER a silent cold synchronous serve from the CLI. search_with_store remains for builders (embed/warm) and daemon-internal load_or_build only.
close_reason: Require-warm routing shipped: semantic.rs rewritten — route_semantic (new 4-state semantic-only router in daemon_router.rs: Hit/DaemonDown/NotReady/Error) is the ONLY serve path; silent cold search_with_store fallthrough removed. DaemonDown -> 'daemon not started — run tldr daemon start'; NotReady relays daemon guidance. Verified end-to-end against the real binary.

title: (EPIC) Phase 1 ship: one warm path — works beautifully or says why
description: GOVERNING PHILOSOPHY (locked 2026-06-03): the tool has exactly TWO modes — works beautifully (warm, full quality) or honestly says it can't and stops. No transient implementations, no degraded/dual paths, no silent slow serves. Fear being designed against: agents silently running at reduced quality 24/7. SCOPE: touchups + routing fixes only — NO new capability (no new model, no reranker, no new daemon APIs, no MCP daemon client; those are Phase 2). STANDARDIZED PARKED-FEATURE MESSAGE: 'not available in this version, <reason in 1 line>'. RUNTIME NOT-READY MESSAGES: 'daemon not started — run tldr daemon start' / 'index not built — run tldr warm'. Codex-reviewed (sound-with-changes; all adjustments folded in): keep parked surfaces (flags/tools) and fail fast — never silently remove; nuke SemanticIndex TYPE last preserving shared BuildOptions/helpers in index.rs; similar is NOT pure reuse (seeded similarity needs a new daemon API -> parked, Phase 2); MCP has no daemon client (-> parked, Phase 2); current paths silently fall back to cold (THE core fix). AUDIT MAP: SemanticIndex consumers = CLI semantic --hybrid (semantic.rs~141), CLI similar (similar.rs~119, Function granularity = same as main store), MCP tldr_semantic (mcp/tools/search.rs~168, cold EVERY agent call — worst offender). tldr search = lexical, no embeddings, untouched. embed/warm = builders, untouched.
close_reason: Phase 1 shipped: one warm path — works beautifully or says why. All 8 children closed. Serve matrix verified live: no daemon -> 'daemon not started — run tldr daemon start'; cold -> 'index not built — run tldr warm'; building -> 'index build in progress — retry when warm completes'; warm -> full-quality results (3ms); parked surfaces -> 'not available in this version, <reason>'. Phase 2 = TLDR-utj.

title: Daemon single-instance hardening: prevent 2 indexing daemons on the same folder
description:
Prevent >1 daemon indexing the SAME project folder (cross-project parallelism is intended & fine). Root cause of the load~147 cold-build storm: a busy (cold-building) daemon was misjudged dead, triggering restarts that slipped past the single-instance guard.

HOLE A (DONE 2026-06-03): start.rs resolved --project with a NON-canonical fallback when canonicalize() failed (current_dir().join). Under load that yields a DIFFERENT socket/PID-lock key for the SAME folder -> two daemons. FIX: resolve_project_root() now FAILS CLOSED ('cannot resolve project root') instead of falling back. Tests: existing-dir canonicalizes, missing-path errors, symlinked path resolves to same canonical root (same-folder => same-key). 6/6 lib start tests pass.

HOLE B (TODO): liveness must be judged by the OS, not the process. check_socket_alive is a bare socket connect; a daemon pegged at 100% CPU mid-~90min build can't answer -> misjudged dead -> start.rs reaps its socket -> restart races in. FIX: judge 'folder already owned' by PID flock held / kill(pid,0) on the registered PID (auto-released by kernel on death; no PID-reuse ambiguity with flock), NOT by a socket reply. Never reap a live owner's socket. Optionally expose a 'warming/building' state in the registry for UX (not for the liveness decision). Net guarantee: tldr daemon start on a folder whose daemon PID is alive can never spawn a second, even under restart spam.

Cross-ref: tldr-cli-demon supervisor re-issues 'daemon start' every tick + launchd KeepAlive — harmless once the start path is bulletproof. First-run thundering-herd across many projects (all launchd jobs cold-building at once) is a separate concern (stagger / global build semaphore).
close_reason: Both holes (canonicalize fail-closed + PID-liveness/locked-reap) implemented, Codex-reviewed, tested; same-folder duplicate-daemon prevention done. Deploy + optional flock-probe/cross-project-stagger noted as separate follow-ups.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: BOTH HOLES IMPLEMENTED + TESTED (2026-06-03), Codex-reviewed (verdict: sound-with-changes; all 3 required changes applied). Hole A: ac4cbb2 (fail-closed canonicalize). Hole B: c9a7d32 (find_entry_unpruned + is_pid_alive PID-liveness bail in run_async; unpruned lookup preserves cleanup state; stale-socket reap relocated under the flock in run_foreground). Tests: 7/7 lib start (incl. run_async_bails_on_live_owner_even_without_a_live_socket — live PID + no socket => bails, the precise Hole B assertion); val006 3/3 (incl. no-clobber-live-socket, guards the reap relocation), val003 3/3, daemon_test 36/36; clippy clean. The flock remains the authoritative backstop. NOT yet deployed — ships in the next 'tldr' binary install. OPTIONAL FOLLOW-UPS (not blocking; not same-folder duplication): (1) a no-write flock PROBE in the start path would be even more authoritative than the registry PID (Codex note); (2) cross-project first-run thundering-herd (all launchd jobs cold-building at once) — a global build semaphore / stagger; this is a DIFFERENT concern from same-folder duplication and would be its own issue if wanted.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DEPLOYED + LIVE-VERIFIED (2026-06-03). Rebuilt tldr release (--features semantic) with Hole A (ac4cbb2) + Hole B (c9a7d32); freshness-gated (binary mtime > start.rs mtime, guarding against a dropped-build stale binary) and atomic-installed to ~/.local/bin/tldr (atomic rename = safe vs the transient hook-spawned tldr procs; no long-lived daemon was running). Live check: '~/.local/bin/tldr daemon start --project /no/such/dir' now returns the Hole A fail-closed error ('cannot resolve project root ... refusing to start so a second daemon can't index the same folder'), confirming the hardened code is the running binary. Net: same-folder duplicate-daemon prevention is now LIVE — the runaway-multiplication root cause of the load~147 storm is structurally prevented even if daemons (re)start.

title: Watcher transition guard + rollout: no dual ownership with C++ fsnotifier; resolve config-gate default
description: Cutover step (parent 4vb, depends on ac0.2). Ensure the in-daemon Rust notify watcher and the external C++ fsnotifier never both watch the tree (bfc 'no dual watcher ownership'). Work: (1) resolve the config-gate default decision (default-OFF/opt-in vs ON vs env TLDR_IN_DAEMON_WATCH); (2) coordinate disabling the C++ fsnotifier when the Rust watcher is active (cross-repo with tldr-cli-demon); (3) flip the default-ON once the C++ side stands down. Cross-repo — needs sequencing with ejm.
close_reason: NOT NEEDED (2026-06-03 user decision): C++ fsnotifier removed outright, not coexisted. ac0.2 ships DEFAULT-ON; no dual-ownership window to guard, no staged config-gate rollout. Cutover collapses to 2 moves: (1) ac0.2 default-ON, (2) delete C++ fsnotifier + supervisor->babysitter.

title: Filesystem-watch cutover: in-daemon notify watcher + C++ supervisor retirement
description:
EPIC (cross-repo program). Move filesystem watching OUT of the external C++ supervisor (tldr-cli-demon, which watches the tree and pokes the daemon via the IPC 'daemon notify' command) and INTO the Rust daemon via the notify crate + notify-debouncer-full: watcher -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker. Then reduce the C++ side to a launchd babysitter (ejm). Decision basis: bfc (Route 2).

WHY ITS OWN EPIC (split out of ac0, 2026-06-03): the watcher is not usearch-store work; it is a coupled, CROSS-REPO cutover. The C++ supervisor is NOT in this repo, so the bfc 'no dual watcher ownership' transition guard cannot be enforced from here alone. Shipping the Rust watcher ON while the C++ fsnotifier is still live = two writers racing deltas. So the cutover needs explicit sequencing across repos.

FOUNDATION ALREADY SHIPPED (in-repo): handle_notify already applies per-file deltas via IndexManager::apply_delta (t8f) + the is_corpus_file source filter (ac0.6); qr9 closed (invalidation off the async path). So ac0.2 = replace the coarse-invalidate + stubbed-reindex branch with a channel-fed worker that calls the SAME delta logic (refactor it into a shared process_dirty_file()).

DEFERRED DECISIONS (resolve inside this epic, not yet): (a) config-gate DEFAULT for the in-daemon watcher — default-OFF/opt-in vs ON vs env-gated (TLDR_IN_DAEMON_WATCH); (b) fate of the IPC Notify command / 'tldr daemon notify' CLI — keep as manual/git-hook poke vs remove.

DESIGN CONSTRAINTS for ac0.2 (advisor-validated 2026-06-03): watch the CANONICAL project root (FSEvents emits /private/var-style canonical paths; the store keys via root_relative — a non-canonical root makes every delta/delete silently MISS, the ss3 bug class). Add an EXPLICIT canonical store_dir+cache_dir exclusion in the event filter AND a startup precondition assert that they are outside the canonical root (the self-write-loop guard must be ENFORCED, not incidental via is_corpus_file's hidden-dir skip). Write the self-write trap test against an IN-TREE store so it is non-vacuous. Pin notify to the version notify-debouncer-full re-exports (avoid two notify versions). Hold the debouncer for the daemon lifetime (dropping it stops watching; drop-on-shutdown is the stop mechanism). Run one integration test through a SYMLINKED tmpdir so a path-canonicalization mismatch fails loudly.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
DE-STALE 2026-06-03 (post-Codex review + user decision; supersedes the cautious framing + 'DEFERRED DECISIONS' in the DESCRIPTION):
RESOLVED:
- C++ fsnotifier will be DISABLED outright (not coexisted), so the 'no dual watcher ownership' guard is MOOT -> c5d CLOSED.
- ac0.2 ships DEFAULT-ON (no config-gate).
- IPC Notify command + 'tldr daemon notify' CLI are KEPT as a manual/git-hook poke -> taf CLOSED.
- C++ side = DISABLE not delete (keep fsnotifier dormant; centralize the guard in respawn_fsnotifier across all 3 respawn sites; delete later). Tracked in tldr-cli-demon-pw5 (supersedes iyb).
- Warm-after-edit: disabling C++ fsnotifier ends supervisor-driven warm-after-edit; ACCEPTABLE because ac0.2 makes the Rust daemon own it in-process (startup/recovery warms unaffected).
CODEX-MANDATED ac0.2 scope (recorded on ac0.2): serialized + coalesced delta worker (older delta can overwrite newer; default-ON+unbounded ingress = burst storm), deletes bypass is_corpus_file, canonicalize path before salsa key, drop watcher before persist_stats. ac0.7 keeps the advanced (>200-file) burst-cap.
DEP: notify-debouncer-full = =0.7.0, notify via its re-export.
NET PATH = 2 moves: (1) tldr-code ac0.2 Rust watcher default-ON, (2) tldr-cli-demon disable guard. Children: ac0.2 + ejm open; c5d + taf closed.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ARCHITECTURE INTERSECTION (ADR-9/ADR-11, epic TLDR-7pp, 2026-06-05): the in-daemon notify watcher becomes the SENSOR for the tier model. Its invalidation semantics CHANGE under lever B (TLDR-iqr): a file event no longer nukes the whole-project cache entry — it (1) evicts ONE Tier-1 FileIR memo entry (content-hash keyed) and (2) sets the project dirty flag; recompose is LAZY on next query (a save burst = one rebuild). Debounce/coalesce work (TLDR-ac0.7) aligns naturally: the dirty flag IS the coalescing point for the analysis flow. When implementing the cutover, target this contract — not the legacy whole-entry invalidation.

title: Notify delta: source-files-only filter (§6 first gate) before chunk_file
description:
CORRECTNESS GAP found in the t8f P1 delta review (advisor). IndexManager::apply_delta runs chunk_file on ANY path a Notify carries. A non-corpus / ignored path (node_modules/, target/, generated files, the .tldr/ dir, unknown extensions, binaries) yields zero chunks -> apply_file_delta writes an EMPTY FileRecord for a file the full build never indexed, diverging the store's per-file records from what ProjectWalker would produce. A path that IS indexable-by-extension but excluded by ignore rules would get INDEXED by the delta though the build skips it. Either way the resident store drifts from a clean rebuild = the ss3 divergence class.

FIX: apply the SAME ProjectWalker ignore rules / source-file gate as the build, as the FIRST gate in apply_delta (design doc §6 'Source-files-only filter'), before chunk_file. Drop the Notify (no-op, store untouched) if the path is not an indexable corpus file. Edit AND delete paths must both honor it (a delete for an ignored path must not touch records).
acceptance_criteria: A Notify for a non-corpus/ignored path (node_modules, target, .tldr, unknown ext, binary) is dropped before chunk_file — no FileRecord written, store len unchanged; an indexable corpus-file Notify still deltas; gate uses the same rules as the build walker (no separate ignore list); covered by a unit/e2e test asserting an ignored-path Notify is a no-op.
notes:
DONE (2026-06-03). §6 first gate landed in IndexManager::apply_delta.
EDIT path: tldr_core::semantic::is_corpus_file(project, file) — single-file gate delegating to the SAME ProjectWalker/WalkBuilder stack as the build (gitignore + git_global + git_exclude + parents, DEFAULT_EXCLUDE_DIRS, generated-dir sentinels, JS/TS preserved dirs, is_binary_or_hidden, Language::from_path). Walks only the O(depth) ancestor chain of the target (filter_entry prunes non-ancestor dirs), so cost is bounded. No separate ignore list (AC requirement met).
DELETE path: store-as-source-of-truth (per advisor) — apply_file_delete is a verified clean no-op (Ok(0), no FileRecord written) for an unknown path, so removed==0 => Filtered (store untouched), removed>0 => Deleted{removed}. This inherits ALL build filter rules by construction (the store IS the walker's output) and zeroes divergence risk — REPLACED the earlier is_plausible_corpus_path path-replica (which could drift from the walker, e.g. JS/TS-preserved dist/).
ORDER (after advisor reorder): is_delete -> cheap cold-store model-capture short-circuit (Skipped, no walk) -> is_corpus_file edit filter (Filtered) -> enrich gate (NeedsRebuild) -> delete/edit. Cold churn (git checkout / npm install before first query) no longer pays a discarded walker build.
New DeltaOutcome::Filtered variant (distinct from cold-store Skipped). Caller (daemon.rs handle_notify) treats it as no-op (only NeedsRebuild invalidates).
TESTS: chunker_tests::is_corpus_file_* (5: accepts recognized source, rejects unknown ext / excluded dir / gitignored / missing file). index_manager tests (7 total): non-corpus edit paths (node_modules/target/unknown-ext/.git) -> Filtered store unchanged; ignored delete -> Filtered; gitignored .py -> Filtered; corpus-file delete -> Deleted{1} store_len 0->. seeded_manager now sets a FileRecord (prior delete test was vacuous without it). e2e test_semantic_delta_edit_keeps_count_and_delete_removes_all green. Full workspace builds; tldr-daemon/tldr-mcp tests green (one unrelated flaky perf microbench bench_cache_hit_latency 1.099us vs 1us target).
close_reason: Closed

title: zxb.4: Wire daemon warm+query onto resident VectorStore
description: Replace Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>> with Mutex<Option<VectorStore>> in DaemonState. Warm command: VectorStore::load (or build+save on miss) + corpus digest check. Query: embed query + search the resident store. Freshness check at load/notify time, NOT per query. Factor the search core from zxb.1 is reused here against the resident store. This is the biggest beneficiary — 4.3s JSON load on every warm becomes near-instant on the common (nothing changed) case.
close_reason: Done. Daemon semantic_index (Mutex<Option<SemanticIndex>>) replaced with semantic_store (Mutex<Option<(EmbeddingModel, VectorStore)>>). Warm uses load_or_build_store (near-instant on fresh store). Query uses query_store against resident store. Notify clears the slot. Build clean.

title: zxb.3: Wire embed command (embed.rs) onto VectorStore::build+save
description: Replace the manual chunk+EmbeddingCache+Embedder flow in embed.rs with VectorStore::build()+save(). The embed command becomes: build the store, persist it, report stats. EmbeddingCache stays as the build-time dedup layer inside VectorStore::build (not removed — that is TLDR-3gj).
close_reason: Done. embed.rs rewired to load_or_build_store(). Removed manual chunk/cache/embedder flow, --include-vectors flag (vectors in store). Build clean.

title: zxb.2: Wire CLI cold search path (semantic.rs) onto search_with_store
description: Replace SemanticIndex::build().search() at semantic.rs:153 with search_with_store(). Decide store_dir location (global cache sibling). Add store-outside-corpus guard. Dense-only path (non-hybrid). Hybrid --hybrid flag keeps SemanticIndex for now (see zxb.5).
close_reason: Done. CLI dense-only path wired to search_with_store(). store_dir_for() added to tldr-core for shared daemon/CLI path resolution. Hybrid stays on SemanticIndex (zxb.5). Build clean, no warnings.

title: zxb.1: Strip SemanticIndex fallback from store_search.rs (TLDR-lx7)
description: Foundation for all other zxb sub-tasks. Remove the SemanticIndex fallback branch from search_with_store(). On any VectorStore failure (load/build/search), propagate a detailed error message — never silently degrade. Changes: (1) Remove SemanticIndex import from store_search.rs. (2) Remove the Err(e) => { eprintln warn; SemanticIndex::build fallback } branch. (3) VectorStore errors propagate as TldrResult::Err with actionable messages. (4) Factor out the search core (embed query + search store + hits_to_report) so the daemon can reuse it against a resident store without the load/build wrapper. (5) Update tests. Does NOT touch any call site — just the helper module.
close_reason: Done. SemanticIndex fallback removed from store_search.rs. Three public functions: search_with_store (cold CLI), load_or_build_store (daemon warm), query_store (daemon per-query). All errors propagate. 7/7 unit tests pass, build clean.

title: DECISION: Route 2 — filesystem watching lives in the Rust daemon (notify crate); C++ supervisor reduced to launchd lifecycle
description: Chosen over keeping the external C++ supervisor (tldr-cli-demon) as the watcher driving the daemon via the IPC Notify command. The in-daemon notify-crate watcher (TLDR-ac0.2) is co-located with the in-RAM index it mutates: event -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker, no cross-process hop. Rationale: consolidation (one Rust process), dissolves TLDR-qr9 by construction, and makes incremental delta (t8f) an in-process channel not an IPC contract. Explicitly NOT a performance claim (notify and the C++ fsnotifier use the same OS primitives). The C++ supervisor shrinks to a launchd-backed babysitter or is retired (TLDR-ejm). HARD precondition: store/cache dir MUST be outside the watched corpus (self-write loop). User decision, 2026-06-02.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-02). Durable decision; closing so it stays queryable without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if revisited.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: REFINEMENT 2026-06-03: Route 2 stands. Cutover mechanics decided: the C++ fsnotifier is DISABLED (guard in respawn_fsnotifier, kept dormant) rather than deleted immediately; the Rust watcher ships DEFAULT-ON; IPC Notify is retained as a manual poke. 'No dual ownership' is satisfied by disabling C++, not by a staged config-gate (c5d closed). See epic TLDR-4vb + tldr-cli-demon-pw5.

title: DECISION: Daemon vector index is fully in-memory (RwLock-resident), not view()/mmap
description: The daemon holds the vector index FULLY IN RAM (load() into a writable Index behind the IndexManager RwLock, mutated in place), NOT served via usearch view()/mmap. Refines TLDR-zl8 (view-for-queries / load-for-mutation) into a two-path model: daemon/warm path = RAM-resident RwLock<VectorStore> with concurrent reads + in-place delta writes; cold CLI (no daemon) MAY view()/mmap or also load to RAM. CONSEQUENCE: the view()/mmap GC reader-grace race (TLDR-pdb) no longer governs daemon query+reindex safety (the RwLock does); pdb narrows to cold-CLI-only and may be moot. Compiler-proven prerequisite (memory concurrency-sync-evidence-2026-06-02): VectorStore + Arc<RwLock<VectorStore>> are Send+Sync with zero surgery. User decision, 2026-06-02.
close_reason: ACCEPTED (ADR record, 2026-06-02). Durable decision; closing so it stays queryable without sitting in the active work queue. Reopen only if revisited.

title: In-daemon filesystem watcher (notify crate) — replace IPC Notify + C++ fsnotifier; rewrite handle_notify to channel
description: Bring file-watch detection INTO the Rust daemon via the notify crate (+ notify-debouncer-full), co-located with the in-RAM index it mutates. Watcher thread -> internal mpsc channel -> reindex worker. Removes the cross-process C++ fsnotifier->IPC hop and dissolves TLDR-qr9 by construction (invalidation flows via channel, never an async-thread mutex). The Notify IPC command becomes optional (keep as manual git-hook poke or delete). Rewrites the stubbed handle_notify (daemon.rs:1172 — currently nukes the whole index; comment admits 'In full implementation, would spawn background reindex task').
design: Route 2 (chosen 2026-06-02 over keeping C++ fsnotifier+IPC). Watch the canonical project root recursively on daemon start; stop on shutdown. Reuse chunker::enumerate_corpus_files as the SINGLE membership filter (watch-set == index-set, coupling-proof per kkt). HARD PRECONDITION: store_dir + cache_dir MUST be outside the watched corpus, else the daemon's own index writes fire events -> reindex -> write -> infinite loop (even more critical with a live watcher than with the poll gate). Debounce editor save-storms / git-checkout bursts via notify-debouncer-full. Honest framing: notify is NOT faster than fsnotifier (same OS primitives FSEvents/kqueue); the win is consolidation (one Rust process), killing qr9, and making t8f an in-process channel not an IPC contract — NOT performance.
acceptance_criteria: Daemon self-watches via notify; file create/modify/delete -> dirty events on internal channel (no IPC); a write INSIDE the store dir triggers nothing (self-write-loop trap test); qr9 invalidation no longer touches a lock on the async path.
notes:
2026-06-02 (Codex review): (1) CORRECT WORDING — handle_notify (daemon.rs:1172) is NOT a full stub: it already records dirty files, invalidates the salsa cache, and sets the resident index to None (coarse invalidation, daemon.rs:1188). ONLY the reindex branch is stubbed (daemon.rs:1202 'would spawn background reindex task'). Scope = replace coarse-invalidate + stubbed-reindex with channel-fed delta, not 'write the handler'. (2) TRANSITION GUARD — during cutover, ensure NO dual watcher ownership: the C++ fsnotifier must be disabled when the in-daemon notify watcher is active (don't run both → double events / races). Coordinate with TLDR-ejm.
2026-06-02 CORRECTION (de-stale): TLDR-qr9 is now CLOSED — its interim fix landed (handle_notify invalidation moved into an awaited spawn_blocking). So ac0.2 no longer 'dissolves an open bug'; it SUPERSEDES the interim spawn_blocking with the channel-fed design (watcher -> mpsc -> reindex worker). File:line refs here predate the qr9 commit and may be off by a few lines.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN REFINEMENT (2026-06-02, post-zxb): The watcher feeds dirty events into the existing dirty_files set. Reindex is DEBOUNCED, not instantaneous — idle timeout OR threshold count triggers a batch reindex of only the accumulated dirty files. This prevents "compute always on" — the daemon is quiet while the user is actively editing, and processes changes in one batch when they pause. The zxb commit landed VectorStore in the daemon (Mutex<Option<(Model, VectorStore)>>), so the watcher now has a resident store to apply deltas to. The notify handler already clears the store on any change (coarse invalidation) — ac0.2 replaces this with fine-grained channel-fed delta via t8f.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: 2026-06-03: RE-PARENTED from ac0 (usearch store epic) to the new TLDR-4vb 'Filesystem-watch cutover' epic — the watcher is cutover work, not store work. Status reset to OPEN (was briefly claimed). BUILD GUIDANCE when picked: implement DEFAULT-OFF / opt-in (config or env TLDR_IN_DAEMON_WATCH) so it lands in-repo without double-watching the live C++ fsnotifier (bfc). Honor the design constraints recorded on 4vb (canonical root; explicit store/cache exclusion + startup precondition assert; non-vacuous in-tree self-write trap test; pin notify to debouncer's re-export; hold debouncer for daemon lifetime; symlinked-tmpdir integration test). Refactor handle_notify's delta body into a shared process_dirty_file() used by both the channel worker and the IPC Notify command.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: 2026-06-03 UPDATE (supersedes the earlier default-OFF guidance): user will remove the C++ fsnotifier outright, so build ac0.2 DEFAULT-ON (self-watch on daemon start) — no config-gate/dual-ownership dance needed (c5d closed). Keep IPC Notify as a manual poke (taf closed: keep). Design constraints from 4vb still apply: canonical project root; explicit canonical store_dir+cache_dir exclusion + startup precondition assert; non-vacuous in-tree self-write trap test; pin notify to debouncer's re-export; hold debouncer for daemon lifetime; symlinked-tmpdir integration test; refactor handle_notify delta body into shared process_dirty_file().

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
CODEX REVIEW (2026-06-03) — ac0.2 is UNDER-SCOPED as 'just add a watcher default-ON'. Must-fix before/with default-ON:
1) DELTA ORDERING (flaw): Notify IPC stays active + watcher worker both run deltas; apply_delta chunks/embeds OUTSIDE the write lock then applies under it (index_manager apply_file_delta accepts supplied vectors), so an OLDER delta can overwrite a NEWER one for the same file. FIX: serialize delta application — single worker draining the channel, deltas applied one-at-a-time; consider newest-wins/coalesce per file.
2) BURST STORM (flaw): default-ON + unbounded ingress risks a reindex storm on git checkout/npm install (each event chunks+embeds; the existing threshold is a stub that doesn't actually reindex/cap). FIX: bounded/coalesced channel + at least basic burst handling IN ac0.2 (this overlaps ac0.7 — fold the minimal coalescing in; leave advanced >200-file->full-rebuild cap to ac0.7).
3) DELETES must NOT be gated through is_corpus_file (returns false when the file is gone) — watcher filter forwards deletes straight to apply_delta's dedicated delete path; is_corpus_file is for edits/creates only.
4) SALSA KEY: handle_notify hashes the RAW path (salsa hash_path); watcher vs IPC canonical mismatch -> wrong key. Normalize/canonicalize the path before invalidate in the shared process_dirty_file().
5) Explicit store/cache exclusion is REQUIRED (is_corpus_file alone insufficient — daemon writes .tldr/cache + .tldr/daemon.log in-tree).
6) DROP the watcher before persist_stats on shutdown (persist writes .tldr/cache which the live watcher would see).
7) Hold debouncer in run() (not moved into a Send-spawned task); store-in-self surfaces Send/Sync on Arc<Self> spawns.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DEP DECISION (2026-06-03): add ONLY 'notify-debouncer-full = "=0.7.0"' (exact-pin per repo convention; 0.8 is an RC) and access notify via its re-export (notify_debouncer_full::notify::*) — structurally avoids two notify versions (Codex). IMPL CAVEAT for checklist #4 (canonicalize path before salsa hash): must FIRST verify self.project is canonical (DAEMON.md claims identity uses canonicalized path) — if process_dirty_file canonicalizes the file but project is raw, root_relative/key_chunks key-mismatch (ss3 class) breaks the existing e2e delta tests (daemon.rs ~2165). Verify project canonicality + run the e2e delta tests as part of the refactor; don't canonicalize blindly.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE CLARIFICATION 2026-06-03 (title is now partly stale): IPC Notify is KEPT (taf), not replaced — the watcher and the kept IPC Notify both feed the shared process_dirty_file(). The C++ fsnotifier is DISABLED (not replaced/removed) — see tldr-cli-demon-pw5. So the live title 'replace IPC Notify + C++ fsnotifier' reads as: KEEP IPC Notify, DISABLE C++ fsnotifier, add the in-daemon watcher (default-ON) as the primary source.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
STEP-1 CANONICALITY VERIFICATION (2026-06-03, pre-process_dirty_file). Verified all three key paths against source; result SIMPLIFIES the plan and DROPS the canonicalize-before-salsa-key fix.

1. self.project canonicality: PRODUCTION = canonical (start.rs:79 canonicalizes before TLDRDaemon::new at start.rs:178). TESTS = NON-canonical (daemon.rs:1312 passes temp.path(); macOS /var symlink). Construction-level canonicalization is OFF THE TABLE — it breaks assert_eq!(daemon.project(), temp.path()) at daemon.rs:1314 and the fake_path assert at 2321. This is BY DESIGN: self.project stays non-canonical and the downstream keying was hardened instead.

2. Vector-store delta keying ALREADY hardened (no per-file canonicalization needed): root_relative (vector_store.rs:997) does lexical strip -> canonical-strip-BOTH -> deterministic fallback; deleted_file_rel (index_manager.rs:398) strips against project AND project.canonicalize(), matching stored keys under a symlinked root (ss3 class) by construction. Canonicalizing the incoming file in apply_delta would BREAK deletes (a gone file cannot canonicalize) — this is the real reason for 'delete passthrough'. Net: pass the path through as-is to apply_delta.

3. Salsa query-cache key: BOTH sides hash the RAW client path. Registration handlers pass vec![hash_path(&file)] on the raw file from the command (daemon.rs:584/703/736/773/965); invalidation in handle_notify (daemon.rs:1093) hashes the raw Notify file the same way. They ALREADY match. Canonicalizing in notify ALONE would DIVERGE from the raw-path registration side -> strictly worse (invalidation would miss). DECISION: do NOT canonicalize before the salsa key. The 'canonicalize-before-salsa-key fix' is dropped; any symmetric canonicalization (both register+invalidate) is a separate, larger change out of ac0.2 scope.

CONSEQUENCE for process_dirty_file(): NO canonicalization anywhere. (a) hash raw file path for salsa invalidation (matches registration); (b) pass raw file path to apply_delta (robust keying + delete passthrough). Confirmed with advisor.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
IN-REPO IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (2026-06-03), shipped DEFAULT-OFF pending cross-repo C++ disable. All gates green: cargo build clean; clippy 0 errors (no new warnings in changed files); watcher unit+smoke 7/7; full daemon suite 267 passed / 2 pre-existing ignored.

FILES CHANGED (tldr-code, branch tldr-cli-daemon-support):
- daemon.rs: extracted process_dirty_file() from handle_notify (canonicalization-free, delete-passthrough — see prior step-1 comment); handle_notify now delegates. Added ReindexOutcome struct. Wired spawn_watcher into run() under #[cfg(feature=semantic)] with a NAMED _watcher_guard (let _ would drop the Debouncer immediately). Added #[cfg(test)] dirty_file_count() observable.
- watcher.rs (NEW, semantic-gated): notify-debouncer-full(500ms) -> watch_decision() filter -> bounded mpsc(1024, try_send drop-on-full) -> single serialized coalescing worker (drains queue into a dedup HashSet, newest-wins; ordering intentionally discarded because apply_delta re-reads disk state) -> process_dirty_file(). spawn_watcher() returns Option<WatcherGuard>; refuses (logged) if store_dir is inside root. watch_decision() is a PURE fn (extracted per advisor) so trap/corpus/symlink/delete tests need no live daemon.
- types.rs: DaemonConfig.enable_watcher (#[serde(default)], DEFAULT FALSE). Default OFF because Move-2 (C++ fsnotifier disable) is CROSS-REPO (tldr-cli-demon / TLDR-ejm, still open); running both watchers double-feeds process_dirty_file (wasteful no-op via content_hash, but pointless). Flip to ON lands WITH the C++ disable.
- Cargo.toml (workspace + tldr-cli): notify-debouncer-full = =0.7.0; notify consumed via its re-export. Lockfile resolves single notify v8.2.0 — no two-version skew.

SELF-WRITE TRAP: closed by a PREFIX exclusion of in-tree <root>/.tldr (where persist_stats writes salsa_stats.json) + store_dir. The prefix check (not is_corpus_file) is load-bearing for DELETES — is_corpus_file canonicalizes and so returns false for a gone file. External store_dir (~/.cache/tldr/stores) is outside root by design; the trap test correctly targets in-tree .tldr, not the (vacuous) external store dir.

ACCEPTANCE STATUS: daemon self-watches via notify ✓; create/modify/delete -> dirty events on internal channel, no IPC ✓; in-store/.tldr write triggers nothing ✓ (trap test incl. delete); qr9 invalidation off the async lock path ✓ (channel->worker; semantic delta in spawn_blocking). REMAINING (not a code gap): flip enable_watcher default ON, coordinated with the cross-repo C++ fsnotifier disable (TLDR-ejm) to avoid dual ownership.
