title: Define MmapVectorStore format + abstraction
description: Design the on-disk format and the Rust abstraction for the vector store. Format: header (magic, version, model name, dim, count) + contiguous f32 vectors region (fixed stride = dim*4, 4-byte aligned) + a hash->offset index map (bincode) at the tail. One file per embedding model (dim is fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / arctic-m 768 / arctic-l 1024). Key = 64-bit hash(content_hash + file_path + function_name) -- same identity as today's CacheKey minus model (model implied by file). Define a trait so the backend is swappable. Must support zero-copy &[f32] reads from the mmap.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by usearch decision (TLDR-rgc). usearch defines the on-disk index format + abstraction; no custom MmapVectorStore format to design. Residual (thin wrapper + metadata sidecar) folded into reframed TLDR-l5d.

title: Lock backend + GC decisions for mmap store
description: Finalize the two open design forks before implementation: (1) Backend = plain mmap (memmap2 + bincode hash->offset map) vs usearch. Leaning plain mmap: usearch builds HNSW on every add() (adds cost to the embed path, our 2nd bottleneck) for a search we don't use; pure-Rust avoids a C++ build dep; brute-force stays exact at any scale this tool targets. (2) GC = reachability-on-full-reindex (leaning; drops cached_at/file_mtime, entry becomes key->Vec<f32>) vs timestamp bookkeeping file. Output: a recorded decision (bd decision) that unblocks the impl children.
design: DECIDED: usearch. C++ build dep (cxx bridge) accepted by user 2026-06-01. usearch exact_search_f32 + view (mmap) + save/load + ScalarKind cover the exact-search + load + persistence + quant goals as a maintained library. GC fork: GC-by-reachability on full re-index (usearch index rebuilt from chunks; drops per-entry timestamps -> simplest, matches the rebuild model). Cascade recorded in memory 'usearch-discovery'/'decision-locked'.
close_reason: DECIDED: usearch (C++ dep accepted). The mmap-vs-usearch fork is resolved in favor of usearch — exact_search_f32 + view + save/load + ScalarKind. GC = reachability-on-full-reindex. Unblocks the (reframed) impl children.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DECISION EVIDENCE (2026-06-01): The mmap-vs-usearch fork was framed on a FALSE premise. This issue's description says usearch 'builds HNSW on every add() for a search we dont use' and implies approximate-only. Scanned usearch/rust/lib.rs: it has exact_search_f32 (L456) = EXACT brute-force, and view (L542) = mmap load. So usearch delivers ac0's EXACT goal (mmap-backed exact search) as a maintained library, collapsing the 8 ac0 impl children into one integration. The HNSW-build-on-add cost is negligible vs the ~30min CPU embed. The decision therefore reduces to a SINGLE axis: usearch adds a C++ compiler dependency (cxx bridge) to the build/CI; plain mmap stays pure-Rust. RECOMMENDATION: usearch, unless 'no C++ in build' is a hard ship constraint. Awaiting that one confirmation before recording the bd decision + superseding 7kf/8pt/k4q.

title: Replace JSON embedding cache with usearch-backed vector store (keep exact search)
description:
## EPIC: Replace the JSON embedding cache with a usearch vector store

### The measured problem
Cold `tldr semantic/similar/embed` = ~4,300ms JSON parse + 500ms model load + 200ms query embed + ~0.1ms brute-force cosine. The 1.4GB cache.json (text-encoded float vectors, multiple models) is loaded IN FULL to serve a handful of lookups. The brute-force cosine is NOT the bottleneck; the JSON load is.

### Decisions — LOCKED (2026-06-01/02, incl. Codex review)
- Backend = **usearch** (NOT hand-rolled memmap2+bincode; the old "usearch vs plain mmap" open decision is CLOSED).
- **exact_search_f32** (NOT HNSW — exact/100% recall, vendor-recommended <1M vectors).
- **load() into a WRITABLE RAM index**, NOT view()/mmap (view() is read-only #97; at ~58MB f32 load() is cheap and keeps the index writable, which incremental needs). load() is scale-bounded — revisit view() past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo.
- **f32 first** (i8 = TLDR-ccg; separate quantized index + quantized query).
- Metric = cos (unit-normalized vectors).
- Win: 1.4GB JSON -> ~58MB f32 binary; daemon RSS ~2.3GB -> ~0.5GB.

### Sidecar — SCOPE EVOLVED (resolved with Codex review)
Original cold-CLI scope: ALL metadata was rebuilt by chunk_code() at search time, so NO persistent sidecar was needed (verified: cache.rs stored only {vector, cached_at, file_mtime}). That no longer holds. With the warm DAEMON path now shipped (TLDR-atc), the store is PERSISTED and served from a resident daemon, so a restart must serve results WITHOUT re-chunking the whole tree. Therefore a metadata **SIDECAR + MANIFEST** is now a **correctness** requirement of l5d (not optional, not deferrable to t8f). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (§4 sidecar/manifest, §7 crash-safe save).

### Targets / honesty
- Cold first-load: ~14s (1.4GB JSON) -> sub-second (usearch load()).
- Warm daemon repeat query: ~45ms — ALREADY ACHIEVED via atc + the root-relative cache-key fix.
- Do NOT promise a fixed latency for directory searches; the sidecar now persists metadata so warm dir searches can skip re-chunking (measure before promising).

### PASS/FAIL = results-equivalence, not latency
Exact brute-force + f32 kept -> new ranked results must be IDENTICAL to the JSON-cache path (n=52 eval + captured baselines). Latency improvement is necessary but NOT sufficient.

### Children
l5d (usearch store + sidecar + manifest + crash-safe save), m01 (rewire EmbeddingCache onto the store, keep a content-addressed dedup layer), ccg (i8 compact mode), t8f (incremental delta), zr6 (filtered/scoped search).
design:
RESOLVED design (supersedes the earlier 'plain mmap vs usearch' + 'NO sidecar' framing).

## Bottleneck (measured)
Cold query = 4.3s JSON parse + 0.5s model load + 0.2s query embed + ~0.1ms cosine. The brute-force cosine is irrelevant; HNSW would make it 0.5ms AND approximate. KEEP exact brute-force semantics via usearch exact_search_f32. KEEP f32.

## Store (usearch)
- key (u64) -> vector. add_f32 on build; remove(key) for deltas; exact_search_f32 for query; save/load (writable RAM, not view()).
- One logical store per model (dim fixed per model: arctic-s 384 / m 768 / l 1024).
- Content-addressed DEDUP layer kept SEPARATE from key->vector: a (content_hash, model, embed_schema) -> vector lookup so renames/ordinal-churn re-add without re-embedding (the current EmbeddingCache role; do NOT drop it).

## Sidecar + manifest (l5d correctness — see design doc)
- Sidecar: key -> {identity, file_rel_path, fn, class, line range, content_hash}; per-file record {keys, mtime, size}.
- Manifest covers EVERY input that changes vectors or chunk boundaries: format_version, generation, embedding_model, model_revision (tokenizer+weights), dimensions, metric, scalar_kind, search_mode, embed_schema, chunk_params, walker_version, root, chunk_count, keys_checksum, sidecar_checksum. load() rejects any mismatch -> full rebuild.
- Crash-safe save: generation-suffixed immutable files + a CURRENT pointer whose atomic rename is the sole commit point + dir fsync + keep gen-1 (design doc §7.1). usearch save() is NOT atomic by itself.
- Snippets: read lazily from source at query time, validated by content_hash, degrade gracefully (no sidecar bloat).

## GC
content_hash in the dedup key => changed content orphans old entries. GC-by-reachability on full re-index (drop keys not re-added) OR a small key->timestamp file. (Decide in m01.)

## Verification = results-equivalence
Captured baseline outputs (scores+snippets) + the n=52 eval are the golden reference. Exact brute-force + f32 => IDENTICAL ranked results old vs new. Latency alone is not pass/fail.
notes:
## Baseline Measurements (JSON-based, release build)

Cache: ~/Library/Caches/tldr/embeddings/cache.json = 1.1 GB, 117,641 entries

### Completed Tests

| Test | Command | Wall time | Internal latency_ms | Result |
|------|---------|-----------|---------------------|--------|
| 1. Fresh embed (cached) | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,393ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 2. Cached embed | `tldr embed config.rs` | 4.36s | 4,315ms | 28 chunks, all cached |
| 3. Semantic search (single file) | `tldr semantic "parse json config" config.rs` | 5.22s | 795ms | 10 results, cache_hit=false |
| 5. Cold search (single file) | `tldr semantic "error handling" config.rs` | 7.37s | 1,098ms | 10 results |
| 6. Config model check | `tldr embed config.rs` (config=arctic-l) | 4.41s | 4,370ms | model: arctic-l ✓ |
| 7. CLI flag override | `tldr embed config.rs --model arctic-s` | 4.41s | 4,378ms | model: arctic-s ✓ |
| 8. Existing tests | `cargo test semantic` | 60s (compile) / 6.88s (run) | — | 8 passed, 17 ignored |

### Key observation
Even for a CACHED 28-chunk file, embed takes 4.3s wall time. That's ~4.3s spent loading/parsing the 1.1GB JSON cache, NOT embedding. The actual search latency (795ms-1098ms) is on top of the cache parse.

### Pending Tests (running — first-time arctic-l embed of 14K chunks)
| Test | Command | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| 4. Similar function search | `tldr similar config.rs --function from_path` | Embedding 14,144 chunks (first time arctic-l) |
| 5b. Cold search (full src/) | `tldr semantic "error handling" src/` | Embedding full directory (first time arctic-l) |

These will complete and be recorded. The one-time embed cost is NOT part of the comparison — only the subsequent cached searches matter for the usearch benchmark.
2026-06-02 DECISION RESOLVED + reconciled with Codex review (thread a708b3161b2e63d89). (1) The 'usearch vs plain mmap (memmap2+bincode)' open decision is CLOSED -> usearch library (decision-locked memory: decision-locked-2026-06-01-vector-store-usearch). The hand-rolled memmap2 plan (and TLDR-j8c 'MmapVectorStore') is OBSOLETE. (2) Backend: usearch load() into a WRITABLE RAM index (NOT view()/mmap) — view() is read-only and at ~58MB f32 mmap earns nothing; load() keeps the index writable for deltas. load() is scale-bounded: revisit view() for the read path past ~20 daemons / 500MB+ monorepo. (3) SIDECAR REVISED: this epic previously said 'NO sidecar needed (metadata from re-chunk at search time)'. That holds for a COLD single-shot CLI, but NOT once the store is PERSISTED + served from a resident daemon (TLDR-atc, now done): a restart would have to re-chunk the whole tree to recover result metadata. So a metadata SIDECAR + MANIFEST is now a CORRECTNESS requirement of l5d, not optional. KEEP these still-valid caveats from the original epic text: exact brute-force (not HNSW); f32 first (i8=ccg, separate quantized index); single-file vs directory latency differ — do NOT promise 730ms for directory searches (re-chunk cost stays unless the sidecar persists metadata — which it now does). Full design: docs/INCREMENTAL_REINDEX_DESIGN.md (317293b).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: PREMISE CORRECTION (2026-06-01): This epic chose hand-rolled plain-mmap partly on 'usearch=approximate/HNSW we dont use'. That is FALSE per usearch/rust/lib.rs: exact_search_f32 (L456) gives exact brute-force, view (L542) gives mmap load, ScalarKind gives optional quantization. usearch achieves this epic's core goal (kill the 4.3s JSON load via mmap, KEEP exact results) as a library — potentially obsoleting most children (j8c/l5d/m01/2a4/3gj/lhu/za0). Open question now narrows to: accept a C++ build dep (usearch) vs more pure-Rust custom code (mmap). See TLDR-rgc + memory 'usearch-discovery-2026-06-01'. If usearch is chosen, this epic's title/scope should be reframed from 'mmap store' to 'usearch-backed vector store'.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: BENEFIT-SIZING (2026-06-01, via tldr explain/structure + daemon route scan): The 4.3s JSON load is paid on EVERY semantic query, not once-per-daemon. CONFIRMED: tldr-daemon exposes 21 routes (ping/status/tree/structure/extract/imports/calls/impact/dead/importers/arch/cfg/dfg/slice/complexity/search[BM25]/context/smells/maintainability/secrets/vuln) and ZERO semantic/similar/embed routes — semantic.rs has 0 daemon references. So every 'tldr semantic' runs cold: SemanticIndex::build -> EmbeddingCache::open -> load_with_lock (cache.rs:236) deserializes the FULL ~1.1GB JSON into a HashMap each invocation (eval RSS ~8GB). The scan it does NOT help: top_k_similar (similarity.rs:238, cyclo 3, called by SemanticIndex.search:429/find_similar:533) is 0.1ms. usearch benefit = view() mmap kills the 4.3s load on every query + RSS GBs->working-set; exact_search_f32 keeps results identical. Floor after usearch ~0.7s (0.5s model load + 0.2s query embed remain — needs a daemon warm path, separate work). usearch does NOT change recall (still 0.857); it is purely load-time + memory + maintenance.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: STATUS ROLLUP (2026-06-02). DONE: l5d (usearch store + sidecar + manifest + crash-safe gen/CURRENT save/load) CLOSED — 3 Codex passes clean. 2a4 (results-equivalence gate) CLOSED — 52/52 equivalent, REAL=0. 7al (real ManifestId inputs) CLOSED — resolved inline in m01 PR1. IN PROGRESS: m01 PR1 LANDED (store_search::search_with_store, the first production caller of VectorStore::load; load-into-RAM + SemanticIndex fallback; tested, NOT yet wired). CRITICAL PATH to ship: TLDR-kkt (store freshness gate: detect source drift -> full rebuild, the §7.3 reconcile, DISTINCT from t8f incremental) -> TLDR-zxb (wire search_with_store into CLI/daemon/MCP, decide store_dir location, capture cold-latency old-vs-new). zxb is HARD blocked-by kkt (must not wire a stale-serving path). Still open under epic: t8f (incremental delta), 3gj (migrate/discard cache.json), ccg (i8 compact), lhu (tests+docs), za0 (GC-by-reachability), zr6 (scoped/filtered search), pdb (reader-grace, only needed if/when view()/mmap is adopted). RELATED (not blocking): 2af (deterministic store tie-break — the 14 TIEs in the eval), 4oz (PRE-EXISTING find_similar path-match bug, breaks tldr similar + the ignored find_similar test; NOT an m01 regression), qr9 (daemon handle_notify mutex — relevant when zxb wires the daemon path).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: POST-ZXB STATUS (2026-06-02): VectorStore is now wired into all production paths (CLI search, embed, daemon warm+query). SemanticIndex fallback removed (TLDR-lx7). The "nothing changed" query path is fast (disk load + stat check). REMAINING BOTTLENECK: rebuild on source drift still goes through EmbeddingCache JSON (4.3s load). Fix chain: ac0.1 (RwLock) -> t8f (incremental delta, debounced batch) -> ac0.2 (watcher) -> 3gj (kill JSON). CONFIRMED FLOW for incremental: file changes accumulate in dirty set -> idle timeout OR threshold -> batch reindex only dirty files -> compute is bursty not constant.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: PROGRESS (2026-06-02): Incremental-reindex delta core landed. TLDR-t8f CLOSED — handle_notify now applies a surgical per-file delta to the resident store (re-embed only changed funcs, remove deleted vectors, meta-only line shifts) instead of a blanket invalidate; full-rebuild fallback intact; verified e2e against a warmed store. Shared key_chunks() guarantees build/delta key parity. Two follow-ups filed on the Notify pipeline: TLDR-ac0.6 (P1 — source-files-only filter, §6 first gate, correctness) and TLDR-ac0.7 (P2 — debounce/coalesce + burst cap, §6). EPIC child status: l5d/atc/ac0.1/zxb/m01/t8f CLOSED; OPEN — ac0.2 (watcher), ac0.5 (resident embedder; note: t8f already added a resident Embedder to IndexManager, so ac0.5 may now be partially satisfied — review), ac0.6, ac0.7, ccg, zr6, 3gj, za0, lhu, pdb, 2af, ac0.3. Epic remains OPEN.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
DE-STALE 2026-06-03 (post-ac0.5 review):
1) DESCRIPTION '### Children' prose (l5d/m01/ccg/t8f/zr6) is OUTDATED. Canonical child list = the dependency graph: 22 children, 13 done (59%). Done incl. ac0.1, ac0.4, ac0.5, ac0.6, t8f, kkt, zxb, 7al, 2a4, j8c, l5d, m01, rgc. OPEN: ac0.2 (P1 — the keystone in-daemon notify watcher), ac0.7, ccg, zr6, lhu, 3gj, za0, pdb.
2) ac0.5 (resident embedder reuse) is DONE + VERIFIED — parity (warm==cold rankings) + build-once-counter + empty-guard + config-call-site tests; commits 79e0bea/302e815. Production model selection is config-resolved at every entry point (CLI semantic/embed/similar, daemon, MCP) — audited.
3) DESIGN '## GC ... (Decide in m01)' pointer is STALE: m01 closed WITHOUT finalizing GC. GC model = rebuild-from-live-chunks on full re-index (per rgc), tracked in za0; view()/mmap reader-grace narrowed to cold-CLI-only in pdb (likely moot under the in-RAM decision 3il).
4) NOTES 'Pending Tests (running...)' table = historical one-time arctic-l embeds, long completed. Ignore.
5) Backend fork RESOLVED: usearch chosen (uwb), exact_search_f32 (zim), load() into writable RAM not view() (3il). The 'plain mmap vs usearch' framing in older notes is obsolete (j8c closed).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: 2026-06-03: The in-daemon filesystem watcher (ac0.2) and the C++ supervisor retirement (ejm) were MOVED OUT of this epic into the new TLDR-4vb 'Filesystem-watch cutover' epic — they are a coupled cross-repo program, not usearch-store work. ac0 stays scoped to the vector store (store/sidecar/delta/quantization/scoped-search). Child count drops accordingly.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: PATTERN ALIGNMENT NOTE (ADR-9, 2026-06-05): the analysis flow's new serving model (epic TLDR-7pp) independently converged on this epic's patterns — immutable snapshot behind ArcSwap (= vector store generation commits), per-file content-hash deltas (= t8f), lazy rebuild on dirty. The two flows now share the same lifecycle shape end-to-end, which was the original two-flow constraint's goal. GC reader-grace (TLDR-pdb) findings should inform the CSR snapshot swap if any mmap variant lands later (tripwire T2).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: CROSS-FILED from TLDR-uiy audit 36/64 (Codex find): load_or_build_store silently rebuilds on ANY VectorStore::load failure (store_search.rs:141-149) — missing/stale rebuild and genuine-load-error are conflated; the latter should surface loudly (lx7 doctrine). Split the two paths when touching the store lifecycle.

title: Wire resolved embedding model into Embedder (provider seam, local-only)
description: Make tldr semantic / tldr embed use the CONFIGURED model instead of the hardcoded default. Resolution order for model: -m flag > config embedding.model > built-in default (arctic-m). parse_model() already maps arctic-xs/s/m/m-long/l (semantic.rs:123) and to_fastembed_model() maps the enum (embedder.rs:187). Provider seam: if embedding.provider != "local", FAIL FAST at load with a clear message ('cloud embedding providers not supported in this build') — fields are still parsed so the file stays forward-valid. This delivers the core goal: per-project default model without recompiling. Tests: config model honored, -m flag overrides config, non-local provider errors cleanly.
close_reason: Added EmbeddingModel::parse() and EmbeddingModel::resolve() to tldr-core. resolve() implements precedence: CLI flag > config embedding.model > built-in default. Provider seam: fails fast with clear message when provider != local. Removed 3 duplicated parse_model() functions from embed.rs, semantic.rs, similar.rs; replaced with config-aware EmbeddingModel::resolve().

title: Config loader: precedence chain + per-field deep merge
description: Resolve effective config across layers. Load ~/.tldr/config.json (global, optional) then <project>/.tldr/config.json (project), deep-merge per field (project wins), then apply CLI-flag overrides (flag wins) as the final layer. Missing file at any layer = no-op. Project root resolution should match existing tldr conventions (the dir containing .tldr / .git / manifest). Output: a single resolved config struct the commands consume. Tests: global-only, project-overrides-global, CLI-overrides-both, all-absent = built-in defaults.
close_reason: Added merge() method for deep per-field merge (project wins over global), resolve() method that loads ~/.tldr/config.json then <project>/.tldr/config.json with deep merge. 5 tests: project-overrides-global, preserves-unset-fields, semantic-disabled-wins, all-absent-defaults, no-project-root.

title: Define TldrConfig serde structs + tolerant JSON parsing
close_reason: Added TldrConfig, EmbeddingConfig, SemanticConfig serde structs in tldr-core/src/config.rs with serde(default) on all fields, no deny_unknown_fields. from_str + from_path (returns default on missing file). 7 unit tests: empty {}, partial, unknown-keys-ignored, full cloud-shaped, missing file, malformed JSON, semantic disabled.

title: Per-project config file (.tldr/config.json) for embedding model + settings
description:
Goal: let users change the embedding model (and future settings) per project WITHOUT recompiling. Today the model is hardcoded (default_value="arctic-m" in semantic.rs:36 / embed.rs; impl Default for EmbeddingModel). No config-file machinery exists in tldr-code (no toml dep, no loader, no app Config struct).

Design (settled):
- ONE JSON file per project: .tldr/config.json. Optional global ~/.tldr/config.json.
- Precedence (deep per-field merge): CLI flag > .tldr/config.json > ~/.tldr/config.json > built-in default. Missing file/field = built-in default = today's behavior.
- Tolerant parsing: serde(default) on everything, NO deny_unknown_fields — ignore unknown keys so newer configs don't break older binaries (forward-compat).
- Schema (single embedding block, provider-discriminated so cloud slots in with no restructure):
  { version:int=1,
    embedding: { provider:"local", model:"arctic-l", endpoint?:str, api_key_env?:str, dimensions?:int },
    semantic:  { enabled:bool=true, langs?:[str] } }
- Built-in default model stays arctic-m (conservative; small/fast). Config opts up to arctic-l etc.
- Note: the semantic EMBEDDING cache is GLOBAL (~/.tldr/embeddings/cache.json) keyed by abs file_path:function:hash:model — per-project model choices coexist safely. Structural Salsa cache is per-project (.tldr/cache).
- No JSON-Schema validation file; loader validates at runtime.

This feature umbrella tracks the sub-tasks.
close_reason: All sub-tasks complete: TldrConfig structs (qw2), config loader with precedence chain (hzv), and embedder wiring with provider seam (5xn). Per-project .tldr/config.json is now supported with global ~/.tldr/config.json fallback and deep merge. CLI flag > project config > global config > built-in default. Cloud provider fails fast with clear error.

title: Install tldr diagnostic tools for supported languages
description:
tldr-code has optional diagnostic tools (doctor, diagnostics) that require external language tools to be installed. Need to run 'tldr doctor --install <language>' for each supported language in the project.

**Problem**: The current install flow does NOT mention these optional tools anywhere. Users (and agents) have no visibility into these additional capabilities, so they never get installed or used. This means diagnostic features like 'tldr doctor' and language-specific analysis are silently unavailable.

**What needs to happen**:
1. Update the install flow/documentation to include an 'Optional Diagnostic Tools' step
2. Make it clear which tools are available per language and how to install them
3. Ensure agents and users discover these capabilities during setup

Languages and their tools:
- Python: pyright, ruff, mypy
- TypeScript: typescript-language-server, tsc
- Go: gopls, golangci-lint
- Rust: rustc, cargo
- Java: checkstyle, spotbugs

Install command: tldr doctor --install <language>

Steps:
1. Run 'tldr doctor' to check current state
2. Add optional tools section to the install flow
3. Install tools for languages used in this project (Rust is primary)
4. Verify installation with 'tldr doctor' again
notes: Discovered during session — the entire workflow (CLAUDE.md, setup docs, onboarding) is missing any mention of these optional diagnostic tools. This is a gap in discoverability, not just installation.
close_reason: Updated CLAUDE.md with build commands, diagnostic tools section (tldr doctor --install), architecture overview, and conventions. INSTALL.md already had the optional tools section; SETUP.md already mentions tldr doctor. The gap was agent instructions not surfacing these capabilities.

title: smells: DataClumps silently unimplemented (zero findings always); FeatureEnvy without --deep runs empty stub silently
description: Found during TLDR-uiy audit 12/64 (Codex-assisted, core-side verified). (1) DataClumps: SmellType::DataClumps is user-reachable via --smell-type data-clumps and listed in should_run_original_detectors (tldr-core/src/quality/smells.rs:2767), but NO detect_data_clumps_* implementation exists anywhere in the pipeline (collect_tier1_ast_smells lines 761-818 never calls one) -> always zero findings, silently. Fix: implement Tier-1 cross-function parameter-set comparison per file, OR error loudly when requested. (2) FeatureEnvy: listed Tier-1 (line 2766) but the Tier-1 path is a deprecated empty stub (lines 1901-1912); real detection only under --deep via callgraph (correct T2). --smell-type feature-envy WITHOUT --deep silently yields nothing. Fix: remove from should_run_original_detectors; loud 'requires --deep' message. Independent of TLDR-n74 (core detectors, not serving path).

title: Daemon-vs-local divergence CLASS: dead/weaker/unfiltered daemon answers across commands (calls, impact, ...; audit-driven checklist)
description:
Found during TLDR-uiy audit 3/64 (2026-06-05), Codex-assisted, code-verified. THREE defects, one path:
A) DEAD DAEMON PATH: DaemonCommand::Calls (tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/daemon.rs ~888) returns serde_json::to_value(ProjectCallGraph) = {"edges":[{src_file,src_func,dst_file,dst_func}]}. CLI (calls.rs:114) deserializes try_daemon_route::<CallGraphOutput> which REQUIRES root/total_edges/shown_edges + call_type per edge -> from_value fails -> .ok() -> None -> SILENT LOCAL FALLBACK EVERY TIME. The '~35x speedup' comment in calls.rs is false. Daemon route for calls has likely never worked since the schemas diverged.
B) V1-vs-V2 ORACLE SPLIT: daemon builds build_project_call_graph (V1); CLI local fallback builds build_project_call_graph_v2. Two different graph builders serve 'the same' command.
C) UNREADABLE WARM CACHE: warm step 1 (daemon.rs ~134, WarmJob) caches the V1 ProjectCallGraph under QueryKey 'calls' — an answer shape no CLI consumer can deserialize. We warm a cache nobody can read.
RESOLUTION: closed by the TLDR-n74 rewrite (CSR snapshot + bincode envelope + flag-parity); no interim patch (user-ratified — effort on a path scheduled for replacement). This bug is the ADR-10 EXHIBIT: silent fallback masked a permanently-dead path.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: EXTENDED TO CLASS ISSUE (user-ratified, audit 4/64). INSTANCE 2: impact — (a) ENRICHMENT PARITY: daemon arm (daemon.rs:910-944) uses plain impact_analysis; local (impact.rs:109-129) adds ast_fallback + enrich_impact_with_references; shared ImpactReport serde type -> deserialization SUCCEEDS -> silently WEAKER answers when daemon is up. (b) FLAG DROP: --file/--lang never forwarded (daemon_router.rs:312-319) -> filtered query returns UNFILTERED results presented as filtered. (c) CACHE KEY: QueryKey(func,depth,lang) lacks file (salsa.rs:85-95) -> collisions between filtered/unfiltered. (d) V1-on-both-paths while calls-local uses V2 -> sibling commands answer from different graphs. All closed by n74 rewrite + per-command daemon-vs-oneshot differential test.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 3: dead — the worst divergence kind yet: ALGORITHM FORK BY DAEMON STATE. (a) daemon Dead arm (daemon.rs:947-1002) always runs build_project_call_graph + dead_code_analysis (graph mode); local DEFAULT runs collect_module_infos_with_refcounts + dead_code_analysis_refcount -> same command answers from different ALGORITHMS depending on daemon liveness. (b) --call-graph and --no-default-ignore never forwarded (params_for_dead = path+entry only, daemon_router.rs:387-396); daemon cache key hashes root+entry only -> graph/refcount + filtered/unfiltered answers collide. (c) LOCAL-ONLY heuristics: use-server/client directive tagging, .d.ts exclusion, MAX_FILES=10_000 cap exist only in the CLI path (dead.rs:182-436) — daemon never applies them. (d) MAX_FILES cap is SILENT (eprintln only, no report marker) — at 100x one-shot dead analyzes 13% of the project. All closed by n74 + differential test.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 4: slice — SEVERITY: WORST YET (WRONG ANSWERS, not just thin/slow). (a) WRONG DIRECTION: router forwards file/function/line only (slice.rs:141-145); DaemonCommand::Slice has no direction/variable fields (types.rs:437-442); daemon arm HARDCODES SliceDirection::Backward + variable=None (daemon.rs:845-876) -> 'tldr slice -d forward' with live daemon returns a BACKWARD slice; header prints the daemon's 'backward' label, silently substituting the request. (b) CACHE: QueryKey lacks direction/variable dims -> wrong-direction results can persist from cache. (c) RICHNESS: daemon path deserializes LegacySliceOutput and reconstructs SliceLine with EMPTY definitions/uses/dep_type/edges (slice.rs:141-245) vs local get_slice_rich. (d) --variable silently ignored on daemon path. USER DECISION (2026-06-05): NO interim fix — wait for n74 (consistent with calls precedent); accepted risk: requires live daemon AND -d forward usage. n74 differential test must include direction and variable axes.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 5: search — NEW FLAVOR: capability exists, public command bypasses it. CLI search.rs has NO daemon route (always local enriched_search) while the real daemon HAS a Search arm (daemon.rs:635-651) consumed only via tldr daemon query (query.rs:29-41). Local path per query: FULL BM25 index rebuild (enriched.rs:901-925, O(corpus)) + with include_callgraph a FULL project call-graph build (enriched.rs:1278-1288) — the per-query-rebuild pattern ADR-9 kills. Closed by n74 (resident BM25 + CSR enrichment + routing).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 6: context — (a) --include-docstrings dropped from envelope AND cache key; daemon arm HARDCODES include_docstrings=true + file=None (daemon.rs:751-770, types.rs:421-429) -> docstring presence varies by daemon state (divergence in the OPPOSITE direction from usual: daemon gives MORE than asked). (b) LANGUAGE RESOLUTION divergence: CLI auto-detects from directory; daemon resolve_language defaults straight to Python -> same invocation, different language by daemon state. (c) get_relevant_context rebuilds WHOLE project graph per request (context/builder.rs:147-162). MITIGATING PRECEDENT: CLI already SKIPS daemon when --file/shorthand present with a comment naming the reason — the skip-rather-than-lie guard; first partial flag-parity respect found. Closed by n74.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 7: smells — DEAD PATH flavor (like calls): the REAL daemon has NO Smells command at all (verified: zero DaemonCommand::Smells matches) -> try_daemon_route('smells') always fails -> silent local fallback every time; '~35x speedup' comment false. The UNWIRED axum crate carries a PARTIAL Smells handler (threshold/smell_type/suggest fields accepted but CLI never sends them; smell_type parser covers 5/18 variants; --deep unreachable; no caching) — dead code masquerading as capability. params_for_smells (CLI side) forwards path/files/include_tests only — flag-parity latent defect if/when a real arm lands. deep_only_warning computed only after the daemon early-return (would be suppressed on daemon-served responses). Closed by n74.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 8 (minor): extract — daemon arm defaults unknown language to Python (daemon.rs:664-680: detect_or_parse_language(None,..).unwrap_or(Python)) while CLI local path propagates None -> UnsupportedLanguage error. Same language-resolution divergence axis as context (instance 6b). ALSO: extract memo key is PATH-hash + lang (content hash only as invalidation dep, salsa.rs:85-105) — superseded by ratified content-hash-keyed T1 contract. Otherwise extract is the CLEANEST current command: route-independent language resolution (P5.BUG-N1), sibling-aware widening, schema parity verified (ModuleInfo identical both paths).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 9 (moderate): imports — explicit --lang SILENTLY DISCARDED on routed path: CLI sends language via params_with_file_lang (imports.rs:62-66) but DaemonCommand::Imports{file} has NO language field (types.rs:482-483) — serde drops the param; daemon re-detects from extension only (daemon.rs:1041-1065). User passing --lang cpp for a .h file gets C parsing via daemon, C++ locally. NOTE: NO Python default here — daemon errors loudly on unknown extension (refuted suspicion; better than extract's arm). Cache: path+lang keyed, content hash as invalidation dep only (consistent with instance 8). NEW DEFECT FLAVOR: param sent but silently dropped at deserialization — argues for DENY-UNKNOWN-FIELDS on the daemon command parser in n74 envelope.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 10 (moderate): importers — --lang dropped on daemon route (CLI-side omission this time: DaemonCommand::Importers HAS an optional language field but params_with_module never sends it, daemon_router.rs:322-329) -> daemon resolve_language defaults Python while local auto-detects-then-Python. Both paths pay FULL PROJECT SCAN per query (find_importers walks the FS + reads matching files, analysis/importers.rs:28-108) — consistent but unindexed; target T2 reverse-import index fixes. Limit/total handling route-independent (GOOD).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 11: complexity — DEAD PATH flavor: REAL daemon has NO Complexity command (verified: zero matches in types.rs enum; Codex task log independently concluded same before hanging) -> try_daemon_route('complexity') always fails -> silent local fallback; '~35x speedup' comment false. --lang would also be dropped (params_with_file_function sends file+function only) if an arm ever lands — latent flag-parity defect. GOOD on local path: M28 shared validator + loud unknown-language error (detect_or_parse_language).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: INSTANCE 12 (latent): change-impact — DaemonCommand::ChangeImpact is DEAD IN PRODUCTION (only a test constructs it; CLI calls core directly). If ever activated as-wired it would diverge: envelope carries files/session/git but the arm IGNORES session+git and cannot express --base/--staged/--uncommitted (types.rs:501-509, daemon.rs:1112-1137). Disposition: the variant either gains the full detection-method envelope or dies (n74 host decision scope).

title: Token-lean -f compact rendering for graph answers — the primary agent surface (ADR-11)
description: Per ADR-11 (TLDR-kd6): -f compact is the most-used surface; design it deliberately for the product metric (TOKENS, not ms). Scope: define compact renderings for callers/callees/impact/dead/hubs/structure answers (columnar/TSV-style, no repeated keys); measure token counts vs -f json on real answers (target 2-4x reduction); make compact the documented default recommendation for MCP/agent paths. Rendering happens CLI-side on small answers (ADR-11 transport split) so this is purely an edge-format task — zero perf coupling. GATE: token measurements recorded per command; goldens added (mind the TLDR-wks lesson: schema/format drift must be tested deliberately).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE NOTE from TLDR-uiy audit 6/64: the per-function CFG/DFG cluster (reaching-defs, and likely taint/available/slice/cfg/dfg — verify per audit) emits IDENTICAL JSON for -f compact and -f json today (e.g. reaching_defs.rs:135). Compact-first means this whole cluster needs token-lean renderings designed, not just graph answers.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: NOTE from audit 8/64: compact means MINIFIED json in available.rs:135 but PRETTY json in reaching_defs.rs/taint.rs — the cluster has two compact behaviors today. 5kw defines ONE token-lean compact semantics for all commands; the QUERY-MODES contract (focused sub-answers) is the complementary token lever.

title: ADR-10 implementation: daemon-required CLI default, loud failure, explicit --oneshot
description: Per TLDR-14i: daemon_router health-checks the socket; absent/unhealthy -> non-zero exit with 'daemon not running — start with: tldr daemon start'. Silent local-build fallback REMOVED. Explicit one-shot surface added (--oneshot flag or subcommand — decide at impl). Update docs + CI recipes (start->warm->run->stop, or explicit one-shot). Lineage: TLDR-lx7 doctrine extended to the analysis flow.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE ADDITIONS (2026-06-05, from Codex design review F2+F6 on TLDR-5qm): (1) PER-COMMAND-CLASS ROUTING POLICY TABLE — the TLDR-uiy audit produces the classification (index-backed vs FS-truth per ADR-10 Amendment 1); this issue implements it as a central policy, NOT per-call-site edits. Current code is fallback-first by design (daemon_router.rs:28-131 returns None on ANY error -> silent local compute; calls.rs:113-192) — that pattern inverts here. (2) STRUCTURED daemon-missing error type, exit non-zero, message names 'tldr daemon start'. (3) --oneshot/--local explicit escape hatches. (4) VERSION HANDSHAKE enforced before routing any index-backed command (socket path already embeds version, server.rs:49-71, but reachable-incompatible daemons currently fail at deserialization); on mismatch, error names both versions + restart instruction. (5) Upgrade-aware messaging + first-run experience: daemon-not-running on a fresh checkout must read as onboarding, not breakage.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: SCOPE ADDITION from TLDR-uiy audit 3/64 (user-ratified): FLAG-PARITY RULE joins the routing policy — every behavior-affecting CLI flag is part of the request envelope; daemon never substitutes defaults for unforwarded flags. Acceptance: per-command differential test daemon-path vs --oneshot-path byte-identical answers (would have caught TLDR-7vb-A, the structure ignore divergence, and the language non-forwarding in one sweep).

title: Resident CSR snapshot + query primitives; retire whole-project answer blobs from the hot path
description: Implements ADR-9's structure: intern table, SoA node table, forward+reverse CSR, name/file/line indexes, ArcSwap snapshot. Wire the seven query primitives (out/in-neighbors, reverse BFS closure, predicate scans, SCC, by-name, by-file/line) and route callers/callees/impact/dead/hubs/cycles/definition/structure through the snapshot, returning SMALL per-answer serializations at the socket boundary. Whole-project JSON results survive only at persist (query_cache.bin) and explicit-export boundaries. Complexity contract per ADR-9 is the review rubric. MEASURED WARNING from the design session: the 10-30MB whole-project JSON serialization (50-150ms) would be the next hot path after A+B — its removal is the point. ACCEPTANCE: per-command output identical for the same queries (CLI-level golden diffs); query latencies recorded; snapshot rebuild time recorded (target single-digit ms at 26k edges).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ADR-11 AMENDMENT (TLDR-kd6): daemon->CLI transport for snapshot answers is BINARY (rkyv preferred — zero-copy view; bincode acceptable); -f json/jsonl/text/compact are CLI-side renderings of the received answer. Whole-graph dumps at the 100x design target make this mandatory: JSON ~850MB/5-8s vs rkyv view ~50-80MB/sub-second. Add to acceptance: transport format benchmark at synthetic 100x scale (generate or replicate corpus) OR at largest available real corpus, recorded.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: MEASUREMENT GATE ADDED (2026-06-05, user-ratified; Codex review F1 on TLDR-5qm): before the CSR snapshot implementation lands, a MINIMAL CSR-BUILD PROTOTYPE must be benchmarked on the frozen rust corpus (/tmp/tldr-bench@001fdcb = 745 files / 20,320 funcs / 26,312 edges) and CONFIRM the rebuild budget (single-digit ms at 26k edges, O(V+E) scaling). ADR-3 discipline: the complexity contract in ADR-9 is currently derived, not measured. Also fold in from same review: F4 generationed snapshot protocol (ADR-9 Amendment 1) and F3 bincode-first versioned envelope (ADR-11 Amendment 1) are now part of this issue's spec.

comments.author: Udhayakumar

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: MEASUREMENT GATE now has its own research issue: TLDR-rfz (blocks this issue). The gate from comment 1 (CSR prototype) + audit 10 (resident BM25) is discharged there BEFORE implementation starts — n74 must not begin until TLDR-rfz records a CONFIRMED/REVISED verdict. Per-name postings (comment 24) explicitly out-of-gate unless triggered.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
SPEC ADDITION (2026-06-06, user-ratified; Codex-reviewed on TLDR-5qm): CONTENT-ADDRESSED CACHE SEGMENT MODEL — the cross-command caching contract (goals: warm once / reuse across CLI flows; single-file edit invalidates the minimal segment).
UNIFYING RULE: every cached artifact declares its exact input slice; key = blake3 of that slice + (schema, analyzer, grammar) versions; invalidation = key mismatch, NEVER explicit flush.
S1 parse: (file content hash, grammar ver) -> tree.
S2 per-FUNCTION facts: key=(body hash, FILE SEMANTIC-ENV HASH [imports + enclosing-class chain], lang, analyzer ver) -> CFG/DFG/SSA bundle (POSITION-RELATIVE storage; identity + absolute position live ONLY in the generation node table), metric row, token fingerprint, embedding. Body edits keep sibling functions warm (49/50 property); import edits invalidate the file honestly; NO identity inside cached values (Codex F1).
S3 compose-inputs per file: the CLOSED SET of what compose consumes — signatures, call-sites, imports, AND receiver-index/local-type facts. Codex F5 confirms the ADR-9 'fold receiver indexes into FileIR' move is LOAD-BEARING: today's compose (apply_type_resolution, receiver indexes) scans raw source. Each sub-fact independently hashed.
S4 CSR snapshot: O(V+E) rebuild on any compose-input change (rebuild-don't-surgery stands). FuncId assignment DETERMINISTIC FROM IDENTITY ORDERING (file, qualified name, ordinal) — never from positions.
S5 derived artifacts (pagerank/betweenness/SCC/cycles/layers <- topology slice = identity table + CSR bytes, positions EXCLUDED; coupling/cohesion <- module-edge hash; inheritance closure <- type-edge hash; clones <- fingerprint multiset, incremental re-compare; health/debt aggregates <- monoid fold over S2 rows): slice hashes are REUSE TICKETS, but lookup ALWAYS goes through a PINNED GENERATION — generation = consistency token (epoch mixing unexpressible, Codex F4), slice hash = reuse ticket (body-only edits keep pagerank warm across rebuilds). REPLACES 'first query per generation pays' with 'first query per input-slice-change pays'.
S6 search: BM25 per-file delta postings + S2 embeddings, content-keyed.
S7 external truth: (git HEAD, tool versions, coverage hash) keys, local-only, never daemon-resident.
T3: S2 persists as an on-disk content-addressed store -> warm restarts. GC = mark-and-sweep from the current file set (live = body hashes referenced by current parses); RAM tier = per-segment byte-budget LRU (CAS + LRU alone grows forever, Codex F6).
ARBITRATION: Codex's secondary equality check on slice-hash reuse DROPPED (user-ratified) — blake3-256 collision risk negligible; the rec targeted weak/rolling hashes.
MEASUREMENT: edit-path hash fan-out added to the TLDR-rfz gate (Codex F7).
RETIRES/REUSES (Codex F8): daemon per-language OnceCell buckets retire (with Am.2 language-unified); S2 persistence reuses the VectorStore content-keyed pattern; embeddings already content-keyed (t8f).
BONUS: makes the TLDR-jsi bug class unexpressible — cache clear = drop CAS; content-addressed keys cannot serve stale-wrong data.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: EPIC FILED for the cache segment model: TLDR-17d (parent: 7pp) with children TLDR-rem (S2 per-function CAS), TLDR-726 (S3 compose-input fold), TLDR-o2w (S4/S5 reuse tickets — blocked by this issue + rem), TLDR-94k (S6 search segments), TLDR-mtq (T3 persistence/GC — closes jsi class). All implementation children gated behind TLDR-rfz. The 2026-06-06 spec comment here remains the authoritative contract text.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
Udhayakumar
comments.text:
LAYER TAXONOMY AMENDMENTS (2026-06-06, post-Codex review on TLDR-5qm; supplements the taxonomy comment above):
A1 (F4): L2 grains NAMED — L2a function-grain (S2: bundles, metric rows, fingerprints; key body+env hash) and L2b file-grain (S3: compose-input facts; key file hash). ONE layer, two grains. RULE CLARIFIED: layer membership = key/lifetime class (content-derived, project-blind), NOT downstream effect.
A2 (F5): EMBEDDINGS move to L2-ASYNC sub-lane — same content-addressed keys + MODEL-VERSION key dimension, but refresh is DECOUPLED from the synchronous edit path (ONNX inference ~100ms+/function must never block structural-fact freshness or queries). Queries serve last-good embedding WITH staleness provenance. Aligns with the resident-Embedder IndexManager design already in-tree.
A3 (F6a): whole-project aggregates (health/debt/project-stats) are NOT L4 reuse-ticket artifacts — any edit changes their slice and hashing the fold input is itself O(V). They are recomputed per generation as an O(V) monoid fold over warm L2a metric rows (us-ms at 20k functions). L4 tickets apply only to artifacts whose slice can survive edits (topology/module-edge/type-edge/fingerprint-multiset keyed).
A4 (F6b): the EXISTING QueryCache (tldr-cli/src/commands/daemon/salsa.rs) is hereby assigned a fate: RETIRED. It memoizes materialized answers — the answer-blob sin ADR-9 kills. Its consumers route to L3/L4. No taxonomy slot for it is added; its absence from the layer stack is a decision, not an omission.
A5 (F1): B2 RESTATED honestly — edit above a function: zero invalidation; edit inside a body: that one function's L2a facts recompute (cheap) + async embedding refresh (A2); NO L3 rebuild (compose-input hashes unchanged). Comment-stripped normalized body hashing PARKED as a future measured lever (complicates position-correct rendering).
A6 (F2): B1 scoped — the ~3s blunt-clear repayment claim applies to the GRAPH path (DaemonState language buckets); the semantic vector store already has per-file delta warmth (t8f) which the model formalizes rather than introduces.
A7 (F7): side lane reworded — external-truth ANSWERS (git HEAD/tool/env-scoped: churn, diagnostics, coverage, doctor, fix) are never served from daemon caches; daemon operational state (counters, liveness) is outside taxonomy scope.
REAFFIRMED AGAINST CODEX (F3 rejected with evidence): B3 STANDS — the real public search path builds BM25 inline per invocation (enriched.rs:925,986 Bm25Index::from_project) AND builds the full call graph per invocation (enriched.rs:1593); CLI search has no daemon routing; in-repo test comment (enriched.rs:2214) confirms 'from_project() rebuilds every time'. Codex cited the UNWIRED tldr-daemon axum crate — the two-daemons trap, THIRD STRIKE. The host-decision-first rule in this spec exists for exactly this reason.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
AMENDMENTS A8-A11 (2026-06-06, Codex rounds 2+3 on TLDR-5qm, full convergence; user-directed course-correction loop):
A8 ASYNC-EMBEDDING CONSISTENCY CONTRACT: semantic/search results from the L2-async embedding lane are JOIN-VERIFIED against the pinned generation before rendering — hits whose function key is absent from the current node table are DROPPED; positions rebased from the node table; the response carries an embedding-staleness field (embedding-lane generation vs snapshot generation). Guarantee: stale embeddings may mis-rank, can NEVER surface deleted/renamed symbols.
A9 ENV-HASH SCOPE: the L2a file semantic-env hash covers DIRECT imports of the file + the enclosing-class chain ONLY — never transitive module metadata. Transitive effects flow through compose/L3, not L2 keys. Blast-radius measurement added to TLDR-rfz (what fraction of real edits touch import lists; functions churned per such edit).
A10 L4 TICKET GRANULARITY: ship the 4 coarse slices (topology / module-edge / type-edge / fingerprint-multiset); refine granularity from post-implementation hit-rate telemetry, never speculation.
A11 CLOSABILITY = CAPTURE, NOT SOLUTION (round-3 convergence): the L2b closed compose-input set captures exactly the inputs today's resolver reads — Codex-verified per-file-only (resolution.rs L258-301; type_resolver.rs L426-612 Python, L828-1005 TS; no cross-file/global inputs). The frozen-corpus byte-hash gates are the MECHANICAL CLOSURE PROOF: under-capture diverges output and fails the gate. Existing fallback/widening (unresolved-receiver) semantics are DOCUMENTED in the spec, preserved byte-identically — not redesigned.
F3 CLOSURE: Codex round-2 CONCEDED B3 after reading the real path (search.rs no daemon hop; from_project inline x2; build_project_call_graph per invocation). Trap strike 3 confirmed by the reviewer itself; host-decision-first rule re-validated.
ROUND-3 VERDICT (Codex): research plan COMPLETE pending the measurement gate only.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
TWO-FLOW OVERLAP MAP (2026-06-06, recorded because the question recurs; code-verified): Flow 1 (structural: parse->FileIR->compose->CSR) and Flow 2 (semantic: chunk->enrich->embed->vector store) SHARE their foundation and today PAY IT TWICE.
CODE FACTS: (1) Flow 2's chunker parses with the SAME tree-sitter entry as Flow 1 (chunker.rs:222 parse_file from ast::parser) — every file parsed twice today. (2) Flow 2 CONSUMES Flow 1 outputs: embedding text includes signature+callees (enrichment EmbeddingUnit); enriched search builds the full call graph per query for context cards (the B3 kill).
SHARED STAGES (optimizing helps BOTH): file enumeration + ignore rules (today two divergent walkers — bpf cluster); tree-sitter parse (L1, biggest shared win); function identity/spans/content-hash (today FuncDef+blake3-target vs ChunkMeta+MD5 — unify); per-function fact registry (L2: CFG bundles + metric rows + fingerprints + BM25 docs + embeddings on ONE (body hash, env hash) key — a 'chunk' IS a function); watcher + delta invalidation (one trigger pipeline).
EXCLUSIVE STAGES (optimizing helps ONE lane): CSR + graph walks = Flow 1 only; ONNX inference + vector index = Flow 2 only (e.g. 3rh arena tuning protects shared daemon headroom but does not speed Flow 1).
DIRECTION OF BENEFIT: Flow 1 substrate -> Flow 2 queries (resident snapshot turns per-query graph builds into O(deg); shared L1 halves Flow-2 indexing). Flow 2 -> Flow 1 = PATTERNS not speed (t8f delta machinery, IndexManager resident-store/RwLock, VectorStore CAS pattern reused by T3/mtq). Flow 2 pioneered; Flow 1 industrializes.
CONSEQUENCE FOR THE MODEL: after n74/17d the 'two flows' become TWO LANES ABOVE ONE SHARED L0-L2 FOUNDATION, diverging only at L3 (CSR vs vector index). 'Will building Flow 2 make Flow 1 faster?' — NO, the arrow points the other way; 'does AST/chunk-index help both?' — YES, they ARE the shared layers.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: A12 (2026-06-06, user-ratified): EMBEDDING KEY IS BODY-LOCAL — key = (body hash, signature hash, model ver). Resolved-callee context is NEVER baked into embedding text; structural context (callers/callees) joins AT QUERY TIME from the pinned generation (O(deg) from L3) and fuses via RRF with dense + BM25 signals (rides utj.3). SUPERSEDES the earlier enrichment-slice-hash note: that note correctly identified the staleness hazard but solved it in the wrong direction (honest key over volatile inputs -> frequent invalidation); A12 removes the volatile input from the artifact instead. CONSEQUENCES: (1) a callee rename re-embeds ZERO functions — only a function's own body edit re-embeds it; (2) query-time context is FRESHER than baked-in (current at query moment vs stale-between-re-embeds); (3) deployed baseline already runs enrich OFF (memory 00) so this costs nothing today; (4) GATE: any future baked-in enrichment proposal must BEAT query-time fusion in the eval harness before earning a place in the embedding key. Design principle extracted: never bake volatile cross-file data into the most expensive immutable artifact — serve volatility from the always-fresh cheap structure.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: REJECTED ALTERNATIVE recorded (2026-06-06, user-proposed for due diligence, jointly rejected): REIFIED CACHE-KEY DEPENDENCY GRAPH (Salsa/Bazel/Incremental-style stored edges + push invalidation). Rejected for THIS system's shape: (1) derivation DAG is STATIC and shallow (L1->L2->L3->L4, ~10 edge types known at design time) — storing per-key edge instances records what we already know; (2) A12 deleted the only volatile many-to-many edge (embedding<-resolved-callees) — remaining edges are 1:1 (function->own facts), one flat index (file->function keys), or N:1 (->snapshot, rebuilt wholesale per ADR-9 rebuild-don't-surgery); (3) stored edges are mutable state with their own staleness — a missed edge update = silently-wrong hit, re-importing the bug class content-addressing makes unexpressible (the retired QueryCache.invalidate_by_input was a crude instance). WHAT SURVIVES of the idea: file->function-keys index (async embed queue + GC mark phase) and the pinned generation as the single L3/L4 consistency edge. REVISIT TRIGGER: if the derivation DAG ever becomes DYNAMIC (analyses reading arbitrary other functions' facts at runtime), recorded dependencies become correct — record this as the explicit condition, not dogma.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DESIGN DOC PUBLISHED (2026-06-06): docs/CACHE_LAYERS_DESIGN.md — the consolidation-grade document for the layered cache architecture: contract, layer rule, L0-L5+side-lane stack with diagram, key design points (A-series), invalidation cascade, command->layer benefit map, two-flow overlap, retired machinery with fates, rejected alternatives with revisit triggers, and the rfz gate. docs/CACHE_ARCHITECTURE.md (current system) banner-linked with QueryCache retirement note. This doc is the seed of the final spec consolidation; n74 comments remain the authoritative amendment trail.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: FAST-LANE STRATEGY RATIFIED (2026-06-06, user): precision fixes proceed AHEAD of the gated rewrite — rfz gate, 3rh arenas, Levers A+B (zro/iqr), 7vb quick wrong-answer subset, d3s/yll observability, lkx decision. The CSR/17d rewrite remains gated on rfz. RERANKER promoted to its own epic (TLDR-utj.2 + 3 children): the search-QUALITY lever the cache architecture does not touch; orthogonal surface, may proceed during research phase.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: rfz GATE CROSS-POST (2026-06-06): measurement complete, PROPOSED verdict REVISED (user arbitration pending — n74 stays blocked). Key numbers: 1× rebuild p50 3.23ms (budget HOLDS, incl. ArcSwap publish); queries all hold with margin (callers 16ns, impact 1.8ns/touched, dead 5.4µs); 100× rebuild 512ms + snapshot 570–920MB (ADR-9's tens-of-ms/~200MB projections EXCEEDED — amendment needed); A9 warm fraction 0.448 vs 0.98 at commit granularity (env-key re-scope decision needed). BM25 REBUILD-VS-INCREMENTAL DECISION (per gate acceptance): INCREMENTAL postings update as default seam — measured p50 1.27ms / max 7.81ms per file vs 250ms full rebuild (sample-scoped; production must use delta-postings per 94k, prototype Vec::remove constant does not transfer); rebuild seam stays reserved per audit 10. Full data: bench/rfz-csr-gate branch, bench/rfz-gate/RESULTS.md.

title: Benchmark baselines post-ADR-8 (harness: scripts/bench_phase_timing.sh)
description:
Repeatable benchmark suite run 2026-06-05 (round-5 binary, Apple Silicon, harness committed at scripts/bench_phase_timing.sh). These are the REGRESSION REFERENCE numbers for the TLDR-olg fix work and any future perf change.

## E1 — Phase-timing baselines (median of N runs; spread <=1.5% everywhere)
lang       corpus     files/funcs/edges        parse    COMPOSE   total    ms/edge
rust       tldr-code  745/20,328/26,327 (N=10) 2551ms   556ms     3116ms   0.021
python     flask      83/1,460/1,183    (N=5)  21ms     241ms     265ms    0.20
python     django     2,917/32,308/43,039(N=5) 499ms    23,680ms  24,278ms 0.55
typescript zod        405/1,905/2,311   (N=5)  130ms    4,709ms   4,833ms  2.04
typescript nest       1,724/3,679/4,582 (N=5)  150ms    1,909ms   2,094ms  0.42
go         caddy      314/2,571/3,585   (N=5)  97ms     1,923ms   2,026ms  0.54
java       junit5     1,724/14,856/12,409(N=5) 231ms    1,181ms   1,444ms  0.095

## E2 — Scaling curve (django alphabetical-prefix subsets, N=3)
730f/5,795e: compose 1,409ms (0.24ms/e) | 1,459f/17,583e: 10,466ms (0.60ms/e) | 2,917f/43,039e: 23,680ms (0.55ms/e)
FINDING: per-edge cost flat at scale -> defect is LINEAR project-level, QUADRATIC per-file (call_sites_f x lines_f), matching the whole-file-scan profile. Big files (zod-style) are the worst case, not big projects. The per-file index fix turns calls_f x lines_f into calls_f + lines_f.

## E3 — Peak RSS, single CLI build
rust 256MB | django py 395MB | zod ts 57MB | caddy go 50MB | junit5 java 208MB
(All sub-400MB: the time defect does NOT have a memory analogue in CLI builds.)

## E4 — Determinism (3-run sha256 of full edge JSON)
rust: identical x3. python/django: identical x3.

## E5 — Daemon warm cycle x3 (restart + warm)
steady: 12-13s / ~550MB peak. Cycle-1 outlier 41s/969MB = 1-file embed delta (the new bench script) -> even a single-file ONNX embed costs +400MB peak: independent re-confirmation that embed owns the daemon's memory ceiling (TLDR-3rh).

## Re-run protocol
scripts/bench_phase_timing.sh <corpus> <lang> [N]  (TLDR_BIN env to point at a candidate binary)
Corpora: /tmp/repos/{flask@36e4a82,django@8c2d3dc,zod@bbc68f9,nest@5e80f30,caddy,junit5}; rust frozen corpus /tmp/tldr-bench@001fdcb.
Acceptance for TLDR-olg fixes: counts byte-identical per corpus, output hashes stable, compose reduction reported against THESE medians.
close_reason: Baselines captured 2026-06-05; harness committed; numbers serve as regression reference for TLDR-olg fix work

title: Python/TS/generic resolvers: same per-call-site-scan defect class as Gate-1 rounds 1-5 (unmeasured; ADR-6 decision input)
description:
The Gate-1 campaign (compose 223s -> 0.56s on rust) fixed SEVEN defects of one class: per-call-site linear work that should be indexed lookups. The rust fixes were: find_by_name secondary index, RustReceiverIndex (per-file binding/impl index, then fully inverted incl. weird receivers), find_by_name routing in resolve_local_fuzzy_match / resolve_type_aware_fallback.

The NON-rust paths still run the legacy shape: resolve_receiver_type (resolution.rs) dispatches per call site to per-language scanners (python/TS/JS/Go/Java/... in type_resolver.rs) that re-scan source text per call — the exact pattern rounds 2-4 killed for rust. Generic paths fixed in round 5 (local_fuzzy/type_aware_fallback) already benefit all languages.

SCOPE: (1) MEASURE first (ADR-3 rule — no fixing before profiling): TLDR_PHASE_TIMING=1 build on a large python and a large TS corpus; (2) if compose dominates, apply the inverted-index treatment per language with the same layered differential gates (ADR-5: exhaustive synthetic + corpus-level value identity + count identity + determinism).

WHY IT MATTERS: one of exactly two missing inputs to the Path A/B call in ADR-6 (TLDR-7mp) — per-language compose economics may differ materially from rust's.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
MEASUREMENT HALF COMPLETE (2026-06-05; ADR-3 honored — measured before fixing). H-lang CONFIRMED with named hot path.

PHASE SPLITS (round-5 binary, --respect-ignore, shallow clones @ /tmp/repos):
- flask   py  83f / 1,460fn / 1,183e:  parse 20ms  / compose 241ms  (91%)  0.20ms/edge
- django  py  2,917f / 32,308fn / 43,039e: parse 465ms / COMPOSE 23.3s (97.6%) 0.54ms/edge
- zod     ts  405f / 1,905fn / 2,311e:  parse 115ms / COMPOSE 4.7s  (97.5%) 2.0ms/edge
- nest    ts  1,724f / 3,679fn / 4,582e: parse 155ms / compose 1.9s  (91%)  0.41ms/edge
- (reference) rust/tldr-code FIXED: 0.02ms/edge; BROKEN baseline was 8.5ms/edge.

PROFILE (django, 10s sample mid-compose): 96% of build_project_call_graph_v2 time in apply_type_resolution -> resolve_receiver_type (the per-call-site dispatcher, type_resolver.rs) -> str::find/StrSearcher::new + find_var_in_line — per-call-site WHOLE-FILE text scans. This is the EXACT pre-round-2 rust defect in the python path. Compose single-threaded as before.

NOTE the zod-vs-nest inversion (4x fewer files, 2.5x slower): cost scales with file_size x call_sites per file, not file count — zod's giant generated-style .ts files are the worst case. Confirms the per-call-site x per-file-scan shape.

VERDICT: same defect class, same fix shape — per-file receiver/binding index (the RustReceiverIndex pattern, generalized per language), answered from one inverted pass; plus the round-5 generic-path fixes already benefit all languages (the resolve_local_fuzzy_match/type_aware_fallback wins are language-agnostic and already shipped).

REMAINING SCOPE (the fix half): generalize the per-file index for python/TS/JS (+ audit go/java/etc. scanners for the same shape), under ADR-5 gates (exhaustive synthetic differential per language + corpus value-identity on real call sites + count identity on these frozen clones + determinism). Frozen corpora for count gates: the four /tmp/repos clones at todays HEADs (record SHAs before fixing).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: ADDITIONAL NUMBERS (2026-06-05): corpus SHAs for frozen count gates: flask@36e4a82, django@8c2d3dc, zod@bbc68f9, nest@5e80f30, caddy + junit5 cloned same day. GO (caddy, 314f/2,571fn/3,585e): parse 96ms / COMPOSE 1.9s (95%) = 0.53ms/edge — defect present. JAVA (junit5, 1,724f/14,856fn/12,409e): parse 224ms / compose 1.1s (82%) = 0.09ms/edge — MILD (java scanner does less per-call work; lower priority). ZOD TS PROFILE: 100% of compose samples in resolve_receiver_type self-time (inline per-call-site line scans) — TS scanner confirmed same defect, third language named. FIX PRIORITY by measured pain: python > TS > go > java.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
PYTHON FIX LANDED (commit follows the measured plan; advisor-shaped: memoize-first, NO substring-trie inversion — proportionality target was 3-5s, achieved 2.8s):

PythonReceiverIndex (type_resolver.rs): per-file line table + enclosing-class snapshot built once; per-DISTINCT-receiver memoized annotation/constructor tables (colon/equals prefilters); per-call-site partition_point. Asymmetric cache semantics replicated from legacy: annotation ABORTS at nearest match (failures cached), constructor CONTINUES (successes only).

MEASURED vs TLDR-hqb baselines (same harness, same corpora):
- django: compose 23,680ms -> 2,848ms (8.3x), total 24,278 -> 3,456ms (7.0x). ms/edge 0.55 -> 0.066.
- flask: compose 241 -> 43ms (5.6x).

GATES (ADR-5): django edge JSON BYTE-IDENTICAL to frozen pre-fix reference (9dacc6147f4f3f4c, 43,039 edges — strongest gate of the campaign: real 2,917-file corpus, pre/post hash match); counts identical django+flask; rust frozen corpus unaffected (745/20,320/26,312); primitive-level exhaustive synthetic differential (annotation/constructor/enclosing + resolve, incl line-0/beyond-EOF/substring-traps/walrus/Union); in-repo corpus gate (26 real sites); 1,056 callgraph tests green; pre-existing failures unchanged (TLDR-6q2 families).

REMAINING SCOPE: typescript (zod 4.7s baseline — next priority), go (caddy 1.9s), java mild (1.2s, may not be worth it). Same recipe: per-file index, primitives swapped, wrapper verbatim, frozen-corpus hash gates (zod/nest/caddy hashes should be captured BEFORE fixing — flask/django pattern).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
TYPESCRIPT/JS FIX LANDED (same recipe as python; codex external review was cancelled mid-flight by user — internal gates carried the verification):

TypeScriptReceiverIndex: per-file line table + brace-depth enclosing-class snapshot; 4-prefix annotation/constructor scans memoized per distinct receiver with ': ' / ' = new ' line prefilters. Serves TS AND JS (one legacy dispatcher route). Quirk differences from python replicated and gate-tested: failed extraction CONTINUES (successes-only caches, both scans); NO EOF fallback in the class walk.

MEASURED vs TLDR-hqb baselines:
- zod: compose 4,709ms -> 224ms (21x — the big-file worst case is gone), total 4,833 -> 383ms. ms/edge 2.04 -> 0.097.
- nest: compose 1,909 -> 381ms (5.0x), total 2,094 -> 569ms.

GATES: zod AND nest edge JSON byte-identical to frozen pre-fix hashes (1e1b1426bfa5893c / a1edb472900b0072); rust frozen corpus counts + django hash unchanged (no cross-language regression); exhaustive synthetic differential (4-prefix priority, fallthrough, brace quirks, line-0/EOF) + ts/js fixtures corpus gate; 1,058 callgraph tests green.

SCOREBOARD (compose, medians): rust 223s->0.56s (400x) | django py 23.7s->2.85s (8.3x) | zod ts 4.7s->0.22s (21x) | nest ts 1.9s->0.38s (5x). REMAINING: go (caddy 1.9s baseline, hash frozen d1cfea4c4d30c206, same recipe ready) and java (1.2s, mild — likely not worth it). After go: every measured language is parse-dominated and TLDR-olg can close.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Go fix remains the last open item here (frozen hash d1cfea4c4d30c206, recipe proven). NOTE from ADR-9/lever B (TLDR-iqr): receiver indexes will FOLD INTO FileIR as parse-time artifacts — implement the go index in that shape if TLDR-iqr lands first; otherwise same bolt-on shape as rust/py/ts and migrate with B.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: LINKAGE from TLDR-uiy audit 4/64 (user-ratified): the per-query reference-scan enrichment (enrich_impact_with_references, P13.AGG13-4 — query-time source-text scanning, banned by ADR-9) survives ONLY as a transitional marked oracle. Each language resolver this issue fixes at compose time KILLS that language's enrichment dependency. olg completion = enrichment fully dead = query path never touches source text. Acceptance test idea: impact answers with enrichment OFF must match enrichment ON for fixed languages.

title: ADR-7: Memory attribution correction — headline peak is EMBED phase; chunk store does not address it
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-05) Stop justifying the chunk store with the headline memory number. The measured 27.0GB peak belongs to the EMBED phase (Flow-2: ONNX inference + accumulate-then-save). Memory work routes accordingly: TLDR-3rh/TLDR-yll own the embed peak; the F-series surgical fixes (F1/TLDR-9ae shipped; F2-F4/TLDR-1in pending) own the callgraph-phase transient.

## Rationale (correction owned)
We carried "the 22GB is the callgraph cache; the chunk store kills it" forward in the benefits statement. Our own measurements say otherwise:
- The callgraph-phase plateau was 11.4GB PRE-F1 (with the to_value DOM); F1 is shipped and the post-F1 number is unmeasured.
- The true daemon peak = max(phase peaks) = the EMBED phase, untouched by any chunk-store design.
- 27.0GB vs the historical 22.7GB is partly BETTER MEASUREMENT, not worse behavior: 27.0 came from the getrusage high-water probe (true max over 4.5h), 22.7 from 15s ps polling (under-samples peaks by construction). Provenance: the overnight diff touched only callgraph/* — semantic/ ran byte-identical to main.

## Alternatives Considered
- Keep quoting the headline number for the chunk store: rejected — it assigns another subsystem's peak to this design and would make Path B look better than it is.

## Status: ACTIVE.

title: ADR-6: Gate-1 PASS — recompose-always stands; chunk-store time case reduced to ~2.7s; Path A/B deferred to two measurements
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-05, supersedes ADR-1's justification) Gate-1 PASSES: compose = 0.56s, under the 2s recompose-per-edit line with margin. Recompose-always v1 stands AS DESIGNED — no escalation needed. HOWEVER the chunk store's TIME justification is re-derived honestly: a full rebuild is now ~3.1s (parse 2.7s + compose 0.56s), so chunk persistence buys ONLY the ~2.7s parse memoization. The Path A (surgical fixes, no storage layer) vs Path B (full two-flow chunk store) decision is DEFERRED until two missing measurements exist:
1. Post-F1 warm memory peak (callgraph-phase transient after the to_value DOM removal) — ~22s measurement, needs daemon authorization.
2. Python/TS resolver compose numbers — same per-call-site-scan defect class, unmeasured; per-language economics may differ materially.

## Rationale
The problem ADR-1 designed for (220s+ rebuilds, minutes-long restarts) largely DISSOLVED under measurement and defect fixes. What Path B uniquely retains regardless: cross-process/CI chunk sharing (~parse only), mmap-evictable steady state, restart without parse. Whether that justifies weeks of storage work is a memory question now, not a time question.

## Alternatives Considered
- Proceed with Path B on the original benefits table: rejected — the table conflated defect costs with design value ("restart 3.5min -> seconds" was mostly the defects).
- Kill Path B now: rejected — memory + multi-language numbers could still justify it; deferring costs nothing.

## Status: ACTIVE — the current architectural position.
close_reason: Superseded by TLDR-asv (ADR-8): Path A ratified by user

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: DECISION INPUT 1 LANDED (TLDR-myd, 2026-06-05): post-F1 warm peak = 11.1GB resident vs 11.4 pre-F1 — F1 did NOT take the callgraph transient (H-mem falsified; plateau is AST/FileIR/index retention, flat-then-cliff signature unchanged). EFFECT ON THE FORK: Path A's memory win now DEPENDS on F2-F4 (TLDR-1in, AST streaming + index dedup + interning) actually delivering — it can no longer be assumed largely-done via F1. Path B's streaming parse->chunk->drop-AST targets exactly the measured plateau, so B gains relative ground on the memory axis (time axis unchanged: ~2.7s). Confirmed wins regardless: warm phase-1 3.5min -> ~110s, settled idle 1.4GB -> 0.8GB. REMAINING INPUT: TLDR-olg (python/TS resolver numbers). Supporting measurements that would sharpen the call: TLDR-335 (size the plateau components), TLDR-d3s (phys_footprint probe — F1's true effect on the COMPRESSED peak is still invisible).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
DECISION INPUT 2 LANDED (TLDR-olg measurement half, 2026-06-05): python/TS compose IS dominated by the same defect class — django 23.3s compose (97.6% of build), zod 4.7s (97.5%); profile names resolve_receiver_type's per-call-site whole-file scans (pre-round-2 rust defect, python path). Fix shape established and de-risked by the rust campaign.

BOTH INPUTS NOW EXIST. PATH A/B DECISION PACKAGE (the call is the user's):
- TIME axis: resolved for rust; py/TS fixable with the established per-file-index pattern (high confidence). Post-fix, all languages likely parse-dominated, full builds in seconds. Chunk store buys ~parse-time only (~2.7s on rust; less elsewhere). TIME NO LONGER DECIDES THE FORK.
- MEMORY axis (the live axis): warm transient ~11GB = retained ASTs/FileIRs/indices (TLDR-myd). Path A needs F2-F4 (TLDR-1in: stream-and-drop ASTs, dedup indices, intern strings) to deliver this surgically. Path B gets it structurally (parse -> chunk -> drop-AST) plus restart-without-parse, cross-process sharing, mmap-evictable steady state — at weeks-scale storage-layer cost.
- SEQUENCING NOTE: the py/TS index fixes are a PURE WIN under either path (no storage dependency) — they are the next implementation phase regardless. The A/B call can wait for F2's feasibility signal (if F2 AST-streaming proves easy, A suffices; if it fights the builder's architecture, B's streaming becomes the cleaner home — and TLDR-335's attribution sizes the prize either way).

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text:
DECISION INPUT 1 — CORRECTED (supersedes this morning's reading; full evidence on TLDR-myd): the callgraph warm transient is ELIMINATED, not persistent. Controlled re-measurement: full warm rebuild = 549MB peak / ~15s (was 11.4GB / 3.5min pre-fix, ~20x). The morning 11.1GB was the semantic delta re-embed (ONNX), mis-attributed.

EFFECT ON THE FORK — this flips the memory axis BACK, decisively toward PATH A:
- Path A no longer needs F2-F4 to deliver the warm-memory win: it is ALREADY DELIVERED by F1 + the Gate-1 rounds. TLDR-1in and TLDR-335 deprioritize (their GB-scale target no longer exists).
- Path B's memory case ~evaporates: the only remaining GB-scale memory problem is the EMBED phase (TLDR-3rh/yll), which no chunk store touches (ADR-7, now triple-confirmed).
- Restart story: rebuild-on-restart is ~15s/549MB; a query_cache.bin disk persist layer additionally exists. The original 'restart = 3.5min + 22GB' premise is fully dissolved.

SCORECARD with both inputs final: TIME -> Path A (rebuilds are seconds; chunks save ~2.7s parse). MEMORY -> Path A (warm transient gone; embed is out of scope for B). REMAINING Path-B-unique value: per-file invalidation granularity, cross-process chunk sharing, mmap-evictable steady state — none currently load-bearing. RECOMMENDATION: Path A. Concretely: ship the py/TS resolver index fixes (TLDR-olg fix half), keep the existing persist layer, park Path B (TLDR-zde storage design) as ADR'd-but-dormant unless a new measured pain re-opens it. ADR-8 (the formal A/B decision record superseding/refining ADR-6) awaits user ratification.

comments.author: Udhayakumar
comments.text: Superseded by TLDR-asv (ADR-8): user ratified Path A 2026-06-05 — both decision inputs measured, scorecard unanimous.

title: ADR-5: Output-identity discipline — perf fixes proven by layered differential gates, never by 'tests pass'
description:
## Decision
(overnight 2026-06-05, formalized in round 5) Every performance fix to resolution logic must be OUTPUT-IDENTICAL and PROVEN so, by layered gates: (1) exhaustive synthetic differential vs the legacy implementation, (2) frozen-corpus count identity (745/20,320/26,312 across all rounds), (3) corpus-level VALUE identity on real call-site receivers (round-5 closer, advisor-prompted: counts cannot catch same-count-different-target divergence), (4) run-to-run determinism of the full 8.4MB edge output.

## Rationale
A 400x speedup that silently changes which edges resolve is a correctness regression wearing a perf medal. The legacy implementation stays in-tree as the reference oracle.

## Alternatives Considered
- "Counts match + suite green" alone: rejected after advisor review — it leaves exactly one divergence mode open (same count, different resolved type).

## Bonus outcome
The discipline also REMOVED pre-existing nondeterminism: the old fuzzy-match paths picked among candidates in HashMap iteration order (random per process); the indexed rewrite is deterministic (TLDR-2af direction).

## Status: ACTIVE.

title: ADR-4: Treat Gate-1 FAIL (223s) as suspected algorithmic defect — profile/fix loop before architecture escalation
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-04 night) On Gate-1 failing at compose=223s (110x over the 2s line), do NOT escalate to heavier architecture (query-time resolution / incremental compose / Glean-style ownership sets). Instead run profile -> name hot path -> output-identical fix -> differential gate -> re-bench loops on the EXISTING pipeline.

## Rationale
223s / 26,312 edges = ~8.5ms per edge — pathological vs comparable tools (ty type-checks all of home-assistant in 5.3s; rust-analyzer resolves workspaces in seconds). That smelled like implementation defects, not inherent cost. Profiling is cheap; escalation stays available if fixes stall.

## Alternatives Considered
- Escalate immediately per the pre-planned ladder: rejected — would have built complex machinery on top of unmeasured defects, repeating ADR-1's mistake at a higher level.

## Outcome (VINDICATED — 400x)
5 rounds, 7 defects, ALL the same class (per-call-site linear scans that should be indexed lookups; all present since initial release): compose 223s -> 0.56s; build 226s -> 3.1s. Gate-1 PASSED with the original architecture intact — the escalation ladder was never needed.

## Method lessons baked in (mistakes owned)
- Round 1 fixed a REAL defect (find_by_name linear scan) and got ZERO speedup — it was not the dominant branch. Lesson: sum ALL profile branches before fixing; do not fix the first named function.
- Round 2's own fix (the receiver index) became round 5's hot path. Lesson: re-profile your own fixes; an index that replaces a scan can hide a smaller scan inside itself.

## Status: ACTIVE — standing method for perf campaigns on this codebase.

title: ADR-3: No status above PROPOSED until viability gates are MEASURED; no storage code before Gate-0/1
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-04 evening, supersedes ADR-2's lock) Process rule: architecture decisions stay PROPOSED-PENDING-GATE until their kill-gates are measured on the real pipeline. Concretely: no storage code before Gate-0 (FileIR purity) and Gate-1 (compose cost <=2s). Compose model corrected to LAZY (on-query-after-dirty) in the same review.

## Rationale
A gate that can kill the design must run before anything is "locked". Adversarial self-review called the prior locking "manufactured false certainty"; the advisor independently re-classified Gate-1 from open-question to approval-blocker.

## Alternatives Considered
- Keep LOCKED, treat Gate-1 as a formality: rejected — that is how weeks get spent building a cache for the cheap step.

## Outcome (VINDICATED — this rule did the work)
Gate-1 ran the same evening and FAILED 110x over the line: the unmeasured premise (parse expensive / compose cheap) was exactly backwards. Zero storage code had been written. The entire cost of the wrong assumption was one evening of measurement instead of weeks of building.

## Status: ACTIVE — this is now the standing rule for TLDR-zde and successor designs.

title: ADR-2: Mark architecture LOCKED before running Gate-1 (process mistake, owned)
description:
## Decision
(2026-06-04 afternoon) Mark the chunk-store architecture choices — two-flow constraint, rkyv chunks, CSR+fst layout, lifecycle, integrity scheme — as LOCKED while Gate-1 (the compose-cost bench) was still UNRUN.

## Rationale (at the time)
Choices were user-confirmed and research-double-confirmed (incl. a counter-evidence pass); locking was meant to stop re-litigating settled questions.

## Why this was a mistake (owned)
Gate-1 decided whether the recompose-based design was viable AT ALL — it was an approval blocker mislabeled as an open question. Locking around an unrun measurement manufactured false certainty. This is the central PROCESS error of the campaign: certainty was assigned by research consensus, not by measurement.

## How it was caught
Adversarial self-review + advisor, the same day, BEFORE the bench ran: "downgrade all LOCKED items to PROPOSED-PENDING-GATE-1". Hours later Gate-1 failed 110x over the pass line, proving the downgrade right.

## Lesson
Research can validate that a library is healthy; only measurement can validate that a design premise is true. Lock nothing upstream of an unrun kill-gate.
close_reason: Superseded by TLDR-k89 (ADR-3): LOCKED downgraded to PROPOSED-PENDING-GATE-1 before the gate ran

comments.author: Udhayakumar
